problems.titleEn: Question 2.1 - Derive the Black-Scholes equation for a stock, S
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.1 - 推导股票 S 的 Black-Scholes 方程
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Derive the Black-Scholes equation for a stock, S. What boundary conditions are satisfied at S = 0 and S = oo?
problems.promptZh: 推导股票 S 的 Black-Scholes 方程。在 S = 0 和 S = oo 时满足什么边界条件？
problems.answer:
For a non-dividend-paying stock,
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
For a call, $C(t,0)=0$ and as $S\to\infty$, $C_S(t,S)\to1$ with $C(t,S)\sim S-Ke^{-r(T-t)}$.
problems.answerEn:
For a non-dividend-paying stock,
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
For a call, $C(t,0)=0$ and as $S\to\infty$, $C_S(t,S)\to1$ with $C(t,S)\sim S-Ke^{-r(T-t)}$.
problems.answerZh:
对不分红股票，
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
对看涨期权，$C(t,0)=0$；当 $S\to\infty$ 时，$C_S(t,S)\to1$，且 $C(t,S)\sim S-Ke^{-r(T-t)}$。
problems.explanation:
Under the risk-neutral measure,
$$dS_t=rS_t\,dt+\sigma S_t\,dW_t,$$
and the bank account satisfies $dB_t=rB_t\,dt$. Let $C(t,S)$ be the derivative price. Ito's lemma gives
$$dC=\left(C_t+rS C_S+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}\right)dt+\sigma S C_S\,dW_t.$$
The discounted price $C/B$ must be a martingale, so its drift is zero. Equivalently, the drift of $C$ must be $rC$. Hence
$$C_t+rS C_S+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}-rC=0.$$

For a European call with strike $K$, if $S=0$ then the stock remains at zero in the Black-Scholes model and the call is worthless, so $C(t,0)=0$. For very large $S$, the call is almost surely deep in the money; its delta tends to $1$ and its value behaves like the forward intrinsic value
$$C(t,S)\sim S-Ke^{-r(T-t)}.$$
problems.explanationEn:
Under the risk-neutral measure,
$$dS_t=rS_t\,dt+\sigma S_t\,dW_t,$$
and the bank account satisfies $dB_t=rB_t\,dt$. Let $C(t,S)$ be the derivative price. Ito's lemma gives
$$dC=\left(C_t+rS C_S+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}\right)dt+\sigma S C_S\,dW_t.$$
The discounted price $C/B$ must be a martingale, so its drift is zero. Equivalently, the drift of $C$ must be $rC$. Hence
$$C_t+rS C_S+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}-rC=0.$$

For a European call with strike $K$, if $S=0$ then the stock remains at zero in the Black-Scholes model and the call is worthless, so $C(t,0)=0$. For very large $S$, the call is almost surely deep in the money; its delta tends to $1$ and its value behaves like the forward intrinsic value
$$C(t,S)\sim S-Ke^{-r(T-t)}.$$
problems.explanationZh:
在风险中性测度下，
$$dS_t=rS_t\,dt+\sigma S_t\,dW_t,$$
银行账户满足 $dB_t=rB_t\,dt$。令 $C(t,S)$ 为衍生品价格。由 Ito 引理，
$$dC=\left(C_t+rS C_S+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}\right)dt+\sigma S C_S\,dW_t.$$
贴现价格 $C/B$ 必须是鞅，因此漂移项为零。等价地，$C$ 的漂移必须等于 $rC$。所以
$$C_t+rS C_S+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}-rC=0.$$

对执行价为 $K$ 的欧式看涨期权，如果 $S=0$，Black-Scholes 模型下股票价格会保持为零，因此看涨期权价值为 $0$，即 $C(t,0)=0$。当 $S$ 很大时，看涨期权几乎必然深度价内；其 delta 趋于 $1$，价值近似为远期内在价值
$$C(t,S)\sim S-Ke^{-r(T-t)}.$$

problems.titleEn: Question 2.2 - Derive the Black-Scholes equation so that an undergrad can understand it
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.2 - 推导布莱克-斯科尔斯方程，以便本科生能够理解它
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Derive the Black-Scholes equation so that an undergrad can understand it.
problems.promptZh: 推导布莱克-斯科尔斯方程，以便本科生能够理解它。
problems.answer:
Use the risk-neutral stock dynamics $dS=rS\,dt+\sigma S\,dW$. Apply Ito's lemma to $C(t,S)$, then require the discounted option price to have zero drift. This gives
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
problems.answerEn:
Use the risk-neutral stock dynamics $dS=rS\,dt+\sigma S\,dW$. Apply Ito's lemma to $C(t,S)$, then require the discounted option price to have zero drift. This gives
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
problems.answerZh:
使用风险中性股票动态 $dS=rS\,dt+\sigma S\,dW$。对 $C(t,S)$ 应用 Ito 引理，再要求贴现期权价格的漂移为零，得到
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
problems.explanation:
An undergraduate-level derivation can be framed as a no-free-money argument. In the Black-Scholes model, after changing to the risk-neutral view, the stock grows on average at the risk-free rate:
$$dS=rS\,dt+\sigma S\,dW.$$
The option price is a smooth function $C(t,S)$. Ito's lemma says its infinitesimal change is
$$dC=\left(C_t+rS C_S+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}\right)dt+\sigma S C_S\,dW.$$
If the option is correctly priced, the discounted value of the option cannot have predictable growth; otherwise there would be arbitrage. Therefore the drift of $C$ must be $rC$. Setting the drift equal to $rC$ gives the Black-Scholes PDE:
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
problems.explanationEn:
An undergraduate-level derivation can be framed as a no-free-money argument. In the Black-Scholes model, after changing to the risk-neutral view, the stock grows on average at the risk-free rate:
$$dS=rS\,dt+\sigma S\,dW.$$
The option price is a smooth function $C(t,S)$. Ito's lemma says its infinitesimal change is
$$dC=\left(C_t+rS C_S+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}\right)dt+\sigma S C_S\,dW.$$
If the option is correctly priced, the discounted value of the option cannot have predictable growth; otherwise there would be arbitrage. Therefore the drift of $C$ must be $rC$. Setting the drift equal to $rC$ gives the Black-Scholes PDE:
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
problems.explanationZh:
本科生层面的推导可以理解为“不能有免费赚钱机会”的论证。在 Black-Scholes 模型中，转到风险中性视角后，股票平均按无风险利率增长：
$$dS=rS\,dt+\sigma S\,dW.$$
期权价格是当前时间和股价的光滑函数 $C(t,S)$。Ito 引理给出
$$dC=\left(C_t+rS C_S+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}\right)dt+\sigma S C_S\,dW.$$
如果期权定价正确，贴现后的期权价值不能有可预测增长；否则会产生套利。因此 $C$ 的漂移必须等于 $rC$。令漂移等于 $rC$，得到 Black-Scholes PDE：
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$

problems.titleEn: Question 2.3 - Explain the Black-Scholes equation
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.3 - 解释布莱克-斯科尔斯方程
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Explain the Black-Scholes equation.
problems.promptZh: 解释布莱克-斯科尔斯方程。
problems.answer:
The Black-Scholes equation is the PDE
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0,$$
which an arbitrage-free derivative price must satisfy under the model assumptions. The payoff determines the terminal/boundary conditions; the PDE itself is common to European claims on the same non-dividend-paying stock.
problems.answerEn:
The Black-Scholes equation is the PDE
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0,$$
which an arbitrage-free derivative price must satisfy under the model assumptions. The payoff determines the terminal/boundary conditions; the PDE itself is common to European claims on the same non-dividend-paying stock.
problems.answerZh:
Black-Scholes 方程是 PDE
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0,$$
它是模型假设下无套利衍生品价格必须满足的方程。收益函数决定终端/边界条件；对同一只不分红股票上的欧式权益，该 PDE 本身相同。
problems.explanation:
For a derivative with price $C(t,S)$ on a non-dividend-paying stock, the Black-Scholes equation is
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
Here $t$ is time, $S$ is the current stock price, $r$ is the risk-free rate, and $\sigma$ is the stock volatility. The term $C_t$ captures time decay, $rSC_S$ comes from the risk-neutral drift of the stock, $\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}$ is the convexity/Ito correction, and $-rC$ discounts the derivative value.

The equation is not enough by itself: the payoff supplies the terminal condition, for example $C(T,S)=\max(S-K,0)$ for a European call. Different payoffs use the same PDE but different terminal or boundary conditions.
problems.explanationEn:
For a derivative with price $C(t,S)$ on a non-dividend-paying stock, the Black-Scholes equation is
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
Here $t$ is time, $S$ is the current stock price, $r$ is the risk-free rate, and $\sigma$ is the stock volatility. The term $C_t$ captures time decay, $rSC_S$ comes from the risk-neutral drift of the stock, $\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}$ is the convexity/Ito correction, and $-rC$ discounts the derivative value.

The equation is not enough by itself: the payoff supplies the terminal condition, for example $C(T,S)=\max(S-K,0)$ for a European call. Different payoffs use the same PDE but different terminal or boundary conditions.
problems.explanationZh:
对不分红股票上的衍生品价格 $C(t,S)$，Black-Scholes 方程为
$$C_t+\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}+rSC_S-rC=0.$$
其中 $t$ 为时间，$S$ 为当前股价，$r$ 为无风险利率，$\sigma$ 为股票波动率。$C_t$ 表示时间变化，$rSC_S$ 来自风险中性股价漂移，$\frac12\sigma^2S^2C_{SS}$ 是凸性/Ito 修正项，$-rC$ 表示衍生品价值的贴现。

该方程本身还不够：收益函数给出终端条件。例如欧式看涨期权满足 $C(T,S)=\max(S-K,0)$。不同收益使用相同 PDE，但终端或边界条件不同。

problems.titleEn: Question 2.4 - Suppose two assets in a Black-Scholes world have the same volatility but different drifts
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.4 - 假设布莱克-斯科尔斯世界中的两种资产具有相同的波动性但不同的漂移
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Suppose two assets in a Black-Scholes world have the same volatility but different drifts. How will the price of call options on them compare? Now suppose one of the assets undergoes downward jumps at random times. How will this affect option prices?
problems.promptZh: 假设布莱克-斯科尔斯世界中的两种资产具有相同的波动性但不同的漂移。它们的看涨期权价格如何比较？现在假设其中一项资产在随机时间经历向下跳跃。这将如何影响期权价格？
problems.answer: With the same volatility, Black-Scholes call prices are the same despite different real-world drifts. Downward jumps make convex-payoff options such as calls more valuable than the pure Black-Scholes diffusion price, because delta hedging loses value at jumps.
problems.answerEn: With the same volatility, Black-Scholes call prices are the same despite different real-world drifts. Downward jumps make convex-payoff options such as calls more valuable than the pure Black-Scholes diffusion price, because delta hedging loses value at jumps.
problems.answerZh: 若波动率相同，即使真实世界漂移不同，Black-Scholes 看涨期权价格也相同。若标的会随机向下跳跃，看涨期权这类凸收益期权通常比纯 Black-Scholes 扩散模型价格更高，因为 delta 对冲在跳跃处会亏损。
problems.explanation:
In Black-Scholes pricing, the real-world drift is replaced by the risk-free drift under the risk-neutral measure:
$$dS_t=rS_t\,dt+\sigma S_t\,dW_t.$$
Therefore two stocks with the same current price, volatility, maturity, strike, and interest rate have the same vanilla call price even if their real-world drifts differ.

If one stock can have downward jumps, the pure Black-Scholes delta hedge is no longer exact. For a convex payoff such as a call, the Black-Scholes price as a function of spot is convex, and a tangent-line hedge lies below the price curve after a jump. A downward jump can leave the hedging portfolio below the value needed to continue replication. To avoid arbitrage, the option on the jump stock must be priced higher than the no-jump Black-Scholes price, all else equal.
problems.explanationEn:
In Black-Scholes pricing, the real-world drift is replaced by the risk-free drift under the risk-neutral measure:
$$dS_t=rS_t\,dt+\sigma S_t\,dW_t.$$
Therefore two stocks with the same current price, volatility, maturity, strike, and interest rate have the same vanilla call price even if their real-world drifts differ.

If one stock can have downward jumps, the pure Black-Scholes delta hedge is no longer exact. For a convex payoff such as a call, the Black-Scholes price as a function of spot is convex, and a tangent-line hedge lies below the price curve after a jump. A downward jump can leave the hedging portfolio below the value needed to continue replication. To avoid arbitrage, the option on the jump stock must be priced higher than the no-jump Black-Scholes price, all else equal.
problems.explanationZh:
在 Black-Scholes 定价中，真实世界漂移会在风险中性测度下被无风险漂移替代：
$$dS_t=rS_t\,dt+\sigma S_t\,dW_t.$$
因此，只要当前价格、波动率、期限、行权价和利率相同，即使两只股票的真实世界漂移不同，普通看涨期权价格也相同。

如果其中一只股票可能向下跳跃，纯 Black-Scholes 的 delta 对冲就不再精确。对看涨期权这类凸收益，Black-Scholes 价格作为现货价格函数是凸的，切线对冲在跳跃后会落在价格曲线下方。向下跳跃可能使对冲组合低于继续复制所需的价值。为避免套利，在其他条件相同下，跳跃股票上的期权价格应高于无跳跃 Black-Scholes 价格。

problems.titleEn: Question 2.5 - Suppose an asset has a deterministic time dependent volatility
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.5 - 假设资产具有确定性的时间依赖性波动性
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Suppose an asset has a deterministic time dependent volatility. How would I price an option on it using the Black-Scholes theory? How would I hedge it?
problems.promptZh:
假设资产具有确定性的时间依赖性波动性。我如何使用布莱克-斯科尔斯理论对期权进行定价？我该如何对冲它？

2.2.问题
problems.explanation:
Details of the following can be found in Section 6.11 of [6]. In a Black-Scholes world where the volatility follows a time-dependent (but still deterministic) function we can follow through the derivation of the Black-Scholes equation as per usual (see Question 2.1), however now the volatility 7 is a function. This gives the modified version of the Black-Scholes equation, OC OC 10°C o2 - + =r; + \sim \sim a(t)'Sf - rC = 0, at ag” tt a agRO % and the previous boundary conditions hold. From here it is not so clear how to proceed.

We instead consider pricing by using the martingale approach. Under the risk-neutral pricing measure, the evolution of the stock price is given by ds = r S,dt + o(t)S,$dW_t$, or equivalently, Our goal is to evaluate E($B_t$'C(T, $S_T$)), where the expectation is taken in the risk-neutral measure, B, is the value of the risk-free asset and C(t, $S_t$) is the value of the option at time t when the stock price is S,. For this we need the distribution of Sy, or equivalently log($S_T$). The above equation is just shorthand for T T log Sp - log So = rT - s/f o(t)*dt + [ o(t)$dW_t$. If we recall some basic facts from stochastic calculus, we notice that for a deterministic function f, \{” f(t)dW; is a normally distributed random variable with zero mean and variance [” f(t)?dt. If we define o as the root-mean-square value of o(t), i.e. T G= zi o(s)2ds, then we have log Sp - logSg= (+ - 50”) T+6VTZ, where Z is a standard normal random variable. Here we notice that this is just the distribution at time T for the log of a geometric Brownian motion with constant volatility o, and we can price using this. For example, we have that the value of a European call option is just given by the Black-Scholes formula with o replaced by a. To hedge, we hold ae units of stock at a time. The only difference is, what value of volatility do we use? At time t, C(t, S,) is the value of the option with root-mean-square volatility taken over the time period [t, T], and this is the value we use to find the hedge.

Here are some possible related questions: e What changes if we use time-dependent drift? e What changes if we use time-dependent interest rates? e Where does the above argument break down if we introduce stochastic volatility? O

problems.explanationZh:
以下详细信息可以在[6]的第6.11节中找到。在布莱克-斯科尔斯世界中，波动率遵循与时间相关（但仍然是确定性）的函数，我们可以像往常一样推导布莱克-斯科尔斯方程（参见问题 2.1），但现在波动率 7 是一个函数。这给出了 Black-Scholes 方程的修改版本，OC OC 10°C o2 — + =r； + a(t)'Sf — rC = 0, at ag” tt a agRO % 并且前面的边界条件成立。从这里开始，不太清楚如何进行。

相反，我们考虑使用鞅法来定价。在风险中性定价测度下，股票价格的演变由 ds = r S,dt + o(t)S,dW 给出，或等价地，我们的目标是评估 E(B;'C(T, Sr))，其中期望采用风险中性测度，B 是无风险资产的价值，C(t, S;) 是股票价格为 S 时 t 时刻的期权价值。为此，我们需要 Sy 的分布，或等效的 log(S7)。上式只是 T T log Sp — log So = rT — s/f o(t)*dt + [ o(t)dW, 的简写。如果我们回忆一下随机微积分中的一些基本事实，我们会注意到对于确定性函数 f，\{” f(t)dW; 是一个均值和方差为零的正态分布随机变量 [” f(t)?d¢。如果我们将 o 定义为 o(t) 的均方根值，即 T G= zi o(s)2ds，则我们有 log Sp — logSg= (+ — 50”) T+6VTZ，其中 Z 是标准正态随机变量。这里我们注意到，这只是波动率 o 恒定的几何布朗运动的对数在时间 7 的分布，我们可以使用它来定价。例如，我们有欧式看涨期权的价值为由 Black-Scholes 公式给出，其中 o 被 a 代替。为了对冲，我们一次持有 ae 单位的股票。唯一的区别是，在时间 t 时，C(t, S,) 是在时间段 [t, T] 内采用均方根波动率的期权价值，这是我们用来寻找对冲的值。

problems.promptEn: In the Black-Scholes world, price a European option with a payoff of max(S3 - K,0) at time T'.
problems.promptZh: 在 Black-Scholes 世界中，对欧式期权进行定价，其收益为 7' 时的 max(S3 — K,0)。
problems.explanation:
The term 'in the Black-Scholes world' refers to the situation where the stock price $S_t$ follows the stochastic differential equation for 4, o > 0 and where $W_t$ is a Brownian motion. The question then asks us to compute, in the risk-neutral measure, the expectation E[max($S_T$ - K,0)], with appropriate discounting factors (a constant continuously compounding interest rate of r is assumed), where T is the maturity date and K is the strike price. The Black-Scholes PDEis still satisfied, with different boundary conditions, or you can use the more probabilistic approach of integrating the expectation against the appropriate density. However all of this is a lot of work, and a large short-cut exists if we stop and think about it for a second. In the risk-neutral measure, the stock price follows the stochastic differential equation The solution to this is well-known and given by $S_t$ = Soexp r - 50 t+ow, >. We are trying to price an option on S?, so what process does S? follow? One option is to apply It's formula, alternatively we can look at the above expression and see immediately that S?=8exp \{ (2r - a”) t+2oW;\}.

Comparing this with the original geometric Brownian motion, we have that in the risk-neutral pricing measure S? is again a geometric Brownian motion with diffusion 2t and drift 2r + 07. We can therefore use the Black formula. We simply have to write the S2, as Fr(0)e YM F/2+Wr compute the values of F7(0) and v and plug them in. Here are some possible related questions: e Write down the boundary conditions satisfied by the new PDE. e What are the Greeks of the option on S$?? O

problems.explanationZh:
术语“在布莱克-斯科尔斯世界中”是指股票价格 S; 的情况。遵循 4 的随机微分方程，o > 0 并且其中 W；是布朗运动。然后，该问题要求我们在风险中性度量中计算期望 E[max(S7 — K,0)]，并使用适当的贴现因子（假设 r 为恒定的连续复利利率），其中 T 是到期日，K 是执行价格。在不同的边界条件下，Black-Scholes PDE 仍然满足，或者您可以使用更具概率的方法将期望与适当的密度进行积分。然而，所有这些工作量很大，如果我们停下来想一想，还有一条很大的捷径。在风险中性测度中，股票价格遵循随机微分方程。该方程的解是众所周知的，由 S 给出； = Soexp r— 50 t+ow，>。我们正在尝试对 S 上的期权进行定价？那么 S 的流程是什么？跟随？一种选择是应用 It6 的公式，或者我们可以查看上面的表达式，立即看到 S?=8exp \{ (2r — a”) t+2oW;\}。

将其与原始几何布朗运动进行比较，我们可以在风险中性定价度量 S？又是一个几何布朗运动，扩散为 2 分，漂移为 2r + 07。因此，我们可以使用布莱克公式。我们只需写出 S2，因为 Fr(0)e YM F/2+Wr 计算 F7(0) 和 v 的值并将它们代入。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 写下新偏微分方程满足的边界条件。 e 新元期权的希腊字母是什么？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.8 - Sketch the value of a vanilla call option as a function of spot
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.8 - 将普通看涨期权的价值绘制为即期函数
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Sketch the value of a vanilla call option as a function of spot. How will it evolve with time?
problems.promptZh: 将普通看涨期权的价值绘制为即期函数。随着时间的推移，它将如何演变？
problems.explanation:
Figure 2.1 shows the value of vanilla call options with 1 year, 6 months and 1 day to expiry. It is unlikely you will sketch these graphs perfectly in an interview, however there are a few important features that should be preserved:
- The vanilla call option value is monotone increasing in spot.
- The value is a convex function of spot, that is the price lies above all of the possible tangent lines.
- A vanilla call option on a non-dividend paying stock with longer time to expiry than an equivalent option is always worth at least as much.

As the last bullet point suggests, the option value decreases as time to expiry decreases and the value approaches its payoff. Here are some possible related questions: sketch the value of a vanilla put option as a function of spot; sketch the value of a digital call/put option as a function of spot; sketch the value of a vanilla call/put option as a function of volatility.

- The vanilla call option value is monotone increasing in spot.
- The value is a convex function of spot, that is the price lies above all of the possible tangent lines.

As the last bullet point suggests, the option value decreases as time to expiry decreases and the value approaches its payoff. Here are some possible related questions: sketch the value of a vanilla put option as a function of spot; sketch the value of a digital call/put option as a function of spot; sketch the value of a vanilla call/put option as a function of volatility.
problems.explanationZh:
图 2.1 显示了距离到期日为 1 年、6 个月和 1 天的普通看涨期权价值。面试中不必画得完全精确，但应保留几个关键特征：
- 普通看涨期权价值随现货价格单调递增。
- 价值是现货价格的凸函数，即价格位于所有可能切线之上。
- 对不支付股息的股票，到期时间更长的同类普通看涨期权价值至少同样高。

最后一点也说明，随着到期时间缩短，期权价值会下降并接近其到期收益。相关问题包括：画出普通看跌期权、数字看涨/看跌期权相对于现货的价值，或画出普通看涨/看跌期权相对于波动率的价值。

problems.titleEn: Question 2.9 - Is it ever optimal to early exercise an American call option
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.9 - 尽早行使美式看涨期权是否是最佳选择
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Is it ever optimal to early exercise an American call option? What about a put option?
problems.promptZh: 尽早行使美式看涨期权是否是最佳选择？看跌期权怎么样？
problems.explanation:
The short answer is that it is never optimal to early exercise an American call option (assuming the underlying stock does not pay dividends), but it can be optimal to early exercise an American put option.

For the American call option, consider a portfolio consisting of one option C and K zero coupon bonds B, which expire at time T. At time T if the non-dividend paying stock price S is above K then the portfolio will be worth (Sp - K)+ K =Sp. If the stock price is below K the portfolio will be worth K. Therefore our portfolio is worth max\{$S_T$, K\}, which is never less than the stock price and sometimes more. The principle of no arbitrage implies that the portfolio must be worth more than the stock at all previous times, giving Rearranging, this yields Ci > S_ - KB. Assuming interest rates are non-negative i.e. By < 1 we have Cr > S& - K5 which says that an option will always be worth more than its value if exercised immediately. Unfortunately there isn't an equivalent argument for put options, and there are times when the optionality value is worth less than receiving the payoff today and hence it is optimal to early exercise. For more details on optimal exercise times of American put options see [13]. Here are some related possible questions: e@ What about when the stock pays dividends, is it ever optimal to early exercise an American option? e@ What if there are negative interest rates? e What can be said about the relationship between the price of European and American call options? Put options? O

At time T if the non-dividend paying stock price S is above K then the portfolio will be worth (Sp - K)+ K =Sp. If the stock price is below K the portfolio will be worth K. Therefore our portfolio is worth max\{$S_T$, K\}, which is never less than the stock price and sometimes more. The principle of no arbitrage implies that the portfolio must be worth more than the stock at all previous times, giving Rearranging, this yields Ci > S_ - KB. Assuming interest rates are non-negative i.e. By < 1 we have Cr > S& - K5 which says that an option will always be worth more than its value if exercised immediately. Unfortunately there isn't an equivalent argument for put options, and there are times when the optionality value is worth less than receiving the payoff today and hence it is optimal to early exercise. Put options? O
problems.explanationZh:
简而言之，提前行使美式看涨期权（假设标的股票不支付股息）永远不是最佳选择，但提前行使美式看跌期权可能是最佳选择。

2.3.解决方案 对于美式看涨期权，考虑一个由一份期权 C 和 K 个零息债券 B 组成的投资组合，该债券在时间 7 到期。在时间 T，如果不支付股息的股票价格 S 高于 K，则该投资组合的价值将为 (Sp —K)+ K = Sp。如果股票价格低于 K，则投资组合的价值将为 K。因此，我们的投资组合的价值为 max\{S7, K\}，永远不会低于股票价格，有时甚至更高。无套利原则意味着投资组合必须比之前所有时间的股票价值都高，重新排列后，得出 Ci > S_— KB。假设利率为非负数，即 < 1，我们有 Cr > S& — K5，这表示如果立即行使，期权的价值将始终高于其价值。不幸的是，看跌期权没有同等的论据，有时期权价值的价值低于今天收到的回报，因此早期行使是最佳选择。有关美式看跌期权最佳行权时间的更多详细信息，请参阅[13]。以下是一些相关的可能问题： e@ 当股票支付股息时，提前行使美式期权是否是最佳选择？ e@如果出现负利率怎么办？ e 关于欧美看涨期权价格之间的关系可以说些什么？放置选项？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.10 - In FX markets an option can be expressed as either a call or a put, explain
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.10 - 解释一下，在外汇市场中，期权可以表示为看涨期权或看跌期权
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: In FX markets an option can be expressed as either a call or a put, explain. Relate your answer to
problems.promptZh: 解释一下，在外汇市场中，期权可以表示为看涨期权或看跌期权。将您的答案与
problems.explanation:
The answer to this question is not initially obvious if one is thinking about the quoted exchange rate, rather than what actually happens when the option is exercised. Consider an option on the AUDUSD exchange rate where AUD\$1,000,000 is sold to buy USD\$800,000 on the option's exercise date. This option could be considered both a call on USD and a put on AUD.

If we take the current exchange rate to be AUD$1 = USD\$0.75, the call option will have a strike of 0.80 and a notional of AUD\$1,000,000. The put will have spot equal to 1.00, strike of 0.75/0.80 = 0.9375 and a notional of AUD\$800,000. After answering this and the previous question the interviewer may ask, why is it never optimal to early exercise the call when it is equivalent to a put, which can be optimally early exercised? The answer is that the notional amount in the foreign currency will grow at its risk free rate, which is equivalent to paying dividends and hence the optimal early exercise arguments do not apply. O

The put will have spot equal to 1.00, strike of 0.75/0.80 = 0.9375 and a notional of AUD\$800,000. After answering this and the previous question the interviewer may ask, why is it never optimal to early exercise the call when it is equivalent to a put, which can be optimally early exercised? The answer is that the notional amount in the foreign currency will grow at its risk free rate, which is equivalent to paying dividends and hence the optimal early exercise arguments do not apply. O
problems.explanationZh:
如果人们考虑的是报价汇率，而不是行使期权时实际发生的情况，那么这个问题的答案最初并不明显。考虑 AUDUSD 汇率的期权，其中 AUD\$1,000,000 is sold to buy USD\$800,000 在期权行权日。该期权可被视为美元看涨期权和澳元看跌期权。

如果我们将当前汇率设为 AUD\$1 = USD\$0.75，则看涨期权的行使价为 0.80，名义金额为 AUD\$1,000,000. The put will have spot equal to 1.00, strike of 0.75/0.80 = 0.9375 and a notional of AUD\$800,000。在回答完这个问题以及面试官可能会问的上一个问题后，为什么当看涨期权相当于可以最佳提前行使的看跌期权时，提前行使看涨期权永远不是最佳选择？答案是，外币的名义金额将以其无风险利率增长，这相当于支付股息，因此最佳早期行权论点不适用。氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.11 - Approximately how much would a one-month call option at-the-money with a million dollar notional and spot 1 be worth
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.11 - 名义价值为 100 万美元且现货 1 的一个月期看涨期权的价值大约是多少
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Approximately how much would a one-month call option at-the-money with a million dollar notional and spot 1 be worth?
problems.promptZh: 名义价值为 100 万美元且现货 1 的一个月期看涨期权的价值大约是多少？
problems.explanation: This question is designed to test one's basic feel for the price of an option. An easy way to answer is by using the approximation formula for at-the-money options. It states that an option's price is approximately 0.4Sp0VT. (The derivation of this formula can be found in [6].) To answer the question we need to know the volatility, which for simplicity we could assume to be 10%. The price of a one month at-the-money call option with million dollar notional and spot 1 is worth approximately \$11,500. Here are some possible related questions: e What is the approximate price of a put option with the same characteristics as above? (Note that the approximation formula is the same for puts and calls as we are at-the-money, by put-call parity). e How accurate is the approximation above? O

problems.explanationZh: 该问题旨在测试人们对期权价格的基本感觉。一个简单的回答方法是使用平价期权的近似公式。它指出期权的价格约为 0.4Sp0VT。 （这个公式的推导可以在[6]中找到。）为了回答这个问题，我们需要知道波动率，为简单起见，我们可以假设波动率为 10%。名义金额为 100 万美元且现货 1 的一个月平值看涨期权的价格约为 11,500 美元。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 具有与上述相同特征的看跌期权的大致价格是多少？ （请注意，根据看跌期权平价，看跌期权和看涨期权的近似公式相同，因为我们处于平价状态）。 e 上述近似值的准确度如何？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.12 - Suppose a call option only pays off if spot never passes below a barrier B
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.12 - 假设看涨期权仅在现货从未低于障碍 B 时才获得回报
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers

problems.promptEn: Suppose a call option only pays off if spot never passes below a barrier B. Sketch the value as a function of spot. Now suppose the option only pays off if spot passes below B instead. Sketch the value of the option again. Relate the two graphs.
problems.promptZh: 假设看涨期权仅在现货从未低于障碍 B 时才获得回报。将价值绘制为现货的函数。现在假设只有当现货价格低于 B 时，该期权才会产生回报。再次勾勒出该选项的价值。将两个图联系起来。
problems.explanation:
After recognising the first option as a down-and-out call, the value should look something similar to the increasing line in Figure 2.2, which is a sketch of a 1 year call with barrier at 95. The key features are zero value for spot below the barrier, increasing value with spot, and similarity to a vanilla call option when far from the barrier.

The second option in Figure 2.2 is a down-and-in call with the same parameters. The two option values when added together should look the same as a vanilla call option: out + in = vanilla.

After recognising the first option as a down-and-out call, the value should look something similar to the increasing line in Figure 2.2, which is a sketch of a 1 year call with barrier at 95. The second option in Figure 2.2 is a down-and-in call with the same parameters. The two option values when added together should look the same as a vanilla call option: out + in = vanilla.
problems.explanationZh:
在将第一个期权识别为 down-and-out call 后，其价值应类似于图 2.2 中的上升曲线；该图展示了障碍为 95 的一年期看涨期权。关键特征是：低于障碍时价值为零，价值随现货增加，并且远离障碍时接近普通看涨期权。

图 2.2 中的第二个期权是参数相同的 down-and-in call。两个期权价值相加应与普通看涨期权一致：out + in = vanilla。

problems.titleEn: Question 2.13 - What is meant by put-call parity
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.13 - 看跌期权平价是什么意思
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What is meant by put-call parity?
problems.promptZh: 看跌期权平价是什么意思？
problems.explanation: Put-call parity states that a European call option minus a European put option, with the same underlying asset, strike price and time to maturity, is equal to the forward contract. Using C' for call, P for put and F' for the forward contract we have Put-call parity is a very useful concept in options pricing and it is model independent, so it must hold no matter which model one is working with. Put-call parity could be used, for example, to price a put option instead of the required call option, due to the fact a put option is sometimes easier to price as its payoff is bounded. The parity gives the call option from the calculated put and forward contract. It can also be used to prove that the drift of the underlying asset does not affect the price of an option. Here are some possible related questions: e Does put-call parity hold for options with early exercise features, e.g. American options? e Prove put-call parity. O
problems.explanationEn: Put-call parity states that a European call option minus a European put option, with the same underlying asset, strike price and time to maturity, is equal to the forward contract. Put-call parity could be used, for example, to price a put option instead of the required call option, due to the fact a put option is sometimes easier to price as its payoff is bounded. The parity gives the call option from the calculated put and forward contract. It can also be used to prove that the drift of the underlying asset does not affect the price of an option. Here are some possible related questions: e Does put-call parity hold for options with early exercise features, e.g. American options? e Prove put-call parity. O
problems.explanationZh: 看跌期权平价规定，标的资产、执行价格和到期时间相同的欧式看涨期权减去欧式看跌期权等于远期合约。使用 C' 表示看涨期权，P 表示看跌期权，F' 表示远期合约，我们有看跌期权平价，这是期权定价中非常有用的概念，并且它与模型无关，因此无论使用哪种模型，它都必须成立。例如，可以使用看跌期权平价来对看跌期权而不是所需的看涨期权进行定价，因为看跌期权有时更容易定价，因为其收益是有限的。平价给出了计算出的看跌期权和远期合约的看涨期权。它还可以用来证明标的资产的漂移不会影响期权的价格。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 看跌期权平价对具有早期行权特征的期权是否成立，例如美式选择？ e 证明看跌期权平价。氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.14 - What happens to the price of a vanilla call option as volatility tends to infinity
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.14 - 当波动性趋于无穷大时，普通看涨期权的价格会发生什么变化
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What happens to the price of a vanilla call option as volatility tends to infinity?
problems.promptZh: 当波动性趋于无穷大时，普通看涨期权的价格会发生什么变化？
problems.explanation:
There are at least two ways to answer this question, however the first is preferred as it does not assume any underlying model. The price of a vanilla call option is monotone increasing in volatility, so as volatility tends to infinity the option price will tend to its maximum value. We know that the upper bound for the price of a call option is the stock price, so as volatility tends to infinity the price of a call option tends to the stock price. The second method of answering this question is to assume we are in a Black-Scholes world, with the option price given by the Black-Scholes formula. We can then argue that as volatility tends to infinity log(£) + (r+ S)T d, - CSW) FH BIT as 0 - 00. oVT

Similarly, log(2) + (r - 2)T dy - 8 FO - BIT ( z) —+—0O a o> OO. oVT The corresponding normal cdf of d, and dz will then be 1 and 0 respectively. So as volatility tends to infinity the option's price will tend to the spot price. Here are some possible related questions: e What happens to the price of a vanilla call option as volatility tends to zero? e What happens to the price of a vanilla call option as time to expiry tends to infinity? O

problems.titleEn: Question 2.15 - Suppose there are no interest rates
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.15 - 假设没有利率。不派息股票的现货价格为 20
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Suppose there are no interest rates. The spot price of a non-dividend paying stock is 20. Option A pays 1 dollar if the stock price is above 30 at any time in the next year. Option B pays 1 if the stock price is above 30 at the end of the year. How are the values of A and B related?
problems.promptZh: 假设没有利率。不派息股票的现货价格为 20。如果明年任何时候股价高于 30，选项 A 支付 1 美元。如果年底股价高于 30，选项 B 支付 1。 A 和 B 的值有何关系？
problems.explanation: The key to this question is relating the American optionality of option A to the European optionality of option B in terms of probabilities. Clearly if option B pays a dollar then option A must also, but the converse is not true. Thus A must be worth more than B. For example, assume that the stock follows Brownian motion. As there are no interest rates this Brownian motion will have zero drift in the risk-neutral measure. If the stock reaches 30 before expiry, then in the risk-neutral measure there is a 50% chance it will be above 30 at expiry. In terms of the two options, if A pays off there is a 50% chance B will pay off, therefore B will be worth half the value of A. If we were to assume that the stock follows Geometric Brownian motion it will have a negative drift in the case of zero interest rates (due to the - o?T'/2 term). We no longer have that option B is worth half the value of option A, it will be worth less than half the value of A. Here is a related question: e In general, what can we say about the value of American digital options compared to European digital options? O
problems.explanationEn: The key to this question is relating the American optionality of option A to the European optionality of option B in terms of probabilities. Clearly if option B pays a dollar then option A must also, but the converse is not true. Thus A must be worth more than B. For example, assume that the stock follows Brownian motion. As there are no interest rates this Brownian motion will have zero drift in the risk-neutral measure. If the stock reaches 30 before expiry, then in the risk-neutral measure there is a 50% chance it will be above 30 at expiry. In terms of the two options, if A pays off there is a 50% chance B will pay off, therefore B will be worth half the value of A. If we were to assume that the stock follows Geometric Brownian motion it will have a negative drift in the case of zero interest rates (due to the - o?T'/2 term). We no longer have that option B is worth half the value of option A, it will be worth less than half the value of A. Here is a related question: e In general, what can we say about the value of American digital options compared to European digital options? O
problems.explanationZh: 这个问题的关键是将选项 A 的美国选择性与选项 B 的欧洲选择性在概率上联系起来。显然，如果选项 B 支付一美元，那么选项 A 也必须支付一美元，但反之则不然。因此，A 的价值一定高于 B。例如，假设股票遵循布朗运动。由于没有利率，这种布朗运动在风险中性指标中的漂移为零。如果股票在到期前达到 30，那么按照风险中性衡量标准，其在到期时有 50% 的机会超过 30。就这两个选项而言，如果 A 支付，B 有 50% 的机会支付，因此 B 的价值将是 A 的一半。如果我们假设股票遵循几何布朗运动，那么在零利率的情况下它将出现负漂移（由于 —o?T'/2 项）。我们不再认为选项 B 的价值是选项 A 价值的一半，它的价值将低于 A 价值的一半。这是一个相关问题： e 一般来说，与欧洲数字期权相比，我们可以说美国数字期权的价值如何？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.16 - How does the value of a call option vary with time
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.16 - 看涨期权的价值如何随时间变化
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: How does the value of a call option vary with time? Prove your result.
problems.promptZh: 看涨期权的价值如何随时间变化？证明你的结果。
problems.explanation: We will take the underlying asset to be nondividend paying and consider two arbitrary expiry times T, and 7) with T, < T. Take an American option C' which expires at time JT, and an American option C» expiring at time 75. C> carries all the rights of C'',, because it can be exercised at any time before 7) and it also carries extra rights, because it can be exercised after 7; whereas C', cannot. Therefore C2 must be worth at least as much as C\} as the extra rights will have a non-negative value. We have shown that as time to expiry increases the option value increases, which makes intuitive sense as extra time gives us more optionality, which our bank/broker will no doubt charge us extra for. Note this argument applies equally well to European call options since they will have the same value as American call options (see Question 2.9). Here are some possible related questions: e Prove this result for European options without using American options. e Will this relationship always hold for European options on dividendpaying assets? O

C> carries all the rights of C'',, because it can be exercised at any time before 7) and it also carries extra rights, because it can be exercised after 7; whereas C', cannot. Therefore C2 must be worth at least as much as C\} as the extra rights will have a non-negative value. We have shown that as time to expiry increases the option value increases, which makes intuitive sense as extra time gives us more optionality, which our bank/broker will no doubt charge us extra for. Note this argument applies equally well to European call options since they will have the same value as American call options (see Question 2.9). Here are some possible related questions: e Prove this result for European options without using American options. e Will this relationship always hold for European options on dividendpaying assets? O
problems.explanationZh: 我们将标的资产视为不支付股息，并考虑两个任意到期时间T和7)，其中T，<T。采用在时间JT到期的美式期权C'和在时间75到期的美式期权C»。C>具有C''的所有权利，因为它可以在7)之前的任何时间行使，并且还具有额外的权利，因为它可以在7之后行使；而C'则不能。因此，C2 的价值必须至少与 C\} 一样多，因为额外的权利将具有非负值。我们已经证明，随着到期时间的增加，期权价值也会增加，这具有直观意义，因为额外的时间给了我们更多的选择权，我们的银行/经纪人无疑会向我们收取额外费用。请注意，这一论点同样适用于欧式看涨期权，因为它们与美式看涨期权具有相同的价值（参见问题 2.9）。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 不使用美式期权，证明欧式期权的结果。 e 对于支付股息资产的欧式期权，这种关系是否始终成立？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.17 - A put and call on a stock struck at the forward price have the same value by put-call parity
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.17 - 根据看跌期权平价，以远期价格执行的股票的看跌期权和看涨期权具有相同的价值
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: A put and call on a stock struck at the forward price have the same value by put-call parity. Yet the value of a put is bounded and the value of a call is unbounded. Explain how they can have the same value.
problems.promptZh: 根据看跌期权平价，以远期价格执行的股票的看跌期权和看涨期权具有相同的价值。然而，看跌期权的价值是有界的，而看涨期权的价值是无界的。解释它们如何具有相同的值。
problems.explanation:
This is a strange question, but interviewers tend to like asking curly questions. Put-call parity holds even though the value of a call is unbounded. One could argue that it doesn't matter that the call value is unbounded, the put and call must have the same value by put-call parity. Another approach is to discuss how options are priced. The price of a derivative is given by the integral of the payoff multiplied by the risk-neutral density, then discounted. The payoff for a call option is unbounded, however when this is multiplied by the risk-neutral density it will be bounded. This is because the risk-neutral density will rapidly decay to zero for any reasonable model.

Here are some possible related questions: e What is the rate of decay of the Black-Scholes density? e What does it mean for a model to have fat tails? e Suppose we worked in the Bachelier model, what would happen? O
problems.explanationEn:
This is a strange question, but interviewers tend to like asking curly questions. Put-call parity holds even though the value of a call is unbounded. One could argue that it doesn't matter that the call value is unbounded, the put and call must have the same value by put-call parity. Another approach is to discuss how options are priced. The price of a derivative is given by the integral of the payoff multiplied by the risk-neutral density, then discounted. The payoff for a call option is unbounded, however when this is multiplied by the risk-neutral density it will be bounded. This is because the risk-neutral density will rapidly decay to zero for any reasonable model.

Here are some possible related questions: e What is the rate of decay of the Black-Scholes density? e What does it mean for a model to have fat tails? e Suppose we worked in the Bachelier model, what would happen? O
problems.explanationZh:
这是一个奇怪的问题，但面试官往往喜欢问一些花哨的问题。即使看涨期权的价值是无限的，看跌期权与看涨期权平价仍然成立。有人可能会争辩说，看涨期权价值不受限制并不重要，根据看跌期权平价，看跌期权和看涨期权必须具有相同的价值。另一种方法是讨论期权如何定价。衍生品的价格由收益积分乘以风险中性密度然后贴现得出。看涨期权的收益是无界的，但是当其乘以风险中性密度时，收益将是有界的。这是因为对于任何合理的模型，风险中性密度都会迅速衰减到零。

2.3.解决方案 以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 布莱克-斯科尔斯密度的衰减率是多少？ e 模型的肥尾意味着什么？ e 假设我们在 Bachelier 模型中工作，会发生什么？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.18 - Suppose we price a digital call in both normal and lognormal models in such a way that the price of call option with the same strike is invariant
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.18 - 假设我们在正态模型和对数正态模型中对数字看涨期权进行定价，使得具有相同行使价的看涨期权的价格保持不变
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Suppose we price a digital call in both normal and lognormal models in such a way that the price of call option with the same strike is invariant. How will the prices differ?
problems.promptZh: 假设我们在正态模型和对数正态模型中对数字看涨期权进行定价，使得具有相同行使价的看涨期权的价格保持不变。价格会有什么不同？
problems.answer: A digital call is the negative strike derivative of a call price. A normal model corresponds roughly to a negative implied-volatility skew relative to a lognormal model, so the digital call price is higher than in the lognormal model when vanilla call prices are matched.
problems.answerEn: A digital call is the negative strike derivative of a call price. A normal model corresponds roughly to a negative implied-volatility skew relative to a lognormal model, so the digital call price is higher than in the lognormal model when vanilla call prices are matched.
problems.answerZh: 数字看涨期权等于看涨期权价格对行权价的负导数。相对于对数正态模型，正态模型大致对应负的隐含波动率偏斜；在匹配普通看涨期权价格时，数字看涨期权价格会高于对数正态模型下的价格。
problems.explanation:
A digital call paying $1_{\{S_T>K\}}$ can be replicated as a tight call spread:
$$\text{Digital}(K)=-\frac{\partial C(K)}{\partial K}.$$
If vanilla calls are priced with an implied volatility smile, then
$$\frac{dC}{dK}=C_K+C_\sigma\,\sigma_K.$$
The no-smile lognormal price corresponds to the $-C_K$ term. The smile correction to the digital is
$$-C_\sigma\,\sigma_K.$$
Since vanilla call vega $C_\sigma$ is positive, a downward-sloping smile $\sigma_K<0$ increases the digital call price.

A normal model can be viewed roughly as a lognormal model whose implied volatility is higher for lower spot/strike levels, i.e. negative skew. Therefore, when vanilla call prices are matched, the digital call is more expensive in the normal model than in the lognormal model.
problems.explanationEn:
A digital call paying $1_{\{S_T>K\}}$ can be replicated as a tight call spread:
$$\text{Digital}(K)=-\frac{\partial C(K)}{\partial K}.$$
If vanilla calls are priced with an implied volatility smile, then
$$\frac{dC}{dK}=C_K+C_\sigma\,\sigma_K.$$
The no-smile lognormal price corresponds to the $-C_K$ term. The smile correction to the digital is
$$-C_\sigma\,\sigma_K.$$
Since vanilla call vega $C_\sigma$ is positive, a downward-sloping smile $\sigma_K<0$ increases the digital call price.

A normal model can be viewed roughly as a lognormal model whose implied volatility is higher for lower spot/strike levels, i.e. negative skew. Therefore, when vanilla call prices are matched, the digital call is more expensive in the normal model than in the lognormal model.
problems.explanationZh:
支付 $1_{\{S_T>K\}}$ 的数字看涨期权可由很窄的看涨价差复制：
$$\text{Digital}(K)=-\frac{\partial C(K)}{\partial K}.$$
如果普通看涨期权使用隐含波动率微笑定价，则
$$\frac{dC}{dK}=C_K+C_\sigma\,\sigma_K.$$
无微笑的对数正态价格对应 $-C_K$ 项。微笑对数字期权的修正为
$$-C_\sigma\,\sigma_K.$$
由于普通看涨期权 vega $C_\sigma$ 为正，向下倾斜的微笑 $\sigma_K<0$ 会提高数字看涨价格。

正态模型可粗略看成一种对数正态模型，其低现货/低行权价处的隐含波动率更高，即负偏斜。因此，在普通看涨期权价格被匹配时，正态模型下的数字看涨期权比对数正态模型下更贵。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Digital option smile/skew formulas were garbled.

problems.titleEn: Question 2.19 - What is riskier: a call option or the underlying

problems.explanation: The Delta of a call option is always between zero and one. The Delta of a stock is always one. The risk of the stock is therefore always bigger; this is, however, misleading in that the value of the stock is generally much, much higher than that of the option. If we put all our money into stocks or call options, it is the call option portfolio that would be riskier. Recall that the Delta of a call option is N(d,). After we divide by the Black-Scholes value, we have to compare N(d,) ee ands —.. S:N(d1) _ Ke-*(F-*) N(do) St Since N (dz) is always positive, the first of these is bigger than the second, and so the Delta as a fraction of the value is bigger. Why is the Delta the correct quantity to observe? If we are not Delta-hedged then the main component of our profit and loss when spot moves by AS will be O Value AS x ———. Os Here are some possible related questions: e@ What about put options? e What about a portfolio of calls and puts? ® Can you derive the Delta of a call option? UO

看涨期权的 Delta 始终在 0 到 1 之间。股票的 Delta 值始终为 1。因此，股票的风险总是更大；然而，这是一种误导，因为股票的价值通常远远高于期权的价值。如果我们把所有的钱都投入股票或看涨期权，那么看涨期权投资组合的风险就会更大。回想一下，看涨期权的 Delta 是 N(d,)。除以 Black-Scholes 值后，我们必须比较 N(d,) ee 和 s —.. S:N(d1) _ Ke-*(F-*) N(do) St 由于 N (dz) 始终为正，因此第一个比第二个大，因此作为值的一部分的 Delta 更大。为什么 Delta 是需要观察的正确数量？如果我们没有进行 Delta 对冲，那么当 AS 现货变动时我们损益的主要组成部分将是 O Value AS x ———。 Os 以下是一些可能的相关问题： e@ 看跌期权怎么样？ e 看涨期权和看跌期权的投资组合怎么样？ ® 您能推导出看涨期权的Delta 值吗？俄勒冈州

problems.titleEn: Question 2.21 - Estimate volatility from a normal-model ATM call
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.21 - 用正态模型平值看涨期权估计波动率
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Assume the stock price at time $T$ is normally distributed as $N(S_0,\sigma^2T)$, where $S_0$ is the current price. If we know the price $C$ of an at-the-money European call expiring at $T$, how can we estimate $\sigma$?
problems.promptZh: 假设股票在时间 $T$ 的价格服从正态分布 $N(S_0,\sigma^2T)$，其中 $S_0$ 为当前价格。若已知到期为 $T$ 的平值欧式看涨期权价格 $C$，如何估计 $\sigma$？
problems.answer:
If $S_T\sim N(S_0,\sigma^2T)$ and the ATM call price is $C$, then
$$C=\frac{\sigma\sqrt T}{\sqrt{2\pi}},\qquad \sigma=C\sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{T}}.$$
problems.answerEn:
If $S_T\sim N(S_0,\sigma^2T)$ and the ATM call price is $C$, then
$$C=\frac{\sigma\sqrt T}{\sqrt{2\pi}},\qquad \sigma=C\sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{T}}.$$
problems.answerZh:
若 $S_T\sim N(S_0,\sigma^2T)$，且平值看涨期权价格为 $C$，则
$$C=\frac{\sigma\sqrt T}{\sqrt{2\pi}},\qquad \sigma=C\sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{T}}.$$
problems.explanation:
For an at-the-money call with strike $K=S_0$ under the normal model,
$$C=E[(S_T-S_0)^+].$$
Write
$$S_T-S_0=\sigma\sqrt T\,Z,\qquad Z\sim N(0,1).$$
Then
$$C=\sigma\sqrt T\,E[Z^+].$$
Since
$$E[Z^+]=\int_0^\infty z\phi(z)\,dz=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}},$$
we get
$$C=\frac{\sigma\sqrt T}{\sqrt{2\pi}}.$$
Therefore
$$\sigma=C\sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{T}}.$$
problems.explanationEn:
For an at-the-money call with strike $K=S_0$ under the normal model,
$$C=E[(S_T-S_0)^+].$$
Write
$$S_T-S_0=\sigma\sqrt T\,Z,\qquad Z\sim N(0,1).$$
Then
$$C=\sigma\sqrt T\,E[Z^+].$$
Since
$$E[Z^+]=\int_0^\infty z\phi(z)\,dz=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}},$$
we get
$$C=\frac{\sigma\sqrt T}{\sqrt{2\pi}}.$$
Therefore
$$\sigma=C\sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{T}}.$$
problems.explanationZh:
对正态模型下行权价 $K=S_0$ 的平值看涨期权，
$$C=E[(S_T-S_0)^+].$$
写成
$$S_T-S_0=\sigma\sqrt T\,Z,\qquad Z\sim N(0,1).$$
则
$$C=\sigma\sqrt T\,E[Z^+].$$
由于
$$E[Z^+]=\int_0^\infty z\phi(z)\,dz=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}},$$
所以
$$C=\frac{\sigma\sqrt T}{\sqrt{2\pi}}.$$
因此
$$\sigma=C\sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{T}}.$$

problems.titleEn: Question 2.23 - Sketch the value of a vanilla call and a digital call as a function of spot
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.23 - 将普通看涨期权和数字看涨期权的价值绘制为即期函数
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Sketch the value of a vanilla call and a digital call as a function of spot. Relate the two.
problems.promptZh: 将普通看涨期权和数字看涨期权的价值绘制为即期函数。将两者联系起来。
problems.explanation:
For a sketch of a vanilla call see Figure 2.1 and for a digital call see Figure 2.3. When relating the two graphs the key features are:
- As spot increases, the slope of the call option price increases until it approaches a constant slope of 1.
- The digital call option price flattens out as the option approaches deep in-the-money, because the digital payoff is capped at 1.
- A vanilla call option price is convex; a digital call price is not. The digital call looks like a cumulative distribution function.

Here is a related question: plot a digital put on the same graph as a digital call. For what value of spot will the two lines intersect?
problems.explanationEn:
For a sketch of a vanilla call see Figure 2.1 and for a digital call see Figure 2.3. When relating the two graphs the key features are:
- As spot increases, the slope of the call option price increases until it approaches a constant slope of 1.
- The digital call option price flattens out as the option approaches deep in-the-money, because the digital payoff is capped at 1.
- A vanilla call option price is convex; a digital call price is not. The digital call looks like a cumulative distribution function.

Here is a related question: plot a digital put on the same graph as a digital call. For what value of spot will the two lines intersect?
problems.explanationZh:
普通看涨期权可参考图 2.1，数字看涨期权可参考图 2.3。比较两张图时，关键特征是：
- 随着现货价格上升，普通看涨期权价格的斜率上升，并逐渐接近常数斜率 1。
- 数字看涨期权价格在深度实值时趋于平坦，因为数字收益被上限 1 截断。
- 普通看涨期权价格是凸的；数字看涨期权价格不是凸的，它更像累计分布函数。

相关问题：把数字看跌期权和数字看涨期权画在同一张图上，它们在什么现货价格处相交？

problems.titleEn: Question 2.25 - What is the fair price for FX Euro/dollar in one year
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.25 - 一年内外汇欧元/美元的合理价格是多少
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What is the fair price for FX Euro/dollar in one year? Risk free rates and spot exchange rate given.
problems.promptZh: 一年内外汇欧元/美元的合理价格是多少？给出无风险利率和即期汇率。
problems.answer:
If $S$ is the spot rate in euros per dollar, $r_{\mathrm{EUR}}$ is the euro rate, and $r_{\mathrm{USD}}$ is the dollar rate, the one-year forward rate is
$$F=S\frac{1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}}}{1+r_{\mathrm{USD}}}.$$
problems.answerEn:
If $S$ is the spot rate in euros per dollar, $r_{\mathrm{EUR}}$ is the euro rate, and $r_{\mathrm{USD}}$ is the dollar rate, the one-year forward rate is
$$F=S\frac{1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}}}{1+r_{\mathrm{USD}}}.$$
problems.answerZh:
若 $S$ 为欧元/美元即期汇率（每 1 美元对应多少欧元），$r_{\mathrm{EUR}}$ 为欧元利率，$r_{\mathrm{USD}}$ 为美元利率，则一年远期汇率为
$$F=S\frac{1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}}}{1+r_{\mathrm{USD}}}.$$
problems.explanation:
Let $S$ be euros per dollar. Buy 1 dollar today by borrowing $S$ euros. After one year, the dollar investment is worth $1+r_{\mathrm{USD}}$ dollars, while the euro loan has grown to $S(1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}})$ euros.

If the one-year forward rate is $F$ euros per dollar, no arbitrage requires
$$F(1+r_{\mathrm{USD}})=S(1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}}).$$
Therefore
$$F=S\frac{1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}}}{1+r_{\mathrm{USD}}}.$$
problems.explanationEn:
Let $S$ be euros per dollar. Buy 1 dollar today by borrowing $S$ euros. After one year, the dollar investment is worth $1+r_{\mathrm{USD}}$ dollars, while the euro loan has grown to $S(1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}})$ euros.

If the one-year forward rate is $F$ euros per dollar, no arbitrage requires
$$F(1+r_{\mathrm{USD}})=S(1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}}).$$
Therefore
$$F=S\frac{1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}}}{1+r_{\mathrm{USD}}}.$$
problems.explanationZh:
令 $S$ 表示每 1 美元对应多少欧元。今天借入 $S$ 欧元并买入 1 美元。一年后，美元投资变为 $1+r_{\mathrm{USD}}$ 美元，而欧元贷款变为 $S(1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}})$ 欧元。

若一年远期汇率为 $F$ 欧元/美元，无套利要求
$$F(1+r_{\mathrm{USD}})=S(1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}}).$$
因此
$$F=S\frac{1+r_{\mathrm{EUR}}}{1+r_{\mathrm{USD}}}.$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: FX forward no-arbitrage formula had corrupted variables and stray characters.

problems.titleEn: Question 2.26 - Digital option paying if one asset outperforms another
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.26 - 一个资产超过另一个资产时支付的数字期权
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: An option pays 1 at time T if S1(T) > S2(T), and 0 otherwise. If the volatility of S1 increases while the other inputs are fixed, what happens to the value of the option?
problems.promptZh: 一个期权在时间 T 若满足 S1(T) > S2(T) 则支付 1，否则支付 0。若在其他输入固定时 S1 的波动率上升，该期权价值会如何变化？
problems.answer: Assuming the other asset's volatility and correlation are fixed, increasing the volatility of S1 lowers the digital's value if it is currently in the money (S1 > S2), but raises its value if it is currently out of the money (S1 < S2).
problems.answerEn: Assuming the other asset's volatility and correlation are fixed, increasing the volatility of S1 lowers the digital's value if it is currently in the money (S1 > S2), but raises its value if it is currently out of the money (S1 < S2).
problems.answerZh: 在另一个资产的波动率和相关性固定时，若当前价内（S1 > S2），提高 S1 的波动率会降低该数字期权价值；若当前价外（S1 < S2），提高 S1 的波动率会提高其价值。
problems.explanation:
This is a digital version of an exchange option. Holding the volatility of $S_2$ and the correlation fixed, higher volatility of $S_1$ spreads out the distribution of the relative performance $S_1/S_2$.

If the option is currently in the money, $S_1>S_2$, extra volatility increases the chance that $S_1$ finishes below $S_2$, so the digital value decreases.

If the option is currently out of the money, $S_1<S_2$, extra volatility gives $S_1$ more chance to overtake $S_2$, so the digital value increases.

At or near the money, the effect is more delicate and depends on the precise model inputs, but the interview intuition is that volatility helps out-of-the-money digitals and hurts in-the-money digitals.
problems.explanationEn:
This is a digital version of an exchange option. Holding the volatility of $S_2$ and the correlation fixed, higher volatility of $S_1$ spreads out the distribution of the relative performance $S_1/S_2$.

If the option is currently in the money, $S_1>S_2$, extra volatility increases the chance that $S_1$ finishes below $S_2$, so the digital value decreases.

If the option is currently out of the money, $S_1<S_2$, extra volatility gives $S_1$ more chance to overtake $S_2$, so the digital value increases.

At or near the money, the effect is more delicate and depends on the precise model inputs, but the interview intuition is that volatility helps out-of-the-money digitals and hurts in-the-money digitals.
problems.explanationZh:
这是交换期权的数字版本。在 $S_2$ 的波动率和相关性固定时，提高 $S_1$ 的波动率会拉宽相对表现 $S_1/S_2$ 的分布。

如果该期权当前价内，即 $S_1>S_2$，额外波动会提高 $S_1$ 到期低于 $S_2$ 的概率，因此数字期权价值下降。

如果该期权当前价外，即 $S_1<S_2$，额外波动给了 $S_1$ 更大概率超过 $S_2$，因此数字期权价值上升。

在平值附近，影响更依赖具体模型输入；但面试直觉是：波动率有利于价外数字期权，不利于价内数字期权。

problems.titleEn: Question 2.27 - In the pricing of options, why doesn't it matter if the stock price exhibits mean reversion
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.27 - 在期权定价中，为什么股价是否呈现均值回归并不重要
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers

problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: In the pricing of options, why doesn't it matter if the stock price exhibits mean reversion?
problems.promptZh: 在期权定价中，为什么股价是否呈现均值回归并不重要？
problems.explanation:
A possible mean reversion process for a stock is

where a is the mean reversion rate and yz is the long term mean. These are the real world dynamics of the stock process, but to price an option we are only interested in the risk-neutral dynamics. When we move to the risk-neutral dynamics the drift of the stock becomes rdt as we saw in Question 2.1. Therefore the real world drift, be it mean reverting or not, does not change the price of the option. Here are some possible related questions: e What is the financial interpretation if the drift of a stock is equal to r? e When changing between probability measures, what happens to the volatility? O

股票可能的均值回归过程是

其中 a 是平均回归率，yz 是长期平均值。这些是股票过程的现实世界动态，但为了给期权定价，我们只对风险中性动态感兴趣。当我们转向风险中性动态时，股票的漂移就变成了 rdt，正如我们在问题 2.1 中看到的那样。因此，现实世界的漂移，无论是否意味着恢复，都不会改变期权的价格。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 如果股票的漂移等于 r，财务解释是什么？ e 当概率度量之间发生变化时，波动率会发生什么变化？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.28 - What are the limits/boundaries for the price of a call option on a non-dividend paying stock
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.28 - 不派息股票的看涨期权价格的限制/界限是什么
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What are the limits/boundaries for the price of a call option on a non-dividend paying stock?
problems.promptZh:
不派息股票的看涨期权价格的限制/界限是什么？

2.2.问题
problems.explanation: We have already seen that the price of a European call option on a non-dividend paying stock is always more than its intrinsic value, see Question 2.9. What about the upper bound? A call option gives the holder the right, but not the obligation to purchase a stock at a future point in time. So at expiry the most we can hold is one stock. Therefore the option cannot be worth more than the stock price as our maximum payoff is the stock price. The price bounds for a European call $C_t$ on a non-dividend paying stock $S_t$ with strike K are S:-KBi< Ch < St, where $B_t$ is the price of a zero-coupon bond expiring at the same time as the option. Here are some possible related questions: e If we denote a call option by C(K,T), where K is the strike and T is the expiry, what can we say about the function C' in terms of K? e What can we say about C(K,T) in terms of T'? (Assume non-dividend paying stock.) O

The price bounds for a European call $C_t$ on a non-dividend paying stock $S_t$ with strike K are S:-KBi< Ch < St, where $B_t$ is the price of a zero-coupon bond expiring at the same time as the option. Here are some possible related questions: e If we denote a call option by C(K,T), where K is the strike and T is the expiry, what can we say about the function C' in terms of K? e What can we say about C(K,T) in terms of T'? (Assume non-dividend paying stock.) O
problems.explanationZh:
我们已经看到，不支付股息的股票的欧式看涨期权的价格总是高于其内在价值，见问题 2.9。上限又如何呢？看涨期权赋予持有人在未来某个时间点购买股票的权利，但没有义务。因此，到期时我们最多可以持有一只股票。因此，期权的价值不能超过股票价格，因为我们的最大回报就是股票价格。欧式看涨期权 C 的价格界限；不支付股息的股票 S；带有 K 的 S:-KBi< Ch < St，其中 B；是与期权同时到期的零息债券的价格。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 如果我们用 C(K,T) 表示看涨期权，其中 K 是行使价，T 是到期日，那么我们可以用 K 来描述函数 C' 吗？ e 我们可以用 T' 来描述 C(K,T) 吗？ （假设不派息股票。） O

problems.titleEn: Question 2.29 - What is the value of a call option for a 98th percentile fall in the stock price
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.29 - 股价下跌 98% 时，看涨期权的价值是多少
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What is the value of a call option for a 98th percentile fall in the stock price?
problems.promptZh: 股价下跌 98% 时，看涨期权的价值是多少？
problems.explanation: This question relates to the concept of Value at Risk (VAR), which is the key measurement for the risk in a bank's trading book. If applying for a job in Risk Management it is very important that you know what VAR is and that you also know some of the common calculation methods, see [8] or [4]. To answer the question we will need to assume a distribution for the stock price. We will work in the Black-Scholes world where the stock is distributed as oe $S_T$ = Sp exp \{ (r - 5) T+oVTN(0,1)\}, where V(0,1) is a standard normal random variable. Finding the 98th percentile move in the stock requires looking up the value where 98 percent of the standard normal's probability lies above this value. Using the inverse normal the value is - 2.05. We will assume some initial market values Sg = 100, r = 0, T = 1, and o = 0.1. Using these parameters the price of call option with strike 100 is 3.99, using the Black-Scholes formula. We now calculate the 98th percentile fall in the stock price over the coming year. If the stock price fell this much it would be worth 100 exp(0.5(0.17) + 0.1(—2.05)) = 81.03. This translates to the call option being worth a measly 0.06. Here are some possible related questions: e How would the 98th percentile move in the stock price vary with time? e What if the stock had a distribution with 'fatter tails' than a log-normal distribution. Would the value of the call be more or less? O

problems.explanationZh: 这个问题涉及风险价值（VAR）的概念，它是衡量银行交易账户风险的关键指标。如果申请风险管理职位，了解 VAR 是什么以及一些常见的计算方法非常重要，请参阅 [8] 或 [4]。为了回答这个问题，我们需要假设股票价格的分布。我们将在 Black-Scholes 世界中工作，其中股票分布为 oe Sr = Sp exp \{ (r— 5) T+oVTN(0,1)\}，其中 V(0,1) 是标准正态随机变量。要查找股票的第 98 个百分位变动，需要查找 98% 的标准正态概率高于该值的值。使用逆法线，该值为 —2.05。我们假设一些初始市场价值 Sg = 100、r = 0、T = 1 和 o = 0.1。使用这些参数，使用 Black-Scholes 公式，行使价为 100 的看涨期权价格为 3.99。我们现在计算来年股价下跌 98 个百分点。如果股价下跌这么多，则价值 100 exp(0.5(0.17) + 0.1(—2.05)) = 81.03。这意味着看涨期权的价值仅为 0.06。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 股价的第 98 个百分位将如何随时间变化？ e 如果股票的分布比对数正态分布“更肥尾”怎么办？通话的价值会增加还是减少？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.30 - What is the price of a call option where the underlying is the forward price of a stock
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.30 - 标的物为股票远期价格的看涨期权的价格是多少
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What is the price of a call option where the underlying is the forward price of a stock?
problems.promptZh: 标的物为股票远期价格的看涨期权的价格是多少？
problems.answer:
Black's formula:
$$C=e^{-rT}\left(FN(d_1)-KN(d_2)\right),$$
where
$$d_1=\frac{\ln(F/K)+\frac12\sigma^2T}{\sigma\sqrt T},\qquad d_2=d_1-\sigma\sqrt T.$$
problems.answerEn:
Black's formula:
$$C=e^{-rT}\left(FN(d_1)-KN(d_2)\right),$$
where
$$d_1=\frac{\ln(F/K)+\frac12\sigma^2T}{\sigma\sqrt T},\qquad d_2=d_1-\sigma\sqrt T.$$
problems.answerZh:
Black 公式为
$$C=e^{-rT}\left(FN(d_1)-KN(d_2)\right),$$
其中
$$d_1=\frac{\ln(F/K)+\frac12\sigma^2T}{\sigma\sqrt T},\qquad d_2=d_1-\sigma\sqrt T.$$
problems.explanation:
If the underlying is the forward price $F$ and $F$ is modeled as lognormal under the appropriate forward measure, the call option is priced by Black's formula:
$$C=e^{-rT}\left(FN(d_1)-KN(d_2)\right),$$
where
$$d_1=\frac{\ln(F/K)+\frac12\sigma^2T}{\sigma\sqrt T},\qquad d_2=d_1-\sigma\sqrt T.$$
This is the same structural formula used for many interest-rate options, such as caps and swaptions, with the forward replacing the spot as the underlying.
problems.explanationEn:
If the underlying is the forward price $F$ and $F$ is modeled as lognormal under the appropriate forward measure, the call option is priced by Black's formula:
$$C=e^{-rT}\left(FN(d_1)-KN(d_2)\right),$$
where
$$d_1=\frac{\ln(F/K)+\frac12\sigma^2T}{\sigma\sqrt T},\qquad d_2=d_1-\sigma\sqrt T.$$
This is the same structural formula used for many interest-rate options, such as caps and swaptions, with the forward replacing the spot as the underlying.
problems.explanationZh:
若标的是远期价格 $F$，并且在合适的远期测度下 $F$ 服从对数正态分布，则看涨期权由 Black 公式定价：
$$C=e^{-rT}\left(FN(d_1)-KN(d_2)\right),$$
其中
$$d_1=\frac{\ln(F/K)+\frac12\sigma^2T}{\sigma\sqrt T},\qquad d_2=d_1-\sigma\sqrt T.$$
许多利率期权（如 cap 和 swaption）也使用同样结构的公式，只是用远期价格替代现货作为标的。

problems.titleEn: Question 2.31 - Prove that the price of a call option is a convex function of the strike price
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.31 - 证明看涨期权的价格是执行价格的凸函数
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Prove that the price of a call option is a convex function of the strike price.
problems.promptZh: 证明看涨期权的价格是执行价格的凸函数。 2.2.3.对冲和复制。
problems.explanation:
The following is based on the proof in Section 2.8 of [6]. To prove the call option is a convex function of the strike price we consider the price as a function of strike, and prove that a line joining any two points on the graph of this function lies on or above the graph. This is a necessary and sufficient property of convex functions. Let C(K) denote the call option price as a function of strike AK'. Then this is equivalent to saying, for Ky, < Ko, for allO<@< 1. The important thing to note here is that the final payoff is convex in strike for a fixed value in the underlying, that is the function ($S_T$ - K)* is convex in K. With this in mind, consider a portfolio long @ call options struck at Ay, long (1 - @) options struck at K and short one option struck at 6K, + (1 - 4) Ke (all options with the same maturity). Since the final payoff is

Sl convex in strike, (2.3) holds at the expiry of the options and we therefore have that our portfolio has non-negative value at expiry. This implies, by no-arbitrage considerations, that the portfolio must be of non-negative value at all times before expiry, and hence (2.3) holds at these times and we have proved convexity of the call option price. Here are some possible related questions: e Sketch the shape of the value of a call option as a function of strike. e Prove that a portfolio with non-negative value at expiry must have non-negative value at all times before expiry. e What other property (besides the one used in the proof above) does a convex function have? e In the Black-Scholes model, prove the call option price is a convex function of spot. O

problems.titleEn: Question 2.32 - What uses could an option be put to
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.32 - 选项可以有什么用途
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What uses could an option be put to?
problems.promptZh: 选项可以有什么用途？
problems.explanation:
The short answer to any question about participation in the financial markets is that there are three sorts of behaviours: e hedging, e speculation, e arbitrage. A hedger uses financial products to reduce risk. For example, an exporter who is paid in foreign currency may use an option to guard against the possibility that the exchange rate moves against him. A speculator takes on risk with the belief either that the compensation for the additional risk makes it worthwhile or because he has specific views on how an asset will behave, and the product allows him to make a profit from these views if they are correct. An arbitrageur exploits mis-pricing in the market, and makes money by buying risk in one place and selling it in another.

Here are some possible related questions: e Which category do you think most participants in the market fall into? e Which category do you think this bank falls into? e Which category do you think personal investors fall into? O
problems.explanationEn:
The short answer to any question about participation in the financial markets is that there are three sorts of behaviours: e hedging, e speculation, e arbitrage. A hedger uses financial products to reduce risk. For example, an exporter who is paid in foreign currency may use an option to guard against the possibility that the exchange rate moves against him. A speculator takes on risk with the belief either that the compensation for the additional risk makes it worthwhile or because he has specific views on how an asset will behave, and the product allows him to make a profit from these views if they are correct. An arbitrageur exploits mis-pricing in the market, and makes money by buying risk in one place and selling it in another.

Here are some possible related questions: e Which category do you think most participants in the market fall into? e Which category do you think this bank falls into? e Which category do you think personal investors fall into? O
problems.explanationZh:
对任何有关金融市场参与问题的简短回答是，存在三种行为：对冲、投机、套利。对冲者使用金融产品来降低风险。例如，以外币付款的出口商可以使用期权来防范汇率变动对他不利的可能性。投机者承担风险的信念要么是额外风险的补偿使其值得，要么是因为他对资产的行为有特定的看法，并且如果这些观点是正确的，该产品允许他从这些观点中获利。套利者利用市场上的错误定价，通过在一个地方购买风险并在另一个地方出售来赚钱。

以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 您认为市场上的大多数参与者属于哪一类？ e 您认为这家银行属于哪一类？ e 您认为个人投资者属于哪一类？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.33 - Suppose spot today is 90
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.33 - 假设今天的现货价格为 90
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Suppose spot today is 90. A call option is struck at 100 and expires in one year. There are no interest rates. Spot moves log-normally in a perfect Black-Scholes world. I claim that I can hedge the option for free. Whenever spot crosses 100 in an upwards direction I borrow 100 and buy the stock. Whenever spot crosses 100 in a downwards direction I sell the stock and repay my loan. At expiry either the option is out-of-the-money in which case I have no position or it is in-the-money and I use the 100 dollar strike to payoff my loan. Thus the option has been hedged for free. Where is the error in this argument?
problems.promptZh: 假设今天的现货价格为 90。看涨期权的执行价格为 100，并在一年后到期。没有利率。在完美的布莱克-斯科尔斯世界中，现货的移动是对数正常的。我声称我可以免费对冲该期权。每当现货价格向上突破 100 时，我就会借入 100 并买入该股票。每当现货价格向下突破 100 时，我就会卖出股票并偿还贷款。到期时，期权要么是价外期权，在这种情况下我没有头寸，要么期权是价内期权，我使用 100 美元的履约金来偿还我的贷款。因此，该期权已被免费对冲。这个说法的错误在哪里？
problems.explanation: This is a tricky one in that you often find that the questioner does not really understand the problem either. The fundamental problem is that the hedging strategy is not well defined. For example, when you first touch 100, how do you know whether the stock will go up or down? It is not clear at that time whether 100 will be crossed so you cannot know what to hold. Thus you really need to buy at 100+ ∈ and sell at 100 -. You then lose 2t for each crossing, and the strategy is no longer riskless. The properties of Brownian motion actually make the problem worse. If it hits a level then it will hit it again infinitely often in an arbitrarily small amount of time. So even in retrospect, it's hard to know when the crossing occurred. Here are some possible related questions: e Do you think this would be an effective approach in practice? e Does the fact that you do not know which way the stock would go when it hits 100 make sense financially? O

Here are some possible related questions: e Do you think this would be an effective approach in practice? e Does the fact that you do not know which way the stock would go when it hits 100 make sense financially? problems.explanationZh: 这是一个棘手的问题，因为你经常会发现提问者也没有真正理解问题。根本问题是对冲策略没有明确定义。例如，当你第一次触及100时，你怎么知道股票会上涨还是下跌？当时还不清楚是否会突破100，所以你不知道该持有什么。因此，您确实需要以 100+ ∈ 的价格买入并以 100 — 欧元的价格卖出。然后，每次交叉您都会损失 2 美分，并且该策略不再是无风险的。布朗运动的特性实际上使问题变得更糟。如果它达到一个水平，那么它会在任意短的时间内无限次地再次达到该水平。因此，即使回想起来，也很难知道穿越是什么时候发生的。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 您认为这在实践中是一种有效的方法吗？ e 当你不知道股票触及 100 点时它会走向何方这一事实在财务上有意义吗？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.34 - Team A plays team B, in a series of 7 games, whoever wins 4 games first wins

tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Team A plays team B, in a series of 7 games, whoever wins 4 games first wins. You want to bet 100 that your team wins the series, in which case you receive 200, or 0 if they lose. However the broker only allows bets on individual games. You can bet X on any individual game the day before it occurs to receive 2.X if it wins and0 if it loses. How do you achieve the desired pay-out? In particular, what do you bet on the first match?
problems.promptZh: A队与B队进行7场系列赛，谁先赢4局谁就获胜。您想下注 100，您的球队将赢得系列赛，在这种情况下，您将获得 200，如果他们输了，您将获得 0。然而，经纪人只允许对单场比赛进行投注。您可以在任何一场比赛发生前一天对任何一场比赛下注 X，如果获胜则获得 2.X，如果失败则获得 0。您如何实现预期的支付？特别是第一场比赛你押注什么？
problems.answer: Bet 31.25 on team A in the first match, then dynamically rebalance after each game using the same binomial-tree replication logic.
problems.answerEn: Bet 31.25 on team A in the first match, then dynamically rebalance after each game using the same binomial-tree replication logic.
problems.answerZh: 第一场比赛押 31.25 在 A 队上；之后每场比赛后按同样的二叉树复制逻辑动态调整投注。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Series betting hedge had no explicit first-bet answer and formulas were corrupted.

problems.titleEn: Question 2.39 - What is pricing by replication
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.39 - 什么是复制定价
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What is pricing by replication?
problems.promptZh: 什么是复制定价？
problems.explanation: Pricing by replication involves trading in financial instruments in such a way as to match the payoff of another instrument that is most likely exotic. For example, a barrier option can be priced using a portfolio of vanilla options whose payoff matches that of the barrier option. See Section 10.2 of [6] for details. One of the main benefits of pricing exotic options by (static) replication with vanilla options is that the exotic will be priced in a way that is consistent with the current volatility smile. There are two main types of replication methods that one should be aware of: static and dynamic. Static replication involves setting up a portfolio today and then not having to do any further trading (or at most trading ona finite number of future dates). Dynamic replication involves continuously trading in the underlying and is the method required to hedge an option using the stock and bond in the Black-Scholes model. Here are some possible related questions: e What are some of the practical problems of dynamic replication? e Given the choice between two different static replicating portfolios that match an option's payoff, what criteria would you use to decide between the two? O
problems.explanationEn: Pricing by replication involves trading in financial instruments in such a way as to match the payoff of another instrument that is most likely exotic.
