One of the main benefits of pricing exotic options by (static) replication with vanilla options is that the exotic will be priced in a way that is consistent with the current volatility smile. There are two main types of replication methods that one should be aware of: static and dynamic. Static replication involves setting up a portfolio today and then not having to do any further trading (or at most trading ona finite number of future dates). Dynamic replication involves continuously trading in the underlying and is the method required to hedge an option using the stock and bond in the Black-Scholes model. Here are some possible related questions: e What are some of the practical problems of dynamic replication? e Given the choice between two different static replicating portfolios that match an option's payoff, what criteria would you use to decide between the two? O
problems.explanationZh: 复制定价涉及以与另一种很可能是奇异的工具的收益相匹配的方式进行金融工具交易。例如，障碍期权可以使用其收益与障碍期权的收益相匹配的普通期权投资组合进行定价。详细信息请参见[6]的第10.2节。通过（静态）复制普通期权来定价奇异期权的主要好处之一是，奇异期权的定价方式将与当前的波动率微笑一致。应该注意两种主要类型的复制方法：静态和动态。静态复制涉及今天建立一个投资组合，然后不必进行任何进一步的交易（或最多在有限数量的未来日期进行交易）。动态复制涉及标的资产的连续交易，是使用 Black-Scholes 模型中的股票和债券对冲期权所需的方法。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 动态复制有哪些实际问题？ e 假设要在两个不同的静态复制投资组合（与期权收益相匹配）之间进行选择，您将使用什么标准在两者之间做出决定？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.41 - The statistics department from our bank tell you that the stock price has followed a mean reversion process for the last 10 years,
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.41 - 我行统计部门告诉您，近10年来股价遵循均值回归过程，年波动率为10%，日波动率为20%
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: The statistics department from our bank tell you that the stock price has followed a mean reversion process for the last 10 years, with annual volatility 10% and daily volatility 20%. You want to sell a European option and hedge it, which volatility do you use?
problems.promptZh: 我行统计部门告诉您，近10年来股价遵循均值回归过程，年波动率为10%，日波动率为20%。您想出售欧式期权并对其进行对冲，您会使用哪种波动率？
problems.answer: Use the 20% daily hedging volatility for pricing and hedging the short European option, because the dynamic hedge is exposed to short-horizon moves. The lower long-run mean-reversion volatility would underprice the option.
problems.answerEn: Use the 20% daily hedging volatility for pricing and hedging the short European option, because the dynamic hedge is exposed to short-horizon moves. The lower long-run mean-reversion volatility would underprice the option.
problems.answerZh: 应使用 20% 的日度对冲波动率来为卖出的欧式期权定价和对冲，因为动态对冲暴露于短期价格波动。较低的长期均值回归波动率会低估期权。
problems.explanation:
For a short option position, the practical question is the volatility of the moves you must hedge. If you delta hedge daily, your hedging error is driven by daily stock moves, not by the long-run mean-reverting behavior over ten years.

Since European option values increase with volatility, using the lower long-run annual volatility would underprice the option and leave the seller undercompensated for short-horizon hedging risk. Under the assumptions in the prompt, use the 20% daily hedging volatility.
problems.explanationEn:
For a short option position, the practical question is the volatility of the moves you must hedge. If you delta hedge daily, your hedging error is driven by daily stock moves, not by the long-run mean-reverting behavior over ten years.

Since European option values increase with volatility, using the lower long-run annual volatility would underprice the option and leave the seller undercompensated for short-horizon hedging risk. Under the assumptions in the prompt, use the 20% daily hedging volatility.
problems.explanationZh:
对卖出的期权头寸，关键问题是你需要对冲的价格波动尺度。如果每天做 delta 对冲，对冲误差由日度股价变动驱动，而不是由十年尺度上的长期均值回归行为驱动。

由于欧式期权价值随波动率增加而增加，使用较低的长期年化波动率会低估期权，使卖方没有获得足够的短期对冲风险补偿。在题目假设下，应使用 20% 的日度对冲波动率。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Mean-reversion volatility answer contained unrelated spillover text.

The pathwise method has a few advantages: it is an unbiased estimate, only requires simulation for one value of @ and is usually more accurate than a finite-difference approximation. It does become more complicated when the payoff is discontinuous (e.g. digital or barrier options), but to get around this we can write f = g+h, with g continuous and h piecewise constant. One advantage with the likelihood ratio method is that only one value of @ needs to be simulated to calculate both the price and the sensitivity. The positivity of Gamma is due to call and put options being convex functions of spot as the second derivative for any convex function is positive. Why does the Gamma become more spiked as maturity approaches? The derivative of Delta will reflect this jump and therefore produce a spiked Gamma. problems.titleZh: 问题 2.45 - 假设如果现货保持在 K1 到 2 的范围内，则期权支付 1，否则支付 0
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers

problems.promptEn: Suppose an option pays one if spot stays in a range K1 to 2 and zero otherwise. What can we say about the Vega?
problems.promptZh: 假设如果现货保持在 K1 到 2 的范围内，则期权支付 1，否则支付 0。关于维加我们能说什么？
problems.explanation: This option, known as a double no-touch, will have a negative Vega. Vega measures the change in option price for a 1% change in volatility. If volatility increases then the probability in the risk-neutral measure of hitting either barrier K1 or K2 increases and so the option price will decrease, giving a negative Vega. Here are some possible related questions: e@ What about the Vega for a double one-touch? e Sketch the Vega for a digital call option and explain its features. O
problems.explanationEn: This option, known as a double no-touch, will have a negative Vega. Vega measures the change in option price for a 1% change in volatility. If volatility increases then the probability in the risk-neutral measure of hitting either barrier K1 or K2 increases and so the option price will decrease, giving a negative Vega. Here are some possible related questions: e@ What about the Vega for a double one-touch? e Sketch the Vega for a digital call option and explain its features. O
problems.explanationZh: 此选项称为双无接触，将产生负 Vega。 Vega 衡量波动率变化 1% 时期权价格的变化。如果波动性增加，那么风险中性指标触及 K1 或 K2 障碍的概率就会增加，因此期权价格将会下降，从而产生负 Vega。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e@ 双一触式 Vega 怎么样？ e 绘制数字呼叫选项 Vega 的草图并解释其功能。氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.46 - All being equal, which option has higher Vega
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.46 - 在所有条件相同的情况下，哪个选项的 Vega 更高
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: All being equal, which option has higher Vega? An at-themoney European call option with spot 100 or an at-the-money European call option with spot 200? (A structurer asked this question and didn't want formulas.)
problems.promptZh: 在所有条件相同的情况下，哪个选项的 Vega 更高？现货 100 的平价欧式看涨期权还是现货 200 的平价欧式看涨期权？ （结构师问了这个问题并且不需要公式。）
problems.explanation:
One way to answer this question is to firstly think about how the values of the two options differ. Both of these options are at-the-money, so doubling the value of spot will double the strike and hence the value of the option, the factor of two multiplies through. Therefore the option with spot 200 (option B) will be worth twice as much as the option with spot 100 (option A). The Vega of option B will be twice the Vega of option A, as the two will multiply through the Vega as well.

Here are some possible related questions: e What happens when the spot of each option moves by say 10%, will the relationship between the Vegas change? e Consider the same two options, what can we say about the difference between the Gammas? O
problems.explanationEn:
One way to answer this question is to firstly think about how the values of the two options differ. Both of these options are at-the-money, so doubling the value of spot will double the strike and hence the value of the option, the factor of two multiplies through. Therefore the option with spot 200 (option B) will be worth twice as much as the option with spot 100 (option A). The Vega of option B will be twice the Vega of option A, as the two will multiply through the Vega as well.

Here are some possible related questions: e What happens when the spot of each option moves by say 10%, will the relationship between the Vegas change? e Consider the same two options, what can we say about the difference between the Gammas? O
problems.explanationZh:
回答这个问题的一种方法是首先考虑两个选项的值有何不同。这两种期权都是平价期权，因此现货价值加倍将使履约价格加倍，因此期权的价值（因子 2）也会乘以。因此，现货 200 的期权（选项 B）的价值将是现货 100 的期权（选项 A）的两倍。选项 B 的 Vega 将是选项 A 的 Vega 的两倍，因为两者也会通过 Vega 相乘。

以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 当每个期权的现货移动例如 10% 时会发生什么，维加斯之间的关系会发生变化吗？ e 考虑相同的两个选项，我们能说一下 Gamma 之间的差异吗？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.47 - How do you construct a Vega neutral portfolio with vanilla call and put options
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.47 - 如何构建包含普通看涨期权和看跌期权的 Vega 中性投资组合
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: How do you construct a Vega neutral portfolio with vanilla call and put options?
problems.promptZh:
如何构建包含普通看涨期权和看跌期权的 Vega 中性投资组合？

2.2.问题 2.2.5。一般的。
problems.explanation: Firstly we need to know what the current Vega of the portfolio is. The Vega for the portfolio will be the sum of all the individual instrument's Vegas, so we find these and add them up. If this value is non-zero we will have to use an option to make it zero. We can use either a call or put as the Vega of a vanilla call is the same as a put, due to put-call parity and the forward contract having zero Vega. We take an option which expires at a similar time to the portfolio and find its Vega. We then buy/sell enough of these options to make the Vega of our portfolio zero. Here are some possible related questions: e Assuming Black-Scholes and given our portfolio is now Vega neutral, will it be Gamma neutral? e If we don't believe in Black-Scholes will our portfolio be Gamma neutral if it is Vega neutral? O
problems.explanationEn: Firstly we need to know what the current Vega of the portfolio is. The Vega for the portfolio will be the sum of all the individual instrument's Vegas, so we find these and add them up. If this value is non-zero we will have to use an option to make it zero. We can use either a call or put as the Vega of a vanilla call is the same as a put, due to put-call parity and the forward contract having zero Vega. We take an option which expires at a similar time to the portfolio and find its Vega. We then buy/sell enough of these options to make the Vega of our portfolio zero. Here are some possible related questions: e Assuming Black-Scholes and given our portfolio is now Vega neutral, will it be Gamma neutral? e If we don't believe in Black-Scholes will our portfolio be Gamma neutral if it is Vega neutral? O
problems.explanationZh: 首先我们需要知道投资组合中当前的 Vega 是什么。投资组合的 Vega 将是所有单个工具的 Vegas 的总和，因此我们找到这些并将它们相加。如果该值非零，我们将不得不使用一个选项使其为零。我们可以使用看涨期权或看跌期权，因为普通看涨期权的 Vega 与看跌期权相同，因为看跌期权平价且远期合约的 Vega 为零。我们选择一个与投资组合同时到期的期权，并找到它的 Vega。然后，我们买入/卖出足够多的这些期权，使我们投资组合的 Vega 为零。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 假设 Black-Scholes 并考虑到我们的投资组合现在是 Vega 中性的，那么它会是 Gamma 中性的吗？ e 如果我们不相信 Black-Scholes，那么我们的投资组合如果是 Vega 中性的，那么它会是 Gamma 中性的吗？ O 2.3.5。一般的。

problems.titleEn: Question 2.48 - How accurate do you think a pricing function should be
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.48 - 您认为定价函数应该有多准确
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: How accurate do you think a pricing function should be?
problems.promptZh: 您认为定价函数应该有多准确？
problems.explanation:
There are many ways to answer this question. The answer the interviewer was looking for was 1E - 4, that is one basis point. Another approach is to consider the uncertainty in the inputs. For example, volatility is estimated rather than observed so an error that is small compared to one Vega is sufficient.

One could also mention that it is error as a fraction of the uncertain part of the price that is important, not as a fraction of the total price. For example, a deeply-in-the-money option is almost all intrinsic, it is the estimate of the value of the optionality that matters. Here are some possible related questions e How much is the vega on a one-year at-the-money call option if the stock price is 100? e If your were model were inaccurate, how would you spot the fact? O
problems.explanationEn:
There are many ways to answer this question. The answer the interviewer was looking for was 1E - 4, that is one basis point. Another approach is to consider the uncertainty in the inputs. For example, volatility is estimated rather than observed so an error that is small compared to one Vega is sufficient.

One could also mention that it is error as a fraction of the uncertain part of the price that is important, not as a fraction of the total price. For example, a deeply-in-the-money option is almost all intrinsic, it is the estimate of the value of the optionality that matters. Here are some possible related questions e How much is the vega on a one-year at-the-money call option if the stock price is 100? e If your were model were inaccurate, how would you spot the fact? O
problems.explanationZh:
有很多方法可以回答这个问题。面试官寻找的答案是 1E - 4，这是一个基点。另一种方法是考虑输入的不确定性。例如，波动性是估计的而不是观察到的，因此与 Vega 相比较小的误差就足够了。

2.3.解决方案人们还可以提到，重要的是错误是价格中不确定部分的一小部分，而不是总价格的一小部分。例如，深度价内期权几乎都是内在的，重要的是对期权价值的估计。以下是一些可能的相关问题 e 如果股价为 100，一年期平值看涨期权的 vega 是多少？ e 如果您的模型不准确，您将如何发现这一事实？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.49 - Assume you have a good trading model that you think will make money
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.49 - 假设您有一个您认为可以赚钱的良好交易模型
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Assume you have a good trading model that you think will make money. What information would you present to your manager to support your claim.
problems.promptZh: 假设您有一个您认为可以赚钱的良好交易模型。您将向您的经理提供哪些信息来支持您的主张。 2.2.6。树木和蒙特卡洛。
problems.explanation:
We will answer this question assuming two different models. Firstly we consider a statistical arbitrage model and then secondly assuming we have a model for pricing exotic derivatives. Statistical arbitrage involves exploiting small mispricings in the market, usually by buying and selling stocks to create a portfolio which has minimal risk, but potential for profit. This portfolio of stocks will often be very large, and thus requiring a large amount of computational power. Therefore one aspect of a good model is that it returns trade information in a useful time frame. What other information would you use to prove your model is a good one? A good check is to back-test the model, seeing how the trades it suggested actually worked on historic data. One could then argue if it made money in the past it should make money in the future. An important test is that it should work “out of sample” that is if we train on a set of data then it should work on real data that it has not been trained on. Typically, statistical arbitrage (also known as “stat arb” or “stab art”) works well in normal times but fails when turbulence occurs. You therefore might want to demonstrate that it still works in a period of turbulence. If you could prove that your model not only makes money in benign markets, but it also does well during a market crash, then your manager will no doubt be interested to hear more. Another point to consider is whether the trading strategy takes into account bid-offer spreads and other transaction costs. It is easy to find strategies that

work when these are ignored, and this will be one of the first things your boss seizes upon. A related issue is liquidity. Many funds and most spectacularly, LTCM, have failed because of a sudden liquidity drought. How much money will your strategy lose in such a case? Now considering exotic derivatives; the first piece of evidence to present would be that your model reproduces the prices of basic instruments. If the model cannot accurately price vanilla options and discount bonds, then traders will need a lot of convincing that it will price exotic instruments correctly. The second piece of evidence to present comes down to what a model should actually do. Apart from giving prices, a model's main purpose is to suggest a hedging strategy. Therefore a new model that provides more accurate hedging ratios, will be popular automatically. How could you provide proof that the hedging is more accurate? Once again we could run back-testing on market data. If the variance of the profit and loss to a trading book is less under your new model than the current model, the hedging is more accurate and hence you have a better model. In addition, you will have to discuss the advantages of the model over the old. These could include e speed; e realism; e ease of calibration; e ability to easily specify new products. Here are some possible related questions: e Discuss some of the main reasons why hedge funds have found themselves in trouble using statistical arbitrage models. e If you had a perfect model for a stock process, what features would the model most likely have? O

O
problems.explanationZh:
我们将假设两个不同的模型来回答这个问题。首先我们考虑统计套利模型，然后假设我们有一个为奇异衍生品定价的模型。统计套利涉及利用市场上的小错误定价，通常通过买卖股票来创建风险最小但有盈利潜力的投资组合。这种股票投资组合通常非常大，因此需要大量的计算能力。因此，好的模型的一个方面是它在有用的时间范围内返回交易信息。您还可以使用哪些其他信息来证明您的模型是一个好的模型？一个很好的检查方法是对模型进行回溯测试，看看它建议的交易如何在历史数据上实际发挥作用。那么人们可能会争辩说，如果它过去赚钱，那么它将来也应该赚钱。一个重要的测试是它应该在“样本外”工作，也就是说，如果我们在一组数据上进行训练，那么它应该在未经训练的真实数据上工作。通常，统计套利（也称为“stat arb”或“stab art”）在正常情况下运作良好，但在发生动荡时会失败。因此，您可能想证明它在动荡时期仍然有效。如果你能证明你的模型不仅在良性市场中赚钱，而且在市场崩溃期间也表现良好，那么你的经理无疑会有兴趣了解更多信息。另一个需要考虑的点是交易策略是否考虑了买卖价差和其他交易成本。很容易找到策略

当这些被忽视时，你就会开始工作，这将是你的老板首先注意到的事情之一。一个相关的问题是流动性。许多基金，尤其是长期资本管理公司（LTCM），都因突然的流动性短缺而倒闭。在这种情况下你的策略会损失多少钱？现在考虑奇异的衍生品；要提供的第一个证据是您的模型复制了基本工具的价格。如果该模型无法准确地为普通期权和贴现债券定价，那么交易者将需要大量说服力来证明它能够正确地为奇异工具定价。要呈现的第二个证据归结为模型实际上应该做什么。除了给出价格之外，模型的主要目的是提出对冲策略。因此，提供更准确的对冲比率的新模型将自然而然地流行起来。您如何提供证据证明对冲更加准确？我们可以再次对市场数据进行回溯测试。如果新模型下交易账户盈亏的方差小于当前模型，则对冲更加准确，因此您拥有更好的模型。此外，您还必须讨论该模型相对于旧模型的优点。这些可能包括 e 速度；现实主义； e 易于校准；能够轻松指定新产品。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 讨论对冲基金在使用统计套利模型时发现自己陷入困境的一些主要原因。 e 如果您有一个完美的库存流程模型，该模型最有可能具有哪些功能？氧

2.3.解决方案 2.3.6.树木和蒙特卡洛。

problems.titleEn: Question 2.50 - A stock is worth 100 today
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.50 - 今天一只股票价值 100 美元
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: A stock is worth 100 today. There are zero interest rates. The stock can be worth 90 or 110 tomorrow. It moves to 110 with probability p. Price a call option struck at 100.
problems.promptZh: 今天一只股票价值 100 美元。利率为零。明天该股票的价值可能是 90 或 110。它以概率 p 移动到 110。将看涨期权定价为 100。
problems.explanation: This is a standard trick question to see if you make the mistake of using p in the answer. This precise example is discussed in detail in [6] so if you claim to have read it, make you sure that you can do this question. The easiest solution is simply to use risk-neutral evaluation. The risk-neutral probability of an up-move is 0.5 by symmetry. The value of a call option is 10 in the up-state and 0 in the down-state so the answer is 10 x 0.5 =5, since there are no interest rates. One could also use a hedging argument. We could hold 6 stocks and minus one option. We choose 6 so that the portfolio has the same value in both states. The two values are 1106 - 10 and 906. These are equal if and only if 6 = 0.5. The value is then 45. By no arbitrage, this portfolio is also worth 45 today. So 50 - Option = 45, and the option price is 5. A third method of doing this problem is to use the stock and bond to replicate the option payoff, which obviously leads to the same value. Here are some possible related questions: e Price using the stock and bond to replicate the option payoff. e@ What about a put struck at 100? (There's a very easy solution!) e How will introducing interest rates affect the price? e Without computations, how will the price with the branches at 80 and 120 compare? O

This precise example is discussed in detail in [6] so if you claim to have read it, make you sure that you can do this question. The easiest solution is simply to use risk-neutral evaluation. The risk-neutral probability of an up-move is 0.5 by symmetry. The value of a call option is 10 in the up-state and 0 in the down-state so the answer is 10 x 0.5 =5, since there are no interest rates. One could also use a hedging argument. We could hold 6 stocks and minus one option. We choose 6 so that the portfolio has the same value in both states. The two values are 1106 - 10 and 906. These are equal if and only if 6 = 0.5. The value is then 45. By no arbitrage, this portfolio is also worth 45 today. So 50 - Option = 45, and the option price is 5. A third method of doing this problem is to use the stock and bond to replicate the option payoff, which obviously leads to the same value. Here are some possible related questions: e Price using the stock and bond to replicate the option payoff. e@ What about a put struck at 100? (There's a very easy solution!) e How will introducing interest rates affect the price? e Without computations, how will the price with the branches at 80 and 120 compare? O
problems.explanationZh: 这是一个标准的技巧问题，目的是看看您是否在答案中错误地使用了 p。这个精确的例子在[6]中有详细讨论，所以如果你声称已经阅读过它，请确保你可以做这个问题。最简单的解决方案就是使用风险中性评估。根据对称性，上涨的风险中性概率为 0.5。看涨期权的价值在上涨状态下为 10，在下跌状态下为 0，因此答案为 10 x 0.5 =5，因为没有利率。人们还可以使用一种对冲论点。我们可以持有 6 只股票并减去一只期权。我们选择 6，以便投资组合在两个州具有相同的价值。这两个值是 1106 — 10 和 906。当且仅当 6 = 0.5 时，它们才相等。那么价值是 45。在没有套利的情况下，这个投资组合今天的价值也是 45。因此 50 — 期权 = 45，期权价格为 5。解决此问题的第三种方法是使用股票和债券来复制期权收益，这显然会导致相同的值。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 使用股票和债券来复制期权收益的价格。 e@ 以 100 的价格执行看跌期权怎么样？ （有一个非常简单的解决方案！） e 引入利率将如何影响价格？ e 如果不计算，80 和 120 分支的价格如何比较？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.51 - At the end of the day, a stock will be 100 with probability p = 0
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.51 - 一天结束时，股票价格为 100 的概率为 p = 0
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: At the end of the day, a stock will be 100 with probability p = 0.6 and 50 with probability 1 - p = 0.4. What is it trading for right now? Value an at-the-money European call option expiring at the end of the day. What if the actual stock price is 75 right now?
problems.promptZh: 一天结束时，股票价格为 100 的概率为 p = 0.6，股票价格为 50 的概率为 1 — p = 0.4。现在它的交易价格是多少？评估当天结束时到期的平价欧式看涨期权。如果现在实际股价是75怎么办？
problems.explanation:
A simple answer is that it could be worth anything between those values. However, given it is a short time horizon we can proceed

as if these probabilities are both real world and risk-neutral. For the given probabilities to be risk neutral we need (neglecting interest rates as the time scale is very small) E($S_T$) = So. We then have that the stock is currently trading at So = 100 x 0.6 + 50 x 0.4 = 80. An at-the-money European call option will have a strike of 80. There is a 60% chance in the risk-neutral measure that it will pay off 20 and a 40% chance it will pay off nothing. Its value must therefore be 20 x 0.6 = 12. If the actual stock price is 75, then we can compute the risk-neutral probabilities and these will be different from the real-world ones. Here are some possible related questions: e Explain why an at-the-money put option expiring at the end of the day is worth 12 also. Do not use probabilities in the explanation. e@ What if tomorrow's stock prices could go up from today's prices by 20 with probability 0.6 and down 10 with probability 0.4? Price a call option expiring tomorrow. O
problems.explanationEn:
A simple answer is that it could be worth anything between those values. However, given it is a short time horizon we can proceed

as if these probabilities are both real world and risk-neutral. For the given probabilities to be risk neutral we need (neglecting interest rates as the time scale is very small) E($S_T$) = So. We then have that the stock is currently trading at So = 100 x 0.6 + 50 x 0.4 = 80. An at-the-money European call option will have a strike of 80. There is a 60% chance in the risk-neutral measure that it will pay off 20 and a 40% chance it will pay off nothing. Its value must therefore be 20 x 0.6 = 12. If the actual stock price is 75, then we can compute the risk-neutral probabilities and these will be different from the real-world ones. Here are some possible related questions: e Explain why an at-the-money put option expiring at the end of the day is worth 12 also. Do not use probabilities in the explanation. e@ What if tomorrow's stock prices could go up from today's prices by 20 with probability 0.6 and down 10 with probability 0.4? Price a call option expiring tomorrow. O
problems.explanationZh:
一个简单的答案是，它可能值这些值之间的任何值。然而，考虑到时间很短，我们可以继续

就好像这些概率既是现实世界的，又是风险中性的。为了使给定的概率是风险中性的，我们需要（忽略利率，因为时间尺度非常小）E(Sr) = So。然后，我们得出该股票目前的交易价格为 So = 100 x 0.6 + 50 x 0.4 = 80。平价欧式看涨期权的行使价为 80。在风险中性衡量标准中，有 60% 的机会获得 20 的回报，40% 的机会没有任何回报。因此，它的值必须是 20 x 0.6 = 12。如果实际股票价格是 75，那么我们可以计算风险中性概率，这些概率将与现实世界的概率不同。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 解释为什么当天结束时到期的平值看跌期权也值 12。不要在解释中使用概率。 e@ 如果明天的股票价格比今天的价格上涨 20（概率 0.6）和下跌 10（概率 0.4）会怎样？为明天到期的看涨期权定价。氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.52 - A stock is worth 100 today
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.52 - 今天一只股票价值 100 美元
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: A stock is worth 100 today. There are zero interest rates. The stock can be worth 90, 100, or 110 tomorrow. It moves to 110 with probability p and 100 with probability g. What can we say about the price of a call option struck at 100.
problems.promptZh: 今天一只股票价值 100 美元。利率为零。明天该股票的价值可能是 90、100 或 110。它以概率 p 移动到 110，以概率 g 移动到 100。对于执行价格为 100 的看涨期权的价格我们能说些什么呢？
problems.explanation:
This example is analyzed in detail in [6], Chapter 3. The essential point is that the model is incomplete so we can only bound the option price. The easiest approach is risk-neutral evaluation. As usual, the real-world probabilities are irrelevant provided they are non-zero. The condition that the expected stock price is today's price is that the probability of an up-move and a down-move are the same. Our three probabilities are therefore p, 1 - 2p,p. These must all be positive for the risk-neutral measure to be equivalent to the real-world measure. This means that p lies in the open range (0,0.5). The price of the call option can therefore vary between zero and 5.

We also look at how to do this via hedging as the interviewer may insist on that approach. The price will be less than that of any super-replicating portfolio, and more than that of any sub-replicating portfolio. Clearly, zero sub-replicates so the price is positive. The portfolios in this problem are just multiples of the stock and the bond; their value is a straight line as a function of the stock price. The three points we have to consider (90,0), (100, 0), and (110, 10). We can fit a straight line through any two. For the first two, we just have the zero line and get a lower bound of zero. For the first and last, we get 0.5 stocks and - 45 bonds. This will dominate the price at 100 since it has value 5 rather than zero there. The value of this super-replicating portfolio is 5 so the price is less than 5. For the final two points, the line has slope 1, so we hold 1 stock and - 100 bonds. This has value zero today so we get the same lower bound of zero. In fact, we could consider any portfolio with a holding of @ stocks and - 1000 bonds, for 0 < @ < 1 and get a lower bound of zero, which passes through the middle point. Making a formal argument that there are no sub-replicating portfolios of higher value is a little tedious, but it is clear geometrically. Here are some possible related questions: e@ Why do the risk-neutral and replication arguments give the same answer? e How would you make a price when the market is incomplete? e Suppose the price of the call option struck at 100 is 2.5, what is the price of a call option struck at 95? C]

problems.explanationZh:
这个例子在[6]第3章中有详细分析。要点是模型不完整，所以我们只能限制期权价格。最简单的方法是风险中性评估。像往常一样，只要现实世界的概率非零，它们就无关紧要。预期股价为今日价格的条件是上涨和下跌的概率相同。因此，我们的三个概率是 p, 1 — 2p,p。这些都必须是积极的，风险中性指标才能与现实世界的指标相当。这意味着 p 位于开范围 (0,0.5) 内。因此，看涨期权的价格可以在 0 到 5 之间变化。

2.3.解决方案我们还研究如何通过对冲来做到这一点，因为面试官可能会坚持这种方法。价格将低于任何超级复制投资组合的价格，并且高于任何子复制投资组合的价格。显然，零子重复，因此价格为正。这个问题中的投资组合只是股票和债券的倍数；它们的价值是股价函数的一条直线。我们必须考虑 (90,0)、(100, 0) 和 (110, 10) 三个点。我们可以通过任意两点拟合一条直线。对于前两个，我们只有零线并得到零下限。对于第一个和最后一个，我们得到 0.5 只股票和 45 只债券。这将主导 100 的价格，因为它的值是 5 而不是 0。这个超级复制投资组合的价值是 5，因此价格小于 5。对于最后两个点，该线的斜率为 1，因此我们持有 1 只股票和 —100 只债券。今天它的值为零，因此我们得到相同的零下限。事实上，我们可以考虑持有 @ 股票和 —1000 只债券的任何投资组合，对于 0 < @ < 1，并得到下限为零，它穿过中间点。正式论证不存在更高价值的子复制投资组合有点乏味，但从几何角度来看是很清楚的。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e@ 为什么风险中性和复制论点给出相同的答案？ e 当市场不完全时，你会如何定价？ e 假设以 100 的价格执行的看涨期权的价格为 2.5，那么以 95 的价格执行的看涨期权的价格是多少？ C]

problems.titleEn: Question 2.53 - Follow-up: given that we have seen that trinomial trees do not lead to unique prices, why do banks use them to compute prices
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.53 - 追问：鉴于我们已经看到三项树不会导致唯一的价格，为什么银行用它们来计算价格
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Follow-up: given that we have seen that trinomial trees do not lead to unique prices, why do banks use them to compute prices?
problems.promptZh: 追问：鉴于我们已经看到三项树不会导致唯一的价格，为什么银行用它们来计算价格？
problems.explanation:
The crucial point here is that when using a model as a computational device, we are not doing no arbitrage arguments. Instead, we are approximating a given risk-neutral measure. In this case, the risk-neutral measure we are approximating is geometric Brownian motion with a growth rate of r.

We can think of four operations: e Passing to the risk-neutral measure. e Discretization with a trinomial tree. e Pricing. e Passing to the limit. The crucial point here is that the first two of these do not commute. One reason whya trinomial tree is a useful computational device is that it does not naturally oscillate in the same was as a binomial tree. That is if the number of time steps in the tree changes by one, the price will not change as much as in a binomial tree. Trinomial trees also have much flexibility in terms of node placement since one can play with 3 node locations and two probabilities, whereas with a binomial tree there are two node locations and one probability. We have to match two quantities (at least asymptotically): mean and variance. This means that one can adapt node placement to the product, so, for example, nodes lie on barriers. Here are some possible related questions: @ What properties must a trinomial tree have to converge to the Black-Scholes price? e Is it necessary for the discretized tree to be risk-neutral? e Are trinomial trees better than binomial ones? O

The crucial point here is that the first two of these do not commute. One reason whya trinomial tree is a useful computational device is that it does not naturally oscillate in the same was as a binomial tree. O
problems.explanationZh:
这里的关键点是，当使用模型作为计算设备时，我们并不是不进行套利论证。相反，我们正在近似给定的风险中性衡量标准。在这种情况下，我们近似的风险中性度量是增长率为 r 的几何布朗运动。

我们可以想到四种操作： e 转向风险中性措施。 e 使用三叉树进行离散化。 e 定价。 e 传递到极限。这里的关键点是前两个不能交换。三项树是一种有用的计算设备的一个原因是它不像二项树那样自然振荡。也就是说，如果树中的时间步数改变 1，价格的变化不会像二项式树中那样大。三项树在节点放置方面也具有很大的灵活性，因为可以使用 3 个节点位置和两个概率，而二项树则有两个节点位置和一个概率。我们必须匹配两个量（至少是渐近地）：均值和方差。这意味着可以根据产品调整节点放置，例如，节点位于障碍物上。以下是一些可能的相关问题： @ 三项树必须具有哪些属性才能收敛到 Black-Scholes 价格？ e 离散树是否必须是风险中性的？ e 三项式树比二项式树更好吗？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.54 - Consider the following binomial tree
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.54 - 考虑以下二项式树。有两种相同的标的资产 A 和 B，具有相同的价格和波动性
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Consider the following binomial tree. There are two identical underlying assets A and B with the same prices and volatility. If all were the same except that research suggests company A will do better than company B, how would the option prices compare?
problems.promptZh: 考虑以下二项式树。有两种相同的标的资产 A 和 B，具有相同的价格和波动性。如果除了研究表明公司 A 会比公司 B 做得更好之外，一切都一样，那么期权价格会如何比较？
problems.explanation:
If two assets have the same price and volatility then their option prices will be the same. This will be true whether we are looking at a binomial tree or we are in the Black-Scholes model. When pricing an option using a binomial tree we are using risk-neutral probabilities and these do not depend on research that says A will perform better than B. This question is analogous to why the drift of the stock price does not affect the option price. See Questions 2.1 and 2.27.

Here is a related question: e In what sort of market could the prices of the two options differ? Why? O
problems.explanationEn:

When pricing an option using a binomial tree we are using risk-neutral probabilities and these do not depend on research that says A will perform better than B. This question is analogous to why the drift of the stock price does not affect the option price. See Questions 2.1 and 2.27.

Here is a related question: e In what sort of market could the prices of the two options differ? Why? O
problems.explanationZh:
如果两种资产具有相同的价格和波动性，那么它们的期权价格将相同。无论我们是在查看二项式树还是在 Black-Scholes 模型中，这都是正确的。当使用二项式树为期权定价时，我们使用的是风险中性概率，并且这些概率并不依赖于表明 A 会比 B 表现更好的研究。这个问题类似于为什么股票价格的漂移不会影响期权价格。参见问题 2.1 和 2.27。

2.3.解决方案 这是一个相关问题： e 在什么样的市场中，两种期权的价格可能不同？为什么？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.55 - Monte Carlo versus binomial tree - when shall you use one or the other
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.55 - 蒙特卡洛与二项式树——什么时候应该使用其中之一
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Monte Carlo versus binomial tree - when shall you use one or the other?
problems.promptZh: 蒙特卡洛与二项式树——什么时候应该使用其中之一？
problems.explanation: The great virtue of a binomial tree is that it is easy to do early exercise. Its rate of convergence for continuous payoffs including American options is 1/n, where n is the number of steps. Since the number of computations increases as n?, this means that the rate of convergence is t1/2 where t is computational time. (Your interviewer may not know this...) For higher dimensional trees, convergence will be slower. Path-dependence is not natural in trees but can be dealt with by using an auxiliary variable which increases the dimension by 1. The great virtues of Monte Carlo are: e Convergence is order t\sim!/? in all dimensions. e Path-dependence is easy. The great downside is that early exercise is hard (see Question 2.57). The other downside is slowness of convergence in low dimensions, although it is no slower than a binomial tree, there are other faster lattice methods in low dimensions. The convergence speed and early exercise can be coped with, but it requires much work. As a rule of thumb, use binomial! trees for low-dimensional problems involving early exercise, and Monte Carlo for high-dimensional problems involving path-dependence. Here are some possible related questions: e How would you price a path-dependent option with early exercise features on a tree? For example, an American Asian option. e How would you price a path-dependent option with early exercise features by Monte Carlo? For example, an American Asian option. e What's the fastest way to do one-dimensional numerical integrations? e How would you speed up a Monte Carlo simulation? O

problems.explanationZh: 二项树的一大优点就是很容易做早期的练习。包括美式期权在内的连续收益的收敛率为 1/n，其中 n 是步数。由于计算次数随着 n? 的增加而增加，这意味着收敛速度为 t1/2，其中 t 是计算时间。 （你的面试官可能不知道这一点......）对于更高维的树，收敛会更慢。路径依赖在树中并不自然，但可以通过使用将维度增加 1 的辅助变量来处理。蒙特卡洛的伟大优点是： e 收敛的阶数是 t!/?在所有维度。路径依赖很容易。最大的缺点是早期锻炼很困难（见问题 2.57）。另一个缺点是低维收敛速度慢，虽然它不比二项式树慢，但低维还有其他更快的格子方法。收敛速度和早期锻炼都可以应付，但需要付出很大的努力。根据经验，使用二项式！树用于涉及早期练习的低维问题，蒙特卡罗用于涉及路径依赖的高维问题。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 您如何为树上具有早期行使特征的路径依赖期权定价？例如，美式亚洲期权。 e 您如何为具有蒙特卡罗早期行使功能的路径依赖期权定价？例如，美式亚洲期权。 e 进行一维数值积分的最快方法是什么？ e 如何加速蒙特卡罗模拟？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 2.56 - Current stock price 100, may go up to 150 or go down to 75
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.56 - 当前股价100，可能会上涨到150或下跌到75
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Current stock price 100, may go up to 150 or go down to 75. What is the price of a call option based on it? What is the Delta?
problems.promptZh: 当前股价为 100，下一期可能上涨到 150，也可能下跌到 75。基于该股票的看涨期权价格是多少？Delta 是多少？
problems.answer: Assuming strike 100 and zero interest, the call price is 50/3 ≈ 16.67 and Delta is 2/3.
problems.answerEn: Assuming strike 100 and zero interest, the call price is 50/3 ≈ 16.67 and Delta is 2/3.
problems.answerZh: 若假设执行价为 100 且利率为 0，则看涨期权价格为 50/3 ≈ 16.67，Delta 为 2/3。
problems.explanation:
The problem omits strike and interest rate, so assume strike $K=100$ and zero interest. The call payoff is $50$ in the up state and $0$ in the down state.

The risk-neutral probability $p$ solves
$$100=150p+75(1-p),$$
so
$$p=\frac13.$$
Therefore the option price is
$$C=\frac13\cdot50+\frac23\cdot0=\frac{50}{3}\approx16.67.$$
The delta is the change in option value divided by the change in stock value:
$$\Delta=\frac{50-0}{150-75}=\frac23.$$
problems.explanationEn:
The problem omits strike and interest rate, so assume strike $K=100$ and zero interest. The call payoff is $50$ in the up state and $0$ in the down state.

The risk-neutral probability $p$ solves
$$100=150p+75(1-p),$$
so
$$p=\frac13.$$
Therefore the option price is
$$C=\frac13\cdot50+\frac23\cdot0=\frac{50}{3}\approx16.67.$$
The delta is the change in option value divided by the change in stock value:
$$\Delta=\frac{50-0}{150-75}=\frac23.$$
problems.explanationZh:
题目未给执行价和利率，因此假设执行价 $K=100$ 且利率为 0。看涨期权在上涨状态收益为 $50$，在下跌状态收益为 $0$。

风险中性概率 $p$ 满足
$$100=150p+75(1-p),$$
所以
$$p=\frac13.$$
因此期权价格为
$$C=\frac13\cdot50+\frac23\cdot0=\frac{50}{3}\approx16.67.$$
Delta 等于期权价值变化除以股票价值变化：
$$\Delta=\frac{50-0}{150-75}=\frac23.$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Binomial option price and delta computations were garbled; strike was mistranslated.

problems.titleEn: Question 2.57 - Explain the Longstaff-Schwartz algorithm for pricing an early exercisable option with Monte Carlo
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.57 - 解释使用蒙特卡罗为早期可行使期权定价的 Longstaff-Schwartz 算法
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Explain the Longstaff-Schwartz algorithm for pricing an early exercisable option with Monte Carlo.
problems.promptZh: 解释使用蒙特卡罗为早期可行使期权定价的 Longstaff-Schwartz 算法。
problems.explanation:
As we saw in Question 2.55 it is difficult to use Monte Carlo for products with early exercise features, such as American options. The Longstaff - Schwartz method is one way to handle early exercise using Monte Carlo. When pricing an early exercisable product, we need to know at a given time whether it will be worth more by exercising now or by holding onto it. The value of holding onto the option, known as the continuation value, is what the Longstaff - Schwartz method estimates via regression. The Longstaff—-Schwartz method is commonly used for such products as Bermudan swaptions.

Before we jump into the algorithm, a few details are necessary. A regression needs to be carried out against some basis functions, for example we might regress y against some explanatory variables x and x”. Choosing good basis functions that will estimate the continuation value accurately is very much an art. The Longstaff - Schwartz algorithm is: (1) Run a certain number of Monte Carlo paths (e.g. 1000) and store the exercise value divided by the numeraire at each possible exercise date for each path. We also need to store the value of the basis functions at the same points. (2) With all this data we now look at the second last exercise date. We perform a regression (usually least-squares) of the value at the last exercise date against the basis functions. This gives coefficients, a;, for the basis functions. (3) Using these coefficients we can calculate the continuation value at the second last exercise time for each path, using for example y = ayx + agx?. (4) We now replace the deflated exercise value stored initially with the continuation value, if the continuation value is greater. (5) Continue back through the exercise dates until reaching time 0. The (biased) value of the product will be the average of the time zero values multiplied by the initial numeraire value. Here are some possible related questions: e Why will the above algorithm produce a biased price? Will the bias be large? Suggest a way to remove the bias. e What other methods do you know of to fit early exercise into Monte Carlo. e@ What are some useful basis functions when pricing a Bermudan swaption? O

Suggest a way to remove the bias. e What other methods do you know of to fit early exercise into Monte Carlo. e@ What are some useful basis functions when pricing a Bermudan swaption? O
problems.explanationZh:
正如我们在问题 2.55 中看到的，对于具有早期行使功能的产品（例如美式期权），很难使用蒙特卡罗。 Longstaff-Schwartz 方法是使用蒙特卡罗处理早期练习的一种方法。在为早期可行权产品定价时，我们需要知道在给定时间，现在行权还是持有它是否会更值钱。持有期权的价值，称为连续价值，是 Longstaff-Schwartz 方法通过回归估计的。朗斯塔夫——施瓦茨方法通常用于百慕大掉期期权等产品。

2.3.解决方案 在我们开始讨论算法之前，有必要了解一些细节。需要针对某些基函数进行回归，例如我们可能针对一些解释变量 x 和 x 进行回归。选择能够准确估计连续值的良好基函数是一门艺术。Longstaff-Schwartz 算法是：（1）运行一定数量的蒙特卡洛路径（例如 1000），并存储每条路径的每个可能行权日期的行权值除以计价器。我们还需要将基函数的值存储在(2) 使用所有这些数据，我们现在查看最后一个锻炼日期的值与基函数的回归（通常是最小二乘法），这给出了基函数的系数 a; (3) 使用这些系数，我们可以计算每个路径的倒数第二个锻炼时间的连续值，例如使用 y = ayx + agx?。 (5) 继续返回执行日期，直到达到时间 0。产品的（偏差）值将是时间零值乘以初始计量值的平均值。 e 为什么上述算法会产生偏差价格？ e 请提出一种消除偏差的方法。 e@ 定价时有哪些有用的基函数。百慕大互换 O？

problems.titleEn: Question 2.58 - What is implied volatility and a volatility skew/smile
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.58 - 什么是隐含波动率以及波动率偏斜/微笑
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What is implied volatility and a volatility skew/smile?
problems.promptZh: 什么是隐含波动率以及波动率偏斜/微笑？
problems.explanation:
Implied volatility is simply the volatility implied from the market price of an option, using some model. It is usually calculated by taking the option's price and finding the volatility in the Black-Scholes formula that returns the same price. For example, consider an at-the-money European call option with 1 year to expiry, spot value 100 and a risk-free rate of 5%. If the market price of this option is \$6.80, then the implied volatility will be 15%.

If one calculates the implied volatility of options with the same expiry date but different strike prices and plots the volatilities, there is often a smile or skew shape. A volatility smile will look something like Figure 2.8.

There are many possible explanations for why some markets exhibit volatility smiles. One possible explanation is that the market is more likely to move up or down by a large amount than is assumed within the Black-Scholes model. Hence the smile reflects the market's view of the imperfections in the Black-Scholes model.

A volatility skew is similar to a smile, but it is only downward sloping, compared to the more symmetric smile. A volatility skew could look something like Figure 2.9.

For a detailed discussion of volatility smiles and skews see Chapter 18 of [6].

Here are some possible related questions:
- Which shape will the implied volatility take for equity markets, skew or smile? Explain.
- When did the volatility smile first appear in the equity market and why?
- If the market was pricing options incorrectly and the smile was not a persistent feature, describe an arbitrage opportunity.

- When did the volatility smile first appear in the equity market and why?
- If the market was pricing options incorrectly and the smile was not a persistent feature, describe an arbitrage opportunity.
problems.explanationZh:
隐含波动率就是在某个定价模型下，由期权市场价格反推出的波动率。通常的做法是把期权价格代入 Black-Scholes 公式，寻找能返回同一价格的波动率。例如，考虑一个平值欧式看涨期权，到期时间 1 年，现货价格 100，无风险利率为 5%。如果该期权的市场价格是 \$6.80，那么隐含波动率就是 15%。

如果对相同到期日、不同执行价的期权分别计算隐含波动率并画图，曲线通常会呈现微笑或偏斜形状。波动率微笑大致如图 2.8 所示。

市场出现波动率微笑有很多可能解释。其中一种解释是，市场认为标的价格大幅上涨或下跌的概率高于 Black-Scholes 模型的假设。因此，微笑反映了市场对 Black-Scholes 模型缺陷的看法。

波动率偏斜与微笑类似，但它主要是向下倾斜，而不是相对对称的微笑。波动率偏斜大致如图 2.9 所示。

关于波动率微笑和偏斜的详细讨论可参见 [6] 第 18 章。

相关问题包括：
- 股票市场中的隐含波动率通常呈偏斜还是微笑？请解释。
- 股票市场中的波动率微笑最早何时出现，为什么？
- 如果市场对期权定价有误，且微笑不是持久特征，请描述一个套利机会。

problems.titleEn: Question 2.59 - What differing models can be used to price exotic foreign exchange options consistently with market smiles
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.59 - 有哪些不同模型可以用来给外汇奇异期权定价，并且与市场微笑保持一致
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What differing models can be used to price exotic foreign exchange options consistently with market smiles? What are the pros and cons of each?
problems.promptZh: 有哪些不同模型可以用来给外汇奇异期权定价，并且与市场微笑保持一致？各自的优缺点是什么？
problems.explanation:
There are many different option pricing models that capture some of the features of market smiles. The large number of models makes it too difficult to cover them all in detail, so we focus our answer on four popular models: jump diffusion, stochastic volatility, Variance Gamma and local volatility. For details of the first three models we refer the reader to [6]; see Chapter 22 of [20] for local volatility.

Jump diffusion models have the foreign exchange rate moving as geometric Brownian motion with an added random jump component. The jump component is usually a Poisson process. If $S_t$ is the exchange rate, we could have
\[
\frac{dS_t}{S_t}=\mu dt+\sigma dW_t+(J-1)dN_t,
\]
where $J$ is a random jump size (J could be log-normally distributed, for example) and $N_t$ is the Poisson process. Jump diffusion models produce what is known as a floating smile. This means that the smile moves when spot moves, so that the bottom of the smile will remain at-the-money. As floating smiles are a feature of foreign exchange markets this is an advantage of jump diffusion models.

One disadvantage of a jump-diffusion model is that the smiles produced flatten with maturity quickly; this is not observed in most markets. See Figure 2.10 for an example in which the smile has largely disappeared after 2 years.

Stochastic volatility models take both the foreign exchange rate and volatility process to be stochastic. There are many popular forms for the volatility process; the Heston model, for example, uses
\[
\frac{dS_t}{S_t}=\mu dt+\sqrt{V_t}\,dW_t^{(1)}, \qquad
dV_t=\kappa(\theta-V_t)dt+\sigma_V\sqrt{V_t}\,dW_t^{(2)},
\]
where $\theta$ is the long term average volatility, $\kappa$ is the rate at which the process reverts to its mean, $\sigma_V$ is the volatility of volatility, and $W_t^{(1)}$ and $W_t^{(2)}$ are Brownian motions with correlation $\rho$.

Stochastic volatility models also produce a floating smile and their smile shape can be easily changed by tweaking the parameters. For example, skew can be introduced by having $\rho$ non-zero, the flattening out of the smile can be adjusted by the mean reversion parameter $\kappa$, etc. Figure 2.11 shows an example smile from the Heston model.

Having the flexibility of changing the smile shape has its disadvantages in that all these parameters need to be fitted in a stable and consistent way with the market, which is not a straightforward task.

Variance Gamma models take the foreign exchange rate and time as a random process. The idea is that foreign exchange rates move when market information arrives and this information arrival is itself a random process. For example, if a large amount of information is arriving then we can expect high volatility, however on a day with minimal information arrival we can expect low volatility. To define a Variance Gamma model we let $b(t;\theta,\sigma)$ be a Brownian motion, i.e.
\[
db_t=\theta dt+\sigma dW_t,
\]
and we let
\[
X(t;\sigma,\nu,\theta)=b(T_t;\theta,\sigma),
\]
where $T_t$ is the time $t$ value of a Gamma process with variance rate $\nu$.

Variance Gamma smiles are similar to jump diffusion smiles, compare Figure 2.10 and Figure 2.12. The smiles tend to flatten out over time as the model becomes more similar to a pure diffusive model.

Local volatility models derive the volatility surface from quoted market prices and then use this surface to price more exotic instruments. It turns out that under risk-neutrality there is a unique diffusion process consistent with market prices. The unique volatility function $\sigma(S_t,t)$ is known as the local volatility function.

Once this local volatility surface has been found, exotic instruments can be priced consistently with market prices. The problem however, is that the volatility surface is the market's current view of volatility and this will change in the future, meaning the exotic options will no longer be consistent with market prices.

Here are some possible related questions:
- Which of the above models are incomplete and why?
- Discuss the features of equity and interest rate smiles as compared to foreign exchange smiles.
- Given the four models above, you have to use one. What criteria would you use to pick a model?
problems.explanationEn:
There are many different option pricing models that capture some of the features of market smiles. The large number of models makes it too difficult to cover them all in detail, so we focus our answer on four popular models: jump diffusion, stochastic volatility, Variance Gamma and local volatility. For details of the first three models we refer the reader to [6]; see Chapter 22 of [20] for local volatility.

Jump diffusion models have the foreign exchange rate moving as geometric Brownian motion with an added random jump component. The jump component is usually a Poisson process. If $S_t$ is the exchange rate, we could have
\[

\]
where $J$ is a random jump size (J could be log-normally distributed, for example) and $N_t$ is the Poisson process. Jump diffusion models produce what is known as a floating smile. This means that the smile moves when spot moves, so that the bottom of the smile will remain at-the-money. The idea is that foreign exchange rates move when market information arrives and this information arrival is itself a random process. For example, if a large amount of information is arriving then we can expect high volatility, however on a day with minimal information arrival we can expect low volatility. To define a Variance Gamma model we let $b(t;\theta,\sigma)$ be a Brownian motion, i.e.
\[
db_t=\theta dt+\sigma dW_t,
\]
and we let
\[
X(t;\sigma,\nu,\theta)=b(T_t;\theta,\sigma),
\]

Here are some possible related questions:
- Which of the above models are incomplete and why?
- Discuss the features of equity and interest rate smiles as compared to foreign exchange smiles.
- Given the four models above, you have to use one. What criteria would you use to pick a model?
problems.explanationZh:
很多期权定价模型都能捕捉市场微笑的一部分特征。模型数量很多，不可能逐一详细介绍，因此这里集中讨论四类常用模型：跳扩散、随机波动率、Variance Gamma 和局部波动率。前三类模型的细节可参见 [6]；局部波动率可参见 [20] 第 22 章。

跳扩散模型令外汇汇率按几何布朗运动演化，同时加入随机跳跃成分。跳跃成分通常是泊松过程。如果 $S_t$ 是汇率，可以写成
\[
\frac{dS_t}{S_t}=\mu dt+\sigma dW_t+(J-1)dN_t,
\]
其中 $J$ 是随机跳跃幅度（例如可以服从对数正态分布），$N_t$ 是泊松过程。跳扩散模型会产生所谓的浮动微笑：现货移动时，微笑也随之移动，使微笑底部保持在平值附近。外汇市场具有浮动微笑特征，因此这是跳扩散模型的一个优点。

跳扩散模型的一个缺点是，生成的微笑会随着期限快速变平，这在多数市场中并不常见。图 2.10 展示了一个例子，其中 2 年后微笑几乎已经消失。

随机波动率模型把外汇汇率和波动率过程都设为随机。波动率过程有很多常见形式，例如 Heston 模型使用
\[
\frac{dS_t}{S_t}=\mu dt+\sqrt{V_t}\,dW_t^{(1)}, \qquad
dV_t=\kappa(\theta-V_t)dt+\sigma_V\sqrt{V_t}\,dW_t^{(2)},
\]
其中 $\theta$ 是长期平均波动率，$\kappa$ 是均值回复速度，$\sigma_V$ 是波动率的波动率，$W_t^{(1)}$ 和 $W_t^{(2)}$ 是相关系数为 $\rho$ 的布朗运动。

随机波动率模型也会产生浮动微笑，并且可以通过调整参数方便地改变微笑形状。例如，令 $\rho$ 非零可以引入偏斜；均值回复参数 $\kappa$ 可以调节微笑变平的速度。图 2.11 展示了 Heston 模型产生的一个微笑示例。

这种灵活性也带来缺点：所有参数都必须以稳定且与市场一致的方式拟合，而这并不简单。

Variance Gamma 模型把外汇汇率和时间都视为随机过程。直觉是，外汇汇率在市场信息到达时发生变化，而信息到达本身也是随机过程。例如，信息大量到达时波动率较高；信息很少时波动率较低。定义 Variance Gamma 模型时，令 $b(t;\theta,\sigma)$ 是带漂移和波动率的布朗运动，即
\[
db_t=\theta dt+\sigma dW_t,
\]
并令
\[
X(t;\sigma,\nu,\theta)=b(T_t;\theta,\sigma),
\]
其中 $T_t$ 是方差率为 $\nu$ 的 Gamma 过程在时间 $t$ 的取值。

Variance Gamma 微笑与跳扩散微笑类似，可比较图 2.10 和图 2.12。随着期限增加，微笑倾向于变平，因为模型会变得更接近纯扩散模型。

局部波动率模型从市场报价推导波动率曲面，再用该曲面为更复杂的奇异产品定价。在风险中性条件下，存在唯一一个与市场价格一致的扩散过程。这个唯一的波动率函数 $\sigma(S_t,t)$ 称为局部波动率函数。

一旦得到局部波动率曲面，就可以用它为奇异产品定价，并与当前市场价格保持一致。但问题在于，波动率曲面只是市场当前对波动率的看法，未来会发生变化，因此奇异期权之后可能不再与市场价格一致。

相关问题包括：
- 上述哪些模型是不完备的？为什么？
- 比较股票和利率市场微笑与外汇微笑的特征。
- 如果必须从上述四个模型中选择一个，你会用什么标准？

problems.titleEn: Question 2.60 - Explain why a stochastic volatility model gives a smile
problems.titleZh: 问题 2.60 - 解释一下为什么随机波动率模型会让人微笑
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Explain why a stochastic volatility model gives a smile.
problems.promptZh: 解释一下为什么随机波动率模型会让人微笑。
problems.explanation: A stochastic volatility model takes volatility to be driven by a random process. It is then possible that the volatility will become large causing large movements in the underlying asset price. These large moves give the distribution of the underlying asset fatter tails than in the Black-Scholes model. This means that options away from the money will be priced more expensively than in Black-Scholes leading to a volatility smile. Another way to think about why the smile occurs is by looking at the second derivative of the option price with respect to the volatility. In the Black- Scholes world our Vega sensitivity (how good our volatility hedge is) will be zero. However, a stochastic volatility model will not necessarily have a non-zero derivative of Vega, therefore there is Vega convexity which is related to the volatility smile. Here are some possible related questions: e Explain why a jump-diffusion model and a Variance Gamma model produce smiles. e Explain why a jump-diffusion model's smile flattens out with time, whereas a stochastic volatility model's smile may not. O
problems.explanationEn: A stochastic volatility model takes volatility to be driven by a random process. It is then possible that the volatility will become large causing large movements in the underlying asset price. These large moves give the distribution of the underlying asset fatter tails than in the Black-Scholes model. This means that options away from the money will be priced more expensively than in Black-Scholes leading to a volatility smile. Another way to think about why the smile occurs is by looking at the second derivative of the option price with respect to the volatility. In the Black- Scholes world our Vega sensitivity (how good our volatility hedge is) will be zero. However, a stochastic volatility model will not necessarily have a non-zero derivative of Vega, therefore there is Vega convexity which is related to the volatility smile. Here are some possible related questions: e Explain why a jump-diffusion model and a Variance Gamma model produce smiles. e Explain why a jump-diffusion model's smile flattens out with time, whereas a stochastic volatility model's smile may not. O
problems.explanationZh:
随机波动率模型认为波动率是由随机过程驱动的。那么波动性可能会变大，导致基础资产价格大幅波动。这些大幅波动使得标的资产的分布比布莱克-斯科尔斯模型中的分布更加肥尾。这意味着远离货币的期权的定价将比布莱克-斯科尔斯期权定价更高，从而导致波动性微笑。思考为什么会出现微笑的另一种方法是查看期权价格相对于波动性的二阶导数。在布莱克-斯科尔斯世界中，我们的 Vega 敏感性（我们的波动性对冲有多好）将为零。然而，随机波动率模型不一定具有非零的 Vega 导数，因此存在与波动率微笑相关的 Vega 凸性。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 解释为什么跳跃扩散模型和方差伽玛模型会产生微笑。 e 解释为什么跳跃扩散模型的微笑会随着时间的推移而变平，而随机波动率模型的微笑可能不会。氧

第 3 章概率 3.1。简介 本章重点讨论概率和随机过程的问题。由于随机过程是现代定价理论的核心，因此与第二章不可避免地有重叠。问题范围很广，从基本的（例如，“一掷公平骰子的期望是什么？”）到相当困难的问题。面试官正在寻找两件事：概率思考的能力，以及研究随机过程的证据。我们将问题分为一般问题（其中许多涉及抛硬币）和有关随机过程的问题。对于更具挑战性的问题，我们提供了参考资料，以便读者研究所有细节。在尝试量化工作面试之前，必须学习概率论；这里有几本关于该主题的优秀书籍： Kai Lai Chung 的《初等概率论》是一个很好的起点。本书主要针对学习第一门概率学本科课程的人。本书还包括关于金融数学的一章。杰弗里·格里米特 (Geoffrey Grimmett) 和大卫·斯特扎克 (David Stirzaker) 的《概率与随机过程》比 Chung 更具挑战性。这本书介绍得很好，值得一读。另一本涵盖基本概率（并继续讨论更高级主题）的优秀书籍是 William Feller 的两卷《概率论及其应用简介》。第一卷涵盖离散分布和事件，第二卷转向连续处理。解释以清晰简洁的方式呈现。

如果您有一定的概率知识，David Williams 的《概率与鞅》是离散时间鞅的一个很好的起点。风格清晰、信息丰富，同时呈现扎实的数学思想。接下来，尝试克里斯·罗杰斯和大卫·威廉姆斯撰写的两卷集“扩散、马尔可夫过程和鞅”。在继续讨论布朗运动和连续时间鞅的基础知识之前，威廉姆斯的大部分材料都会在本书中快速重复。第二卷讨论随机微积分。 Kai Lai Chung 和 Ruth Williams 所著的《随机积分导论》是一本非常可读的随机积分理论阐述。然而，它假设您了解连续时间鞅，因此您必须首先在其他地方学习。如果您想要更多采用数学金融方法的书籍，本书第一作者 Mark Joshi 所著的《数学金融的概念与实践》是一个很好的起点。 Steven Shreve 的《金融随机微积分》第一卷和第二卷也是很好的介绍。相对于《数学金融的概念与实践》，本书更倾向于数学而不是金融。 3.2.问题3.2.1。一般的。问题 3.1。考虑下面的游戏。玩家只掷一次骰子。翻转面上每个点的回报为 1 美元。假设公平骰子，你应该将这场游戏的票价定在什么水平？问题 3.2。假设我们玩一个游戏。我最多可以掷骰子 3 次。每次我掷骰子时，您可以将显示的数字视为美元，也可以再次掷骰子。您的预期奖金是多少？问题 3.3。我们来玩个游戏吧。有四个密封的盒子。一箱有100斤，其他都是空的。玩家可以支付X来打开一个盒子并可以多次拿走里面的东西。假设这是一场公平的游戏，那么X的值是多少？

3.2.问题 问题 3.4。我们玩一个游戏：我从 1 到 100 中选择一个数字 n。如果你猜对了，我付给你 n 美元，否则付给你零。你愿意花多少钱来玩这个游戏？问题 3.5。假设你有一枚公平的硬币。你从一美元开始，如果你抛一个 H，你的头寸加倍，如果你抛一个 7'，你的头寸减半。 如果你无限地抛硬币，你拥有的钱的预期价值是多少？问题 3.6。假设我们抛一枚公平的硬币，让 N 表示直到出现正面（包括最后一次抛掷）为止的抛掷次数。什么是 E(.V) 和 Var(N)？问题 3.7。我们玩一个游戏，用一枚公平的硬币。当连续出现两个正面（H）或反面（T）时，游戏停止。预计比赛结束需要多长时间？问题 3.8。对于一枚公平的硬币，需要抛掷多少次才能连续出现三个正面？问题 3.9。你扔了一枚有偏见的硬币。头被抛起之前预计需要多长时间？连续两个头？问题 3.10。我有一个袋子，里面装着九枚普通硬币和一枚双头硬币。我取出一枚硬币并翻转三遍。它每次都会出现。出现双标的概率是多少？问题 3.11。我从口袋里掏出一枚看起来很普通的硬币，翻转了三下。每次都是一个头。你认为下一次翻转也是正面的概率是多少？如果我抛硬币 100 次并且每次都是正面怎么办？问题 3.12.你扔一枚公平的硬币一百万次。 6 个头和 6 个尾的字符串的预期数量是多少？问题 3.13.假设您正在向圆板投掷飞镖。您预计距中心的距离是多少？做出任何必要的假设。假设如果您在 R/2 半径内连续击中 10 次，您将赢得一美元，其中 FR 是棋盘的半径。每次尝试都要支付10c。如果你尝试 100 次，你预计会损失/赚多少钱？如果您是职业选手并且您击中 R/2 内侧的概率是击中 R/2 外侧的概率的两倍，您的答案会改变吗？

问题 3.14.一个女人有两个孩子。其中一个是女孩，另一个是男孩的概率是多少？问题 3.15.一个家庭有两个孩子，您知道其中至少有一个是女孩。两个都是女孩的概率是多少？如果你知道老大是个女孩怎么办？如果你知道他们有一个叫克拉丽莎的女孩怎么办？问题 3.16.该月的第四个工作日是星期四的概率是多少？问题 3.17.您正在玩俄罗斯轮盘赌。六发左轮手枪的相邻弹膛中恰好有两颗子弹。桶旋转。扣动扳机，枪不开火。下一个是你，你是再次旋转还是扣动扳机？问题 3.18。考虑一副 52 张牌，顺序为 A > K > Q >...> 2。我先选一张，然后你选一张，我的牌比你的大的概率是多少？问题 3.19.假设有 2 支球队参加锦标赛。一旦一支球队输了，就出局。还假设你事先知道球队的排名，并且你知道排名较高的球队总是会赢得排名较低的球队的比赛。一开始，所有球队都被随机分配互相比赛。在决赛中，最好的球队打第二好的球队的概率是多少？问题 3.20。一个抽屉里有 2 只红袜子和 2 只黑袜子。如果你拿出 2 只袜子问题 3.21. 如果我从一张普通牌中抽出两张牌，它们都是 A 的概率是多少？ 问题 3.22. 假设我们有一只蚂蚁从一个顶点移动到另一个顶点，它沿着每个顶点随机选择一个边缘。我们选择立方体的一个顶点并将蚂蚁放在那里，蚂蚁需要多少分钟才能返回到同一顶点？ QugEsTION 3.23。

3.2.你提出的问题有一米长，但方向是随机的——要么朝向一端等待的鲨鱼，要么朝向另一端的最终自由。如果 x（整数）是您从安全端开始的距离（以米为单位），则将您的生存概率确定为 z 的函数。问题 3.24，罗密欧和朱丽叶同意在晚上 9 点到 10 点之间的某个时间见面。他们每个人都在这个时间间隔内随机选择一个时间出现并等待对方十五分钟。他们真正见面的概率有多大？问题 3.25。一架飞机有一百个座位，正好有一百人登机。他们在登机口排队的顺序和飞机上座位的顺序一模一样，从1到100。不过，第一个人是奶奶，她视力不太好，所以她没有坐飞机上的第一个座位，而是随机选了一个座位坐下。现在的规则是：当任何其他人进入飞机时，他或她将尝试坐在自己的座位上。如果不可能，此人会随机选择一个空闲座位。最终，大家都登上了飞机。最后一个人（编号 100）坐在指定座位号上的概率是多少？问题 3.26.假设三个资产A、B、C'，A和B的相关系数为0.9，B和C的相关系数为0.8。 A和C的相关系数有可能为0.1吗？问题 3.27.设x,y均匀分布在[0,1]上，将单位区间[0, 1]分成3段，这3段直线可以构造成三角形的概率是多少？问题 3.28.均匀 U(a, bd) 随机变量的分布函数是什么？问题 3.29.假设 £1, %9,...,2%p 独立同分布于 [0,1] 且在区间上均匀。最大值的期望值是多少？最大值和最小值之差的预期值是多少？问题 3.30.给出一个具有无限方差的分布的例子。问题 3.31.假设随机变量 X 具有概率密度函数 fx (x)。 g(X) 有什么密度函数？

problems.titleEn: Question 3.1 - Consider the following game
problems.titleZh: 问题 3.1 - 考虑下面的游戏。玩家只掷一次骰子

problems.promptEn: Consider the following game. The player tosses a die once only. The payoff is 1 dollar for each dot on the upturned face. Assuming a fair die, at what level should you set the ticket price of this game?
problems.promptZh: 考虑下面的游戏。玩家只掷一次骰子。翻转面上每个点的回报为 1 美元。假设公平骰子，你应该将这场游戏的票价定在什么水平？
problems.answer: The fair ticket price is 3.5 dollars; to make a profit, charge more than 3.5 dollars.
problems.answerEn: The fair ticket price is 3.5 dollars; to make a profit, charge more than 3.5 dollars.
problems.answerZh: 公平票价为 3.5 美元；若想盈利，应收取高于 3.5 美元的价格。
problems.explanation:
For a fair six-sided die, the expected payoff is
$$E=\frac{1+2+3+4+5+6}{6}=3.5.$$
Thus the actuarially fair ticket price is 3.5 dollars. A casino or seller would charge more than this to earn a positive expected profit.
problems.explanationEn:
For a fair six-sided die, the expected payoff is
$$E=\frac{1+2+3+4+5+6}{6}=3.5.$$
Thus the actuarially fair ticket price is 3.5 dollars. A casino or seller would charge more than this to earn a positive expected profit.
problems.explanationZh:
对公平六面骰，期望收益为
$$E=\frac{1+2+3+4+5+6}{6}=3.5.$$
因此公平票价为 3.5 美元。赌场或卖方若要获得正期望利润，需要收取高于这个价格的票价。

problems.promptEn: Suppose we play a game. I roll a die up to three times. Each time I roll, you can either take the number showing as dollars, or roll again. What is your expected winnings?
problems.promptZh: 假设我们玩一个游戏。我最多可以掷骰子 3 次。每次我掷骰子时，您可以将显示的数字视为美元，也可以再次掷骰子。您的预期奖金是多少？
problems.explanation:
This problem should be approached from the end. If instead I only offered you one roll of the dice, what are your expected winnings? Clearly the answer here is o1 K(1 die) = I\sim = 3.5. (1 die) » Ps i=1 Now if I offer you two dice, you can either take the result of the first roll, or proceed onto the second die. The optimal strategy is to take the first roll if it is better than your expected winnings from the second roll, or continue otherwise. Thus we take the first roll if it is 4 or greater, otherwise we continue, giving E(2 dice) = E(max(\{first roll, expected winnings from 1 die\}) = >x6+—x54+—=x4+—=x35= 4.25. rr Sor cre Repeating the same idea for the third die gives E(8 dice) = E(max\{first roll, expected winnings from 2 dice\}) =5xX64+5x94+-x*425=4-. + 6 + 3 Note that this is essentially the problem of pricing an option on a multinomial tree, and this is why the problem is very popular with interviewers.

Here are some possible related questions: e How many rolls would I need to offer you before you will only accept a 6 on the first roll? e What sort of option does this problem relate to? e How does your answer change if the payment is the square of the number showing? e What effect will it have if I charge you \$1 for each additional roll? O

The optimal strategy is to take the first roll if it is better than your expected winnings from the second roll, or continue otherwise. Thus we take the first roll if it is 4 or greater, otherwise we continue, giving E(2 dice) = E(max(\{first roll, expected winnings from 1 die\}) = >x6+—x54+—=x4+—=x35= 4.25. rr Sor cre Repeating the same idea for the third die gives E(8 dice) = E(max\{first roll, expected winnings from 2 dice\}) =5xX64+5x94+-x*425=4-. + 6 + 3 Note that this is essentially the problem of pricing an option on a multinomial tree, and this is why the problem is very popular with interviewers.

problems.explanationZh:
这个问题应该从最后开始解决。如果我只给你掷一骰子，你的预期奖金是多少？显然，这里的答案是 o1 K(1 die) = I = 3.5。 (1 die) » Ps i=1 现在，如果我给你两个骰子，你可以选择第一个骰子的结果，或者继续第二个骰子。最佳策略是，如果第一次掷骰比您第二次掷骰的预期奖金要好，则进行第一次掷骰，否则继续。因此，如果它是 4 或更大，我们就进行第一次掷骰，否则我们继续，给出 E(2 个骰子) = E(max(\{第一次掷骰，1 个骰子的预期奖金\}) = >x6+—x54+—=x4+—=x35= 4.25。rr Sor cre 对第三个骰子重复相同的想法，得出 E(8 个骰子) = E(max\{第一次掷骰，2 个骰子的预期奖金\}) =5xX64+5x94+-x*425=4- 请注意，这本质上是多项式树上的期权定价问题，这就是为什么这个问题很受面试官欢迎。

problems.titleEn: Question 3.3 - Let's play a game
problems.titleZh: 问题 3.3 - 我们来玩个游戏吧。有四个密封的盒子
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers

problems.promptEn: Let's play a game. There are four sealed boxes. There is 100 pounds in one box and the others are empty. A player can pay X to open a box and take the contents as many times as they like. Assuming this is a fair game, what is the value of X?
problems.promptZh:
我们来玩个游戏吧。有四个密封的盒子。一箱有100斤，其他都是空的。玩家可以支付X来打开一个盒子并可以多次拿走里面的东西。假设这是一场公平的游戏，那么X的值是多少？

3.2.问题
problems.explanation:
Since this is a fair game, we must have that by playing optimally the player has an expected cost equal to their expected winnings. What is the optimal strategy? Say we set a price Y to play the game. The price is 'fair' in the sense that the player will not, on average, incur a loss by playing at least once. If they do not win on their first pick, then clearly it is optimal to continue playing. If it was worth playing when the odds of picking the correct box were 1/4, then if you can play for the same price with the increased odds of 1/3 it is worthwhile. So we have that the optimal strategy is to continue playing until you pick the correct box. This means every time we play this strategy, we will win the 100 pounds. Thus we need to find a price X such that the expected cost of playing until you win is 100. Note E(cost) = XP(win on first attempt) + 2XP(win on second attempt) + 3XP(win on third attempt) + 4XP(win on fourth attempt). We are dealing with conditional probabilities, that is the probability of winning on your second attempt is the probability of picking the correct box the second time after not picking it the first time. Thus, for example, P(win on second attempt) = 3 x 3 This gives the equation for X 100 = 2.5X, and solving we see X = 40.

As a quick 'sanity check', why must the price be at least 25 (think about it before you read on)? If the price was less than 25, playing just once would be a game favourable to the player. While this might seem trivial, such checks can be quite handy as a quick way to detect silly mistakes. Here are some possible related questions: e Instead of maintaining a constant price, if you choose to continue playing after two incorrect guesses the price changes to 7. What is the fair value of X and Z? e Generalise the above problem to the case of n boxes. O
problems.explanationEn:
Since this is a fair game, we must have that by playing optimally the player has an expected cost equal to their expected winnings. What is the optimal strategy? Say we set a price Y to play the game. The price is 'fair' in the sense that the player will not, on average, incur a loss by playing at least once. If they do not win on their first pick, then clearly it is optimal to continue playing. If it was worth playing when the odds of picking the correct box were 1/4, then if you can play for the same price with the increased odds of 1/3 it is worthwhile. So we have that the optimal strategy is to continue playing until you pick the correct box. This means every time we play this strategy, we will win the 100 pounds. Thus we need to find a price X such that the expected cost of playing until you win is 100. Note E(cost) = XP(win on first attempt) + 2XP(win on second attempt) + 3XP(win on third attempt) + 4XP(win on fourth attempt). We are dealing with conditional probabilities, that is the probability of winning on your second attempt is the probability of picking the correct box the second time after not picking it the first time. Thus, for example, P(win on second attempt) = 3 x 3 This gives the equation for X 100 = 2.5X, and solving we see X = 40.

As a quick 'sanity check', why must the price be at least 25 (think about it before you read on)? If the price was less than 25, playing just once would be a game favourable to the player. While this might seem trivial, such checks can be quite handy as a quick way to detect silly mistakes. Here are some possible related questions: e Instead of maintaining a constant price, if you choose to continue playing after two incorrect guesses the price changes to 7. What is the fair value of X and Z? e Generalise the above problem to the case of n boxes. O
problems.explanationZh:
由于这是一场公平的游戏，我们必须让玩家以最佳方式进行游戏，其预期成本等于其预期收益。最优策略是什么？假设我们设定了玩游戏的价格 Y。价格是“公平”的，因为平均而言，玩家至少玩一次不会遭受损失。如果他们没有在第一顺位获胜，那么显然继续比赛是最佳选择。如果当选择正确盒子的几率为 1/4 时值得玩，那么如果您可以以相同的价格玩增加的 1/3 几率，那么它是值得的。所以我们的最佳策略是继续玩，直到你选择正确的盒子。这意味着每次我们采用这个策略，我们都会赢得 100 英镑。因此，我们需要找到一个价格 X，使得玩游戏直到获胜的预期成本为 100。注意 E(成本) = XP(第一次尝试获胜) + 2XP(第二次尝试获胜) + 3XP(第三次尝试获胜) + 4XP(第四次尝试获胜)。我们处理的是条件概率，即第二次尝试获胜的概率是在第一次没有选择正确的盒子后第二次选择正确的盒子的概率。因此，例如，P(第二次尝试获胜) = 3 x 3 这给出了 X 100 = 2.5X 的方程，求解后我们看到 X = 40。

作为快速“健全性检查”，为什么价格必须至少为 25（在继续阅读之前请考虑一下）？如果价格低于25，只玩一次对玩家来说是有利的游戏。虽然这看起来微不足道，但这种检查可以非常方便地作为检测愚蠢错误的快速方法。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 如果您在两次错误猜测后选择继续玩，价格将变为 7，而不是保持恒定价格。 X 和 Z 的公允价值是多少？ e 将上述问题推广到有 n 个盒子的情况。氧

problems.titleEn: Question 3.4 - We play a game: I pick a number n from 1 to 100
problems.titleZh: 问题 3.4 - 我们玩一个游戏：我从 1 到 100 中选择一个数字 n
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: We play a game: I pick a number n from 1 to 100. If you guess correctly, I pay you $n and zero otherwise. How much would you pay to play this game?
problems.promptZh: 我们玩一个游戏：我从 1 到 100 中选择一个数字 n。如果你猜对了，我付给你 n 美元，否则付给你零。你愿意花多少钱来玩这个游戏？
problems.answer: The game value is 1/H_100 dollars, about 0.193 dollars, where H_100=sum_{k=1}^{100} 1/k.
problems.answerEn: The game value is 1/H_100 dollars, about 0.193 dollars, where H_100=sum_{k=1}^{100} 1/k.
problems.explanation:
This is a zero-sum strategy question. If the picker chooses number k with probability q_k, and you guess k, your expected payoff is k q_k.

The picker minimizes your best response by making k q_k constant across all k. Thus q_k = c/k.

Since probabilities sum to 1, c sum_{k=1}^{100} 1/k = 1, so c = 1/H_100.

With this strategy your expected payoff is 1/H_100 no matter which number you guess. Therefore the fair price is 1/H_100 dollars, approximately 0.193 dollars.
problems.explanationEn:
This is a zero-sum strategy question. If the picker chooses number k with probability q_k, and you guess k, your expected payoff is k q_k.

The picker minimizes your best response by making k q_k constant across all k. Thus q_k = c/k.

Since probabilities sum to 1, c sum_{k=1}^{100} 1/k = 1, so c = 1/H_100.

With this strategy your expected payoff is 1/H_100 no matter which number you guess. Therefore the fair price is 1/H_100 dollars, approximately 0.193 dollars.
problems.explanationZh:
这是一个零和策略问题。若出题者以概率 q_k 选择数字 k，而你猜 k，则你的期望收益为 k q_k。

出题者为了最小化你的最佳反应，会让所有 k q_k 相等。因此 q_k = c/k。

由于概率和为 1，有 c sum_{k=1}^{100} 1/k = 1，所以 c = 1/H_100。

在这个策略下，无论你猜哪个数字，期望收益都是 1/H_100。因此公平价格为 1/H_100 美元，约 0.193 美元。

problems.titleEn: Question 3.5 - Suppose you have a fair coin
problems.titleZh: 问题 3.5 - 公平硬币倍增/减半游戏
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Suppose you have a fair coin. You start with a dollar, and if you toss a H, your position doubles, if you toss a 7', your position halves.. What is the expected value of the money you have if you toss the coin infinitely?
problems.promptZh: 假设你有一枚公平硬币。你从 1 美元开始；若抛出 H，你的头寸翻倍；若抛出 T，你的头寸减半。如果无限次抛硬币，你拥有的钱的期望值是多少？
problems.answer: After n tosses, the expected wealth is (5/4)^n dollars, so the expected value tends to infinity as n goes to infinity.
problems.answerEn: After n tosses, the expected wealth is (5/4)^n dollars, so the expected value tends to infinity as n goes to infinity.
problems.answerZh: 抛 n 次后，期望财富为 (5/4)^n 美元；因此当 n 趋于无穷时，期望值趋于无穷大。
problems.explanation:
Each toss multiplies the current wealth by
$$M=\begin{cases}2,&\text{with probability }1/2,\\
1/2,&\text{with probability }1/2.\end{cases}$$
Thus
$$E[M]=\frac12\cdot2+\frac12\cdot\frac12=\frac54.$$
For independent tosses, wealth after $n$ tosses is
$$W_n=\prod_{j=1}^n M_j,$$
so
$$E[W_n]=\prod_{j=1}^n E[M_j]=\left(\frac54\right)^n.$$
As $n\to\infty$, this expectation diverges to infinity.
problems.explanationEn:
Each toss multiplies the current wealth by
$$M=\begin{cases}2,&\text{with probability }1/2,\\
1/2,&\text{with probability }1/2.\end{cases}$$
Thus
$$E[M]=\frac12\cdot2+\frac12\cdot\frac12=\frac54.$$
For independent tosses, wealth after $n$ tosses is
$$W_n=\prod_{j=1}^n M_j,$$
so
$$E[W_n]=\prod_{j=1}^n E[M_j]=\left(\frac54\right)^n.$$
As $n\to\infty$, this expectation diverges to infinity.
problems.explanationZh:
每次抛硬币会把当前财富乘以
$$M=\begin{cases}2,&\text{概率 }1/2,\\
1/2,&\text{概率 }1/2.\end{cases}$$
因此
$$E[M]=\frac12\cdot2+\frac12\cdot\frac12=\frac54.$$
独立抛掷 $n$ 次后的财富为
$$W_n=\prod_{j=1}^n M_j,$$
所以
$$E[W_n]=\prod_{j=1}^n E[M_j]=\left(\frac54\right)^n.$$
当 $n\to\infty$ 时，该期望发散到无穷大。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Fair-coin multiplicative game had T mistranslated as 7 and broken expectation formulas.

problems.titleEn: Question 3.6 - Suppose we toss a fair coin, and let N denote the number of tosses until we get a head (including the final toss)
problems.titleZh: 问题 3.6 - 假设我们抛一枚公平的硬币，让 N 表示直到出现正面（包括最后一次抛掷）为止的抛掷次数
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Suppose we toss a fair coin, and let N denote the number of tosses until we get a head (including the final toss). What is E(.V) and Var(N)?
problems.promptZh: 假设我们抛一枚公平的硬币，让 N 表示直到出现正面（包括最后一次抛掷）为止的抛掷次数。什么是 E(.V) 和 Var(N)？
problems.explanation:
The random variable N has a geometric distribution. Some care should be taken to avoid ambiguities arising from the inclusion of the

final toss - some definitions include it, giving a state space \{1,2,3,...\}, while others do not, giving a state space \{0, 1,2,...\}. Here we use the former, although this is not simply a question asking you to recite expectations and variances of common distributions so the definition is not important as long as we are clear. You may well already know the answer, but odds on if you're being asked this in an interview they want to see if you can derive it, so keep the answer to yourself and just use it to check what you arrive at from first principles. Given our definition of N, it is clear that if N = k, then we had k - 1 consecutive tails followed by 1 head. From this we conclude P(N = k) = 0.50.5 = 0.5%, for k = 1,2,... Using the standard calculation for E(X) we have CO E(X) = 5° 0.5*k. k=1 If you remember the formula for this series then you are done. If you don't, or forget it under the pressure of the interview, here's a nice trick for quickly calculating it (which is easily extended to similar problems): E(X) = 0.514+2x 0.57 +3 x 0.53+--- => 0.5E(X) = 0.57 +2 x 0.5° +3 x 0.544--- => 0.5E(X) = 0.51+0.57 + 0.53 +---, the final line being the difference of the first two. This is also just the sum of the probabilities P(N = k) and hence adds to 1, thus E(X) = 2. Applying the same technique (twice) gives 2 - 0.5 E(X') = ——" =6 (*") 0.52, and so Var(X) = 2.

Here are some possible related questions: e I pay you \$1 at the end of this year, \$2 at the end of next year and so on. The effective annual interest rate is t«. Derive, from first principles, the value of this payment stream. ® Derive the expectation and variance of a random variable with a Poisson distribution. e What is the mean and expectation if we redefine N to be the number of tails before we toss a head? O
