problems.explanationZh:
如果一个类使用内存分配，那么它将使用新命令。这将返回一个指向动态分配内存的指针。如果我们将指针作为类数据成员，那么如果我们使用编译器的默认运算符，复制和赋值操作将导致问题。特别是，编译器提供的复制构造函数将是“浅”复制构造函数，即它只会复制指针。这样做的效果是原始对象和副本将指向同一块分配的内存。这会导致很多问题。例如，正确编写的对象应该在其析构函数中删除指向的内存（如果之前没有的话）。浅拷贝会导致这种情况发生两次，并导致崩溃。更一般地，即使以某种其他方式正确处理删除，两个对象也会紧密地联系在一起，并且行为不会是复制普通对象的行为。分配的问题是类似的，此外还有一个额外的复杂性，即被覆盖的对象将丢失其指向先前分配的内存的指针。这意味着该内存永远不会被释放，并且会发生内存泄漏。关于这个问题需要注意的一些额外要点是，该类还需要正确编写的析构函数，并且许多问题可以通过使用智能指针来避免。这里还值得一提的是“三法则”：如果一个类有析构函数、复制构造函数和赋值运算符之一，那么它通常需要全部三个。就我个人而言，我更喜欢“几乎为零的规则”：当一个类具有抽象方法时，除了一个空的虚拟析构函数之外，这三个函数都不应该有。原因是内存分配和释放处理得更好

7.3.智能指针的解决方案。编写（或在 Boost 中查找）具有所需行为的智能指针类一次，然后使用并重用它。这在编写新类时更不容易出现错误，并且花费的时间也少得多。有关几乎为零规则的更多信息，请参阅[7]的第二版。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 您知道哪些智能指针？ e@为什么抽象类需要虚拟析构函数？橙汁

problems.titleEn: Question 7.17 - Write the code for a sorting algorithm
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.17 - 编写排序算法的代码。讨论程序的速度和内存
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Write the code for a sorting algorithm. Discuss the speed and memory of your program. How can you improve it?
problems.promptZh:
编写排序算法的代码。讨论程序的速度和内存。你可以如何改进它？

7.2.问题
problems.explanation:
The optimal solution to sort an array is to use the Standard Library: \#include <algorithm> MyArray // uses STL sort algorithm to sort input STLSort (const MyArray& input // array to sort ) \{ MyArray tmp(input); std::sort(tmp.begin(), tmp.end()); return tmp; \} This will not do it in place. If you want to do it without copying the array, then don't copy the array, change the input to non-const and the return type to void. It is unlikely that the interviewer will be satisfied with this response, however, since they are probably trying to test your skill with algorithms. It is also worth knowing that the STL sort algorithm is not guaranteed to be a stable sort, that is it can change the order of equivalent elements (i.e. neither a < b not b > a.) If they ask you for a stable algorithm use stable_sort instead.

7. CODING IN C++ Here is a simple implementation of the insert sort algorithm: template<class T> void InsertSort(T start, T end) \{ for(T i=start; i<end; ++i) \{ T::value_type value = *i; T dec = i; while(dec > start && *(dec-1)>=value) \{ *dec=*(dec-1); --dec; \} *dec=value; \} \} The type T is an iterator (or pointer). This allows the code to be generic and applied to a vector of any type with comparison operators. Insert sort is simple but not particularly fast. It works as follows: e Sort first 7 elements. e Take element 7 + 1 and sift upwards until you find the place where it fits in. e Insert element 7 + 1 in the correct position and shuffle the elements downwards to get the first 7 + 1 elements to be sorted. e Keep on going until 7 reaches the end. Important points to know about it are: e It is stable. e It is in-place and requires no extra memory.

e It is order n? in complexity. e The speed depends on how sorted the array already is. If the array is sorted then the speed is order n. e It can be used incrementally. i.e. sort a stream of numbers as they arrive rather than having to see them all before starting. There exist faster algorithms which are order n log n. One easy such algorithm is merge sort. With merge sort, one sorts the top half and bottom, and then merges the two. To sort each half, we can recursively call merge until the array is of length one. The main downside of this algorithm is that it is really fiddly to implement without extra temporary memory. Note that one could shift to using another algorithm once the array got small, e.g. use insert sort if length is less than 20. Other algorithms that are popular are heap sort and quick sort. There is extensive discussion on Wikipedia of the pros and cons of various choices. Here are some possible related questions: e Do you think it is worth hand-coding STL algorithms? e Implement merge sort. e How would you sort a list? (Lists are non-contiguous in memory so random access iterators cannot be used.) e If objects were expensive to copy and assign, how would this affect choice and implementation of a sort routine? e Why would you want a stable sort? O

problems.explanationZh:
对数组进行排序的最佳解决方案是使用标准库： \#include <algorithm> MyArray // 使用 STL 排序算法对输入进行排序 STLSort (const MyArray& input // 要排序的数组 ) \{ MyArray tmp(input); std::sort(tmp.begin(), tmp.end());返回tmp； \} 这不会到位。如果您想在不复制数组的情况下执行此操作，则不要复制数组，将输入更改为非 const 并将返回类型更改为 void。然而，面试官不太可能对这个回答感到满意，因为他们可能正在尝试测试你的算法技能。还值得知道的是，STL 排序算法不能保证是稳定排序，即它可以更改等效元素的顺序（即既不是 a < b 也不是 b > a。）如果他们要求您提供稳定的算法，请使用 stable_sort 代替。

7. C++ 中的编码 下面是插入排序算法的简单实现： template<class T> void InsertSort(T start, T end) \{ for(T i=start; i<end; ++i) \{ T::value_type value = *i; T 十进制 = i; while(dec > 开始 && *(dec-1)>=值) \{ *dec=*(dec-1); ——十二月； \} *dec=值； \} \} 类型 T 是一个迭代器（或指针）。这允许代码是通用的，并可以通过比较运算符应用于任何类型的向量。插入排序很简单，但不是特别快。其工作原理如下： e 对前 7 个元素进行排序。 e 取出元素 7 + 1 并向上筛选，直到找到适合的位置。 e 将元素 7 + 1 插入到正确的位置，然后向下打乱元素，以获得要排序的前 7 + 1 个元素。 e 继续前进，直到 7 到达终点。需要了解的重要一点是： e 它是稳定的。 e 它就位，不需要额外的内存。

7.3.解 e 是 n 阶？在复杂性上。 e 速度取决于数组的排序方式。如果数组已排序，则速度为 n 阶。 e 可以增量使用。即在数字流到达时对其进行排序，而不是在开始之前必须查看所有数字。存在更快的 n log n 阶算法。一种简单的此类算法是合并排序。使用归并排序时，先对上半部分和下半部分进行排序，然后将两者合并。为了对每一半进行排序，我们可以递归调用 merge 直到数组的长度为一。该算法的主要缺点是在没有额外临时内存的情况下实现起来非常繁琐。请注意，一旦数组变小，人们可以转向使用另一种算法，例如如果长度小于 20，则使用插入排序。其他流行的算法是堆排序和快速排序。维基百科上对各种选择的利弊进行了广泛的讨论。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 您认为值得手动编码 STL 算法吗？ e 实施归并排序。 e 您将如何对列表进行排序？ （列表在内存中是不连续的，因此不能使用随机访问迭代器。） e 如果对象的复制和分配成本很高，这将如何影响排序例程的选择和实现？ e 为什么你想要一个稳定的排序？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 7.18 - Computer problem: If z is of type int, keep increasing x and plot
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.18 - 计算机问题：如果z是int类型，继续增加x并绘图
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Computer problem: If z is of type int, keep increasing x and plot. What is the graph like? If x is of type float?
problems.promptZh: 计算机问题：如果z是int类型，继续增加x并绘图。图表是什么样的？如果 x 是 float 类型？
problems.explanation:
In C++ the maximum value of an int is typically 231 _1. If we add one to that we get the largest negative number: —23'. Repeating therefore gives us a saw-tooth type wave. If we take a float or double then eventually adding one has no effect because the value 1 as a fraction of the number is too small to show up in the significant digits which are stored as the value of the number.

7. CODING IN C++ Here are some possible related questions: e What is the largest value that can be represented by a double in C++? e What is the smallest value that can be represented by a double in C++? e What strategies would you use to minimize floating point round off error in C++? e How do you access the constant for the largest number in C++? O

在 C++ 中，int 的最大值通常为 231 _1。如果我们加一，我们就会得到最大的负数：—23'。因此，重复就会产生锯齿形波。如果我们采用浮点型或双精度型，那么最终加 1 没有任何效果，因为值 1 作为数字的分数太小，无法显示在存储为数字值的有效数字中。

7. C++ 中的编码 以下是一些可能的相关问题： e C++ 中 double 可以表示的最大值是多少？ e C++ 中 double 可以表示的最小值是多少？ e 您将使用什么策略来最小化 C++ 中的浮点舍入误差？ e 在 C++ 中如何访问最大数常量？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 7.19 - Where are “static” variables stored
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.19 - “静态”变量存储在哪里
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Where are “static” variables stored?
problems.promptZh: “静态”变量存储在哪里？
problems.explanation: There are three main storage areas for variables in C++: e the heap; e the stack; e the data segment. Static variables are stored in the data segment, all the memory and locations for each variable are assigned before the program starts. They are not necessarily initialized, however, until their declaration is reached in the code. The heap is used for dynamically allocated memory, e.g. anything allocated using new or malloc. The stack is used for ordinary (automatic) variables. Here are some possible related questions: e When are static variables destroyed? e Give an example of whena static variable would be useful. O

They are not necessarily initialized, however, until their declaration is reached in the code. The heap is used for dynamically allocated memory, e.g. anything allocated using new or malloc. The stack is used for ordinary (automatic) variables. Here are some possible related questions: e When are static variables destroyed? e Give an example of whena static variable would be useful. O
problems.explanationZh: C++ 中变量的存储区域主要有以下三个： e 堆； e 堆栈； e 数据段。静态变量存储在数据段中，每个变量的所有内存和位置在程序启动之前分配。然而，在代码中到达它们的声明之前，它们不一定被初始化。堆用于动态分配的内存，例如使用 new 或 malloc 分配的任何内容。堆栈用于普通（自动）变量。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 静态变量何时被销毁？ e 举例说明静态变量何时有用。氧

problems.titleEn: Question 7.20 - How is virtuality implemented in C++
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.20 - C++ 中的虚拟性是如何实现的
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: How is virtuality implemented in C++?
problems.promptZh: C++ 中的虚拟性是如何实现的？
problems.explanation:
This is arguably a trick question in that the C++ Standard says nothing about how virtual functions are implemented. So the correct answer is “However the compiler writer felt like doing it.” Nevertheless there is a standard approach which most if not all compilers use. This is to have table called the “virtual function table” where pointers to all the virtual functions are stored. There will be one copy of the table for each

class. Each object from a class with virtual functions will contain a pointer to the table for its class. Note that every object is slightly larger if virtual methods are declared. There is also an issue with some compilers as to how many copies of the virtual function table there are. One solution adopted by some older compilers was to place the table in the same translation unit as the definition of the destructor. This meant that if you declared virtual “MyClass () \{\} in the header file for your class, then a copy of the virtual function table was put in the object file for every source file that directly or indirectly included it. For this reason, it is sometimes recommended to always define the destructor in the source file. Most modern compilers should be smarter than this, however. Here are some possible related questions: e Are static virtual functions possible in C++? Should they be? e It is possible to call a pure virtual function? O

For this reason, it is sometimes recommended to always define the destructor in the source file. Most modern compilers should be smarter than this, however. Here are some possible related questions: e Are static virtual functions possible in C++? Should they be? e It is possible to call a pure virtual function? O
problems.explanationZh:
这可以说是一个棘手的问题，因为 C++ 标准没有提及虚拟函数是如何实现的。所以正确的答案是“但是编译器编写者想要这样做。”尽管如此，大多数（如果不是全部）编译器都使用一种标准方法。这是一个称为“虚拟函数表”的表，其中存储了指向所有虚拟函数的指针。每个人都会有一份表格副本

7.3.解决方案类。具有虚函数的类中的每个对象都将包含一个指向其类的表的指针。请注意，如果声明了虚方法，每个对象都会稍微大一些。一些编译器还存在一个问题，即虚拟函数表有多少个副本。一些较旧的编译器采用的一种解决方案是将表放置在与析构函数的定义相同的翻译单元中。这意味着，如果您在类的头文件中声明了 virtual “MyClass () \{\}，则虚函数表的副本将被放入直接或间接包含它的每个源文件的目标文件中。出于这个原因，有时建议始终在源文件中定义析构函数。然而，大多数现代编译器应该比这更聪明。以下是一些可能的相关问题：e C++ 中可能存在静态虚函数吗？应该吗？e 可以调用纯虚函数吗？O

problems.titleEn: Question 7.21 - What is the problem with virtualization from the point of view of optimization
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.21 - 从优化的角度来看，虚拟化存在什么问题
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What is the problem with virtualization from the point of view of optimization? What can a compiler do when a function is not virtualized?
problems.promptZh: 从优化的角度来看，虚拟化存在什么问题？当函数未虚拟化时编译器可以做什么？
problems.explanation:
There are a number of issues with virtual functions and optimization. The first is that they are typically called using pointer indirection, that is the processor has to jump to a location contained in a pointer rather than a pre-determined location. This results in a few extra instructions. The second is more serious: if the compiler and linker do not know what is contained in the function call they cannot perform any optimizations based on its contents. In particular, virtual functions cannot be inlined. In addition, a virtual function table will be created for the class and every object from the class will need to contain a pointer to it. For large numbers of smal] objects, this can be a real issue.

7. CODING IN C++ One should realize that these are not huge issues unless the code contained in the function is very short. Thus it makes sense when a method is complicated for it to be virtual but for accessor functions there can be a real performance hit. A further possible issue is that the virtual function tables for different classes can be in quite different parts of memory, this can result in the memory cached in the processor having to change which results in further slow-downs. Here are some possible related questions: e If we replaced a virtual function with a function pointer would that help optimization? e If we used templates instead would that help? e Do you think that virtual functions are worth the overhead? O Solution to Question to 7.22. The delete command is used to deallocate memory that was allocated using the new command, that is the memory for a single object. The delete[] command performs the corresponding task when memory was allocated using new[], that is an array of objects. The C++ standard states if you call the wrong operator then you get undefined behaviour. In practice, if you are lucky then you get a memory leak and if you are unlucky you get a crash. (Some people would prefer a crash since it is easier to spot!) Here are some possible related questions: e What happens if you delete a null pointer? e It is possible to redefine the new and delete commands. How would you redefine them so that they work seamlessly when the wrong operator is called? e Why do you think the language has the two commands rather than just one? O

problems.explanationZh:
虚拟功能和优化存在许多问题。首先，它们通常使用指针间接调用，即处理器必须跳转到指针中包含的位置而不是预先确定的位置。这会产生一些额外的指令。第二个更严重：如果编译器和链接器不知道函数调用中包含什么，它们就无法根据其内容执行任何优化。特别是，虚拟函数不能内联。此外，将为该类创建一个虚函数表，并且该类中的每个对象都需要包含一个指向它的指针。对于大量小物体，这可能是一个真正的问题。

7. C++ 中的编码 人们应该认识到，除非函数中包含的代码非常短，否则这些都不是大问题。因此，当一个方法因虚拟而变得复杂时，这是有意义的，但对于访问器函数来说，可能会真正影响性能。另一个可能的问题是，不同类的虚拟函数表可能位于内存的不同部分，这可能导致处理器中缓存的内存必须更改，从而导致速度进一步减慢。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 如果我们用函数指针替换虚函数是否有助于优化？ e 如果我们使用模板会有帮助吗？ e 您认为虚拟函数值得花费这些开销吗？ O 7.22 问题的解答。 delete命令用于释放使用new命令分配的内存，即单个对象的内存。当使用 new[]（对象数组）分配内存时，delete[] 命令执行相应的任务。 C++ 标准规定，如果调用错误的运算符，则会出现未定义的行为。在实践中，如果你幸运的话，就会发生内存泄漏，如果你不幸的话，就会发生崩溃。 （有些人更喜欢崩溃，因为它更容易发现！）以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 如果删除空指针会发生什么？ e 可以重新定义新命令和删除命令。您将如何重新定义它们，以便在调用错误的操作员时它们可以无缝工作？ e 为什么你认为该语言有两个命令而不是一个？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 7.23 - A student claims that the
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.23 - 一名学生声称 C++ 中的
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers

problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: A student claims that the?: construct in C++ is redundant since it can be implemented in terms of other language features. He suggests replacing it with the following function int condif (bool condition, int i, int j) \{ if (condition) return i; else return j; \} Do you agree with the student that for integers there is no difference?
problems.promptZh: 一名学生声称 C++ 中的?: 结构是多余的，因为它可以用其他语言功能来实现。他建议将其替换为以下函数 int condif (bool condition, int i, int j) \{ if (condition) return i;否则返回j； \} 你是否同意学生的观点，即整数没有区别？
problems.explanation: The C++ standard states that for the 7: operator only one of the two final expressions is evaluated. If one used the function condif, they would both be evaluated. This has two consequences: speed and side-effects. In particular, if the expressions are expensive to evaluate then computing both of them will slow the code down. It is also important to realize that in C++ there is little difference between an expression and an operation so the expressions can do things other than return a value. The use of condif will result in any changes that these operations cause occurring for both of them rather than just one. A secondary speed issue is that a function call would take more time than calling an inbuilt operator. Here are some possible related questions: ® How would you write code that correctly simulates the behaviour of?: for integers? e Extend this code to work for a general type. O
problems.explanationEn: The C++ standard states that for the 7: operator only one of the two final expressions is evaluated. If one used the function condif, they would both be evaluated. It is also important to realize that in C++ there is little difference between an expression and an operation so the expressions can do things other than return a value. The use of condif will result in any changes that these operations cause occurring for both of them rather than just one. problems.titleEn: Question 7.24 - Uniform draws per Gaussian draw in polar Box-Muller
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.24 - Polar Box-Muller 中每个高斯样本需要多少均匀样本
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn:
We present C++ code that gives draws from a standard Gaussian distribution with mean 0 and standard deviation 1. Estimate the number of uniform draws for each Gaussian draw. \#include <cmath> // this gives one uniform and updates seed double drawUniform(long& seed);

7. CODING IN C++ // draws a standard Gaussian double drawGaussian(long& seed) \{ static bool drawNotDone=true; static double otherDraw; double factor, rSquared, x, y; if (drawNotDone) \{ do \{ x = 2.0 * drawUniform(seed) - 1.0; y = 2.0 * drawUniform(seed) - 1.0; rSquared =x * xX + y * y; \} while (rSquared >= 1.0 || rSquared == 0.0); factor = sqrt(-2.0 * log(rSquared) / rSquared); otherDraw = x * factor; drawNotDone=false; return y * factor; \} else \{ drawNotDone=true; return otherDraw; \} \}
problems.promptZh: 下面的 C++ 代码用 polar Box-Muller 方法生成均值 0、标准差 1 的标准高斯样本。估计每生成一个高斯样本平均需要多少个均匀随机数。
problems.answer:
The acceptance probability for a proposed pair is the unit disk area inside the square $[-1,1]^2$:
$$p=\frac{\pi}{4}.$$
Each accepted pair uses 2 uniform draws and produces 2 Gaussian draws, so the expected number of uniform draws per Gaussian draw is
$$\frac{1}{p}=\frac{4}{\pi}\approx1.27.$$
problems.answerEn:
The acceptance probability for a proposed pair is the unit disk area inside the square $[-1,1]^2$:
$$p=\frac{\pi}{4}.$$
Each accepted pair uses 2 uniform draws and produces 2 Gaussian draws, so the expected number of uniform draws per Gaussian draw is
$$\frac{1}{p}=\frac{4}{\pi}\approx1.27.$$
problems.answerZh:
每个候选点对的接受概率是正方形 $[-1,1]^2$ 中单位圆的面积比例：
$$p=\frac{\pi}{4}.$$
每个被接受的点对使用 2 个均匀样本并产生 2 个高斯样本，因此每个高斯样本平均需要的均匀样本数为
$$\frac{1}{p}=\frac{4}{\pi}\approx1.27.$$
problems.explanation:
The code proposes $(x,y)$ uniformly in the square $[-1,1]^2$ and accepts it only if
$$0<x^2+y^2<1.$$
The square has area $4$ and the unit disk has area $\pi$, so a proposed pair is accepted with probability
$$p=\frac{\pi}{4}.$$

The number of proposed pairs needed for one accepted pair has expectation $1/p=4/\pi$. Each proposed pair consumes 2 uniform draws, and each accepted pair produces 2 independent Gaussian draws. These factors cancel, so the expected number of uniform draws per Gaussian draw is
$$\frac{4}{\pi}\approx1.27.$$
problems.explanationEn:
The code proposes $(x,y)$ uniformly in the square $[-1,1]^2$ and accepts it only if
$$0<x^2+y^2<1.$$
The square has area $4$ and the unit disk has area $\pi$, so a proposed pair is accepted with probability
$$p=\frac{\pi}{4}.$$

The number of proposed pairs needed for one accepted pair has expectation $1/p=4/\pi$. Each proposed pair consumes 2 uniform draws, and each accepted pair produces 2 independent Gaussian draws. These factors cancel, so the expected number of uniform draws per Gaussian draw is
$$\frac{4}{\pi}\approx1.27.$$
problems.explanationZh:
代码在正方形 $[-1,1]^2$ 中均匀生成 $(x,y)$，并且只在
$$0<x^2+y^2<1$$
时接受该点对。正方形面积为 $4$，单位圆面积为 $\pi$，所以候选点对的接受概率为
$$p=\frac{\pi}{4}.$$

得到一个被接受点对所需的候选点对数量期望为 $1/p=4/\pi$。每个候选点对消耗 2 个均匀样本，而每个被接受点对产生 2 个独立高斯样本。这两个因子抵消，因此每个高斯样本平均需要的均匀样本数为
$$\frac{4}{\pi}\approx1.27.$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Box-Muller acceptance probability and uniform-draw estimate were OCR-corrupted.

problems.titleEn: Question 7.25 - Reverse a string in C++
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.25 - 用 C++ 反转字符串
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Write a C++ function that takes as input a character string and returns a new string that is the reverse of its input. Write another variant that performs the reversal in place without doing any memory allocation.
problems.promptZh: 写一个 C++ 函数，输入一个字符串并返回它的反转字符串。再写一个不做额外内存分配、原地反转的版本。
problems.explanation:
The first version allocates a new string and constructs it from reverse iterators. Its time complexity is $O(n)$ and it uses $O(n)$ extra memory.

The in-place version swaps symmetric character pairs. It also runs in $O(n)$ time but uses $O(1)$ extra memory. The loop stops at s.size()/2, so it works for both even and odd lengths.
problems.explanationEn:
The first version allocates a new string and constructs it from reverse iterators. Its time complexity is $O(n)$ and it uses $O(n)$ extra memory.

The in-place version swaps symmetric character pairs. It also runs in $O(n)$ time but uses $O(1)$ extra memory. The loop stops at s.size()/2, so it works for both even and odd lengths.
problems.explanationZh:
第一个版本分配一个新字符串，并通过反向迭代器构造结果。时间复杂度为 $O(n)$，额外空间为 $O(n)$。

原地版本交换对称位置的字符。它同样是 $O(n)$ 时间，但额外空间为 $O(1)$。循环到 s.size()/2 停止，因此奇数长度和偶数长度都能正确处理。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Reverse-string solution had invalid C++ fragments and spillover from the next question.

problems.titleEn: Question 7.27 - Consider the following code: \#include <iostream> \#include <memory> using namespace std; class Alpha \{ public: Alpha(): ptrAlpha(
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.27 - 考虑以下代码： \#include <iostream> \#include <memory> using namespace std;类 Alpha \{ public: Alpha(): ptrAlpha(new int) \{ t Alpha() \{ 删
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn:
Consider the following code: \#include <iostream> \#include <memory> using namespace std; class Alpha \{ public: Alpha(): ptrAlpha(new int) \{ t \sim Alpha() \{ delete ptrAlpha; \} virtual void MyFunction() \{ cout << "In Alpha" << endl; \} private: int* ptrAlpha; \}; class Beta: public Alpha \{ public: Beta(): ptrBeta(new int) \{\} \sim Beta() \{ delete ptrBeta; \}

7. CODING IN C++ void MyFunction () \{ cout << "In Beta" << endl; \} private: int *ptrBeta; I; class Gamma: public Alpha \{ public: Gamma(): ptrGamma(new int) \{ \} \sim Gamma() \{ delete ptrGamma; \} void MyFunction () \{ cout << "In Gamma" << endl; \} private: int *ptrGamma; \}; int main() \{ \{
problems.explanation:
This is a question about virtual functions and virtual destruction. The first part to note is that the method MyFunction is declared virtual in the base class. Once a method is virtual it is always virtual so it will be virtual in the inherited classes too. The pointer alpha1 is a pointer to an object of type Beta so the invocation of the method will yield “In Beta.” Similarly with the reference alpha2, the object pointed to is of type Gamma so we get “In Gamma.” The correctness of the program is more subtle. A important rule to follow is that a function containing virtual functions should always have a virtual destructor, and this program does not. We should therefore be suspicious. In fact, there is a memory leak caused by this omission. When the auto_ptr goes out of scope, the pointed to-object is deleted but the destructor of the base class is called, not that of the inherited class. This can be fixed simply by putting virtual in front of the base class destructor. Here are some possible related questions: e Why do you think destructors are not always virtual in C++?

e What are the pros and cons of using auto_ptrs? e What is your favourite smart pointer? O
problems.explanationEn:
This is a question about virtual functions and virtual destruction. The first part to note is that the method MyFunction is declared virtual in the base class. Once a method is virtual it is always virtual so it will be virtual in the inherited classes too. The pointer alpha1 is a pointer to an object of type Beta so the invocation of the method will yield “In Beta.” Similarly with the reference alpha2, the object pointed to is of type Gamma so we get “In Gamma.” The correctness of the program is more subtle. A important rule to follow is that a function containing virtual functions should always have a virtual destructor, and this program does not. We should therefore be suspicious. In fact, there is a memory leak caused by this omission. When the auto_ptr goes out of scope, the pointed to-object is deleted but the destructor of the base class is called, not that of the inherited class. This can be fixed simply by putting virtual in front of the base class destructor. Here are some possible related questions: e Why do you think destructors are not always virtual in C++?

e What are the pros and cons of using auto_ptrs? e What is your favourite smart pointer? O
problems.explanationZh:
这是一个关于虚函数和虚销毁的问题。首先要注意的是方法 MyFunction 在基类中被声明为 virtual。一旦一个方法是虚拟的，它就始终是虚拟的，因此它在继承的类中也将是虚拟的。指针 alpha1 是指向 Beta 类型对象的指针，因此该方法的调用将产生“In Beta”。与引用 alpha2 类似，指向的对象属于 Gamma 类型，因此我们得到“In Gamma”。程序的正确性更加微妙。要遵循的一个重要规则是，包含虚函数的函数应该始终具有虚析构函数，而该程序没有。因此我们应该持怀疑态度。事实上，正是由于这个遗漏而导致了内存泄漏。当 auto_ptr 超出范围时，所指向的对象将被删除，但会调用基类的析构函数，而不是继承类的析构函数。只需将 virtual 放在基类析构函数前面即可解决此问题。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 为什么你认为析构函数在 C++ 中并不总是虚拟的？

7.3.解决方案 e 使用 auto_ptrs 的优点和缺点是什么？ e 您最喜欢的智能指针是什么？氧

7. C++ 编码 \{ public: Beta() \{ cout << "Creating Beta" << endl; \} Beta() \{ cout << "摧毁 Beta" << endl; \} \}； class Gamma: public Alpha \{ public: Gamma () \{ cout << "创建 Gamma" << end1; t Gamma() \{ cout << "摧毁伽玛" << endl; \} 私有：Beta b； \}； int main() \{ \{ 伽玛 c; \} 字符d；
problems.explanation: This is a question about order of creation and destruction: if we have a class Gamma inherited from Alpha and containing a data member of type Beta in what order are they created and destroyed? The general rule is most primitive first for construction and last for destruction. A base class is more primitive than a class inherited from it, and data members are more primitive than the class they are members of. The bases of a class are more primitive than its data members. In this case, this means we get the output Creating Alpha Creating Beta Creating Gamma Destroying Gamma Destroying Beta Destroying Alpha Here are some possible related questions: e What other possible orderings could you use, and would they be as good? e Why are there curly brackets around the declaration of the object c? How would the output change if they were deleted? e What happens if a class has multiple data members? O

The general rule is most primitive first for construction and last for destruction. A base class is more primitive than a class inherited from it, and data members are more primitive than the class they are members of. The bases of a class are more primitive than its data members. In this case, this means we get the output Creating Alpha Creating Beta Creating Gamma Destroying Gamma Destroying Beta Destroying Alpha Here are some possible related questions: e What other possible orderings could you use, and would they be as good? e Why are there curly brackets around the declaration of the object c? How would the output change if they were deleted? e What happens if a class has multiple data members? O

problems.titleEn: Question 7.29 - Compute square roots without std::sqrt
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.29 - 不使用 std::sqrt 计算平方根
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Write a C++ function to compute square roots without using the standard library. Wrap your function in a main program so that it can be called from the command line.
problems.promptZh: 写一个 C++ 函数，在不调用标准库平方根函数的情况下计算平方根。可以把它包装进 main 程序，从命令行读取输入。
problems.explanation:
Newton's method solves $g^2=x$. Starting from a positive guess $g$, the update is
$$g_{\text{new}}=\frac12\left(g+\frac{x}{g}\right).$$
For positive $x$ and a positive initial guess, this converges quickly to $\sqrt{x}$. The implementation checks negative input, handles zero separately, and stops when the squared error is small relative to the scale of $x$.
problems.explanationEn:
Newton's method solves $g^2=x$. Starting from a positive guess $g$, the update is
$$g_{\text{new}}=\frac12\left(g+\frac{x}{g}\right).$$
For positive $x$ and a positive initial guess, this converges quickly to $\sqrt{x}$. The implementation checks negative input, handles zero separately, and stops when the squared error is small relative to the scale of $x$.
problems.explanationZh:
Newton 方法用于求解 $g^2=x$。从正的初始猜测 $g$ 出发，迭代为
$$g_{\text{new}}=\frac12\left(g+\frac{x}{g}\right).$$
当 $x>0$ 且初始猜测为正时，该迭代会快速收敛到 $\sqrt{x}$。实现中需要检查负输入，单独处理零，并在平方误差相对 $x$ 的尺度足够小时停止。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Square-root implementation had corrupted code and prompt spillover.

problems.titleEn: Question 7.30 - Binary tree of integers in C++
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.30 - 用 C++ 表示整数二叉树
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Implement in C++ a data structure to represent a binary tree which contains one integer at each node. Discuss the empty tree. Write a function that takes a tree and returns the tree depth, with an empty tree having depth zero. Write a function that returns the maximum element in a non-empty tree.
problems.promptZh: 用 C++ 实现一种数据结构来表示一棵每个节点包含一个整数的二叉树。讨论空树。写一个函数返回树的深度，其中空树深度为零；再写一个函数返回非空树中的最大元素。
problems.explanation:
Using std::unique_ptr makes ownership explicit: each node owns its left and right subtrees. The empty tree is simply nullptr.

The depth function is recursive. An empty tree has depth zero; a non-empty tree has depth one plus the maximum depth of its two children.

The maximum-value function is only defined for a non-empty tree. It recursively computes the maximum of the current node, the left subtree, and the right subtree.
problems.explanationEn:
Using std::unique_ptr makes ownership explicit: each node owns its left and right subtrees. The empty tree is simply nullptr.

The depth function is recursive. An empty tree has depth zero; a non-empty tree has depth one plus the maximum depth of its two children.

The maximum-value function is only defined for a non-empty tree. It recursively computes the maximum of the current node, the left subtree, and the right subtree.
problems.explanationZh:
使用 std::unique_ptr 可以明确所有权：每个节点拥有自己的左子树和右子树。空树就是 nullptr。

深度函数使用递归。空树深度为零；非空树深度为一加左右子树深度的较大者。

最大值函数只对非空树有定义。它递归地比较当前节点、左子树和右子树中的最大值。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Binary-tree answer fields were empty and the original solution was truncated.

problems.titleEn: Question 7.31 - Uses of const in C++
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.31 - C++ 中 const 的用法
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What do you know about const?
problems.promptZh: 你对 C++ 中的 const 了解多少？
problems.answer: const can qualify variables, function parameters, return values, member functions, data members, and pointer declarations. It expresses that a value or object state should not be modified through that name, though mutable and const_cast create important caveats.
problems.answerEn: const can qualify variables, function parameters, return values, member functions, data members, and pointer declarations. It expresses that a value or object state should not be modified through that name, though mutable and const_cast create important caveats.
problems.answerZh: const 可以修饰变量、函数参数、返回值、成员函数、数据成员和指针声明。它表示不能通过该名称修改值或对象状态，但 mutable 和 const_cast 是重要例外与风险点。
problems.explanation:
Common uses:

- const int x = 3; means x cannot be reassigned.
- void f(const Big& x); avoids copying and promises not to modify x.
- int size() const; means the member function should not modify the observable state of the object.
- const int* p is a pointer to const int; int* const p is a const pointer to mutable int; const int* const p is both.
- A const data member must be initialized in the constructor initializer list.

mutable allows selected members to change even inside a const member function, often for caches. const_cast can remove constness from a reference or pointer, but modifying an object that was originally declared const is undefined behavior.
problems.explanationEn:
Common uses:

- const int x = 3; means x cannot be reassigned.
- void f(const Big& x); avoids copying and promises not to modify x.
- int size() const; means the member function should not modify the observable state of the object.
- const int* p is a pointer to const int; int* const p is a const pointer to mutable int; const int* const p is both.
- A const data member must be initialized in the constructor initializer list.

mutable allows selected members to change even inside a const member function, often for caches. const_cast can remove constness from a reference or pointer, but modifying an object that was originally declared const is undefined behavior.
problems.explanationZh:
常见用法包括：

- const int x = 3; 表示 x 不能被重新赋值。
- void f(const Big& x); 避免复制，并承诺不修改 x。
- int size() const; 表示成员函数不应修改对象的可观察状态。
- const int* p 是指向常量 int 的指针；int* const p 是指针本身为常量、所指 int 可变；const int* const p 二者都是常量。
- const 数据成员必须在构造函数初始化列表中初始化。

mutable 允许某些成员即使在 const 成员函数中也可改变，常用于缓存。const_cast 可以移除引用或指针上的 const 限定，但如果对象最初就是 const，修改它会导致未定义行为。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: const question had no direct answer and the Chinese prompt contained spillover from later questions.

problems.titleEn: Question 7.32 - Uses of static in C++
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.32 - C++ 中 static 的用法
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Give all the places where you could use the keyword static.
problems.promptZh: 列举 C++ 中可以使用 static 关键字的地方。
problems.answer: static is used for local variables with static storage duration, class static data members, class static member functions, and namespace-scope internal linkage. In modern C++, anonymous namespaces are often preferred for internal linkage.
problems.answerEn: static is used for local variables with static storage duration, class static data members, class static member functions, and namespace-scope internal linkage. In modern C++, anonymous namespaces are often preferred for internal linkage.
problems.answerZh: static 可用于具有静态存储期的局部变量、类的静态数据成员、类的静态成员函数，以及命名空间作用域的内部链接。在现代 C++ 中，内部链接常优先使用匿名命名空间表达。
problems.explanation:
Main uses of static:

- A static local variable inside a function is initialized once and keeps its value between calls.
- A static data member belongs to the class, not to each individual object.
- A static member function can be called as ClassName::function() and has no this pointer.
- At namespace scope, static gives a variable or function internal linkage, meaning it is visible only in that translation unit.

Thread-safety matters. Since C++11, initialization of function-local statics is thread-safe, but later mutation of shared static state still needs synchronization.
problems.explanationEn:
Main uses of static:

- A static local variable inside a function is initialized once and keeps its value between calls.
- A static data member belongs to the class, not to each individual object.
- A static member function can be called as ClassName::function() and has no this pointer.
- At namespace scope, static gives a variable or function internal linkage, meaning it is visible only in that translation unit.

Thread-safety matters. Since C++11, initialization of function-local statics is thread-safe, but later mutation of shared static state still needs synchronization.
problems.explanationZh:
static 的主要用法包括：

- 函数内的静态局部变量只初始化一次，并在多次调用之间保留值。
- 静态数据成员属于类本身，而不是每个对象各有一份。
- 静态成员函数可用 ClassName::function() 调用，并且没有 this 指针。
- 在命名空间作用域，static 会让变量或函数具有内部链接，即只在当前翻译单元可见。

还要注意线程安全。C++11 起，函数内静态局部变量的初始化是线程安全的，但之后对共享静态状态的修改仍需要同步。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: static question had no answer summary and needed modern C++ wording.

problems.titleEn: Question 7.33 - Polymorphism, abstract classes, pointers, and references
problems.titleZh: 问题 7.33 - 多态、抽象类、指针和引用
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Define the following terms @ What is polymorphism? @ What is an abstract class? e What is a pointer? e@ What is a reference?
problems.promptZh: 定义以下术语：什么是多态？什么是抽象类？什么是指针？什么是引用？
problems.answer: Polymorphism means using a common interface while behavior depends on the dynamic type. An abstract class has at least one pure virtual function and cannot be instantiated. A pointer stores an address and can be null or reseated. A reference is an alias for an existing object and normally cannot be reseated.
problems.answerEn: Polymorphism means using a common interface while behavior depends on the dynamic type. An abstract class has at least one pure virtual function and cannot be instantiated. A pointer stores an address and can be null or reseated. A reference is an alias for an existing object and normally cannot be reseated.
problems.answerZh: 多态是通过共同接口调用对象，而具体行为取决于动态类型。抽象类至少含有一个纯虚函数，不能直接实例化。指针保存地址，可以为空，也可以改指向别处。引用是已有对象的别名，通常不能重新绑定。
problems.explanation:
Polymorphism in C++ is usually implemented with virtual functions. Client code can call a base-class interface while the derived-class implementation runs.

An abstract class contains at least one pure virtual function, written for example as:

    virtual double value(double x) const = 0;

It cannot be instantiated directly; only concrete derived classes can be instantiated.

A pointer is an object whose value is a memory address. It can be null, can be reassigned, and must be dereferenced to access the pointed-to object.

A reference is an alias for another object. It must be bound when created, cannot normally be rebound, and is used syntactically like the object itself.
problems.explanationEn:
Polymorphism in C++ is usually implemented with virtual functions. Client code can call a base-class interface while the derived-class implementation runs.

An abstract class contains at least one pure virtual function, written for example as:

    virtual double value(double x) const = 0;

It cannot be instantiated directly; only concrete derived classes can be instantiated.

A pointer is an object whose value is a memory address. It can be null, can be reassigned, and must be dereferenced to access the pointed-to object.

A reference is an alias for another object. It must be bound when created, cannot normally be rebound, and is used syntactically like the object itself.
problems.explanationZh:
C++ 中的多态通常通过虚函数实现。调用方可以使用基类接口，而运行时执行的是派生类实现。

抽象类至少包含一个纯虚函数，例如：

    virtual double value(double x) const = 0;

抽象类不能直接实例化；只能实例化具体派生类。

指针是一个值为内存地址的对象。它可以为空，可以重新赋值指向别处，访问所指对象时需要解引用。

引用是另一个对象的别名。它创建时必须绑定，通常不能重新绑定，语法上像直接使用原对象。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Definitions of polymorphism, abstract class, pointer, and reference had missing answer fields and spillover.

problems.titleEn: Question 8.1 - At 3.15pm, how many degrees separate the hour and minute hand of a clock
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.1 - 下午 3.15 时，时钟的时针和分针相距多少度
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: At 3.15pm, how many degrees separate the hour and minute hand of a clock?
problems.promptZh: 下午 3.15 时，时钟的时针和分针相距多少度？
problems.explanation: There are 12 hours on a clock and hence 30 degrees between each hour. Obviously it takes one hour for the hour hand to move from the 3 to the 4. Since it is one quarter past 3 (i.e. the minute hand will be exactly on 3), there will be 30/4 = 7.5 degrees between the two hands. Here are some possible related questions: e How many degrees at a quarter to four? e What is the first time before quarter past three when the two hands lie on top of each other? e What is the first time after quarter past three when the two hands lie on top of each other? O
problems.explanationEn: There are 12 hours on a clock and hence 30 degrees between each hour. Obviously it takes one hour for the hour hand to move from the 3 to the 4. Since it is one quarter past 3 (i.e. the minute hand will be exactly on 3), there will be 30/4 = 7.5 degrees between the two hands. Here are some possible related questions: e How many degrees at a quarter to four? e What is the first time before quarter past three when the two hands lie on top of each other? e What is the first time after quarter past three when the two hands lie on top of each other? O
problems.explanationZh: 时钟有 12 个小时，因此每个小时之间的温差为 30 度。显然，时针从 3 移动到 4 需要一个小时。由于现在是 3 点一刻（即分针将恰好位于 3），因此两根指针之间将存在 30/4 = 7.5 度。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 四分之一到四度是多少度？ e 在三点一刻之前，两只手第一次放在一起是什么时候？ e 三点一刻之后，两只手第一次放在一起是什么时候？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 8.2 - What's 15 cubed
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.2 - 15的立方是多少
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What's 15 cubed?
problems.promptZh: 15的立方是多少？
problems.explanation:
Well, clearly we can plug this in our calculator and read the display. However, in an interview that will not be an option. Instead, you will probably be expected to do it in your head. (If you think that's unreasonable, do not tell them so.) The correct answer is 3375. To do this sort of problem, you need to brush up on your mental arithmetic. The first thing is to simply do a few sums in your head and see how they go. You can also practice estimating the magnitude of solutions. 15 is about half-way between 10 and 20. These cube to 1000 and 8000. Cubing is convex so the answer will be less than the average of these. So guessing that it's not too far from, but less than, 4000 is a place to start. If you feel that your arithmetic skills need brushing up, then you need to get a book on the topic. One nice book is “Speed Mathematics Using the Vedic System” by Vali Nasser, [10]. One way to get better at cubes is to memorise squares. Thus work out the squares of all numbers below, for example 32, and memorise them. You then

8. LOGIC/BRAINTEASERS only have to do the square by the number in your head. In this case, it would be * 225 = 10 * 2254 0.5 « 10 * 225 which is not too hard. Here are some possible related questions: e@ What is 16 cubed? (Express as a power of 2.) e Estimate the cube root of 5000. O

problems.titleEn: Question 8.3 - A 4 x 4 x 4 cube is made up of 1 x 1 x 1 cubes
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.3 - 4 x 4 x 4 立方体由 1 x 1 x 1 立方体组成
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: A 4 x 4 x 4 cube is made up of 1 x 1 x 1 cubes. If I paint the outside of the cube, how many 1 x 1 x 1 cubes have paint on them?
problems.promptZh: 4 x 4 x 4 立方体由 1 x 1 x 1 立方体组成。如果我在立方体的外部涂漆，有多少个 1 x 1 x 1 立方体上涂有油漆？
problems.explanation: As usual, there are two approaches: one is tedious and error prone, the other easy. The bad approach is to count the number of cubes on each face and then subtract duplicates. The easy approach is to count the number of cubes with no paint. These simply constitute a 2 x 2 x 2 cube. So there are 8 cubes without paint. We therefore have 64-8 = 56 cubes with paint. Here are some possible related questions: e What about with a 5 x 5 x 5 cube? e How many cubes will have paint on more than one face? e How many cubes will have paint on more than two faces? e What will happen to the fraction of cubes with paint as the number of small cubes goes to infinity? O

problems.explanationZh: 像往常一样，有两种方法：一种是乏味且容易出错的，另一种是简单的。糟糕的方法是计算每个面上的立方体数量，然后减去重复的立方体。简单的方法是计算没有油漆的立方体的数量。这些简单地构成了一个 2 x 2 x 2 的立方体。所以有 8 个没有油漆的立方体。因此，我们有 64-8 = 56 个涂有油漆的立方体。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 5 x 5 x 5 的立方体怎么样？ e 有多少个立方体的多个面上都有油漆？ e 有多少个立方体的两个以上的面上都有油漆？ e 当小立方体的数量趋于无穷大时，涂有油漆的立方体的分数会发生什么变化？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 8.4 - Shortest surface path across a unit cube
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.4 - 单位立方体表面上的最短路径
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: An ant wants to go from one corner of a cube of volume 1 cubic meter to the opposite corner, staying on the surface. What is the shortest distance it must travel?
problems.promptZh: 一只蚂蚁想从体积为 1 立方米的正方体一个顶点出发，沿表面走到相对顶点。它需要走的最短距离是多少？
problems.answer:
The shortest path along the surface is
$$\sqrt5\text{ meters}.$$
problems.answerEn:
The shortest path along the surface is
$$\sqrt5\text{ meters}.$$
problems.answerZh:
沿表面的最短路径为
$$\sqrt5\text{ 米}.$$
problems.explanation:
A cube with volume $1$ cubic meter has side length $1$ meter. Unfold two adjacent faces of the cube into a $1$ by $2$ rectangle. The shortest surface path becomes the rectangle's diagonal:
$$\sqrt{1^2+2^2}=\sqrt5.$$
problems.explanationEn:
A cube with volume $1$ cubic meter has side length $1$ meter. Unfold two adjacent faces of the cube into a $1$ by $2$ rectangle. The shortest surface path becomes the rectangle's diagonal:
$$\sqrt{1^2+2^2}=\sqrt5.$$
problems.explanationZh:
体积为 $1$ 立方米的正方体边长为 $1$ 米。把相邻两个面展开成一个 $1$ 乘 $2$ 的矩形。沿表面的最短路径就是该矩形的对角线：
$$\sqrt{1^2+2^2}=\sqrt5.$$
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Cube shortest-path prompt and square-root notation were corrupted.

problems.titleEn: Question 8.5 - A lily-pad doubles in area every second
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.5 - 睡莲的面积每秒都会增加一倍
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: A lily-pad doubles in area every second. After one minute, it fills the pond. How long would it take to quarter fill the pond?
problems.promptZh: 睡莲的面积每秒都会增加一倍。一分钟后，水就填满了池塘。将池塘填满四分之一需要多长时间？
problems.explanation: The key to this sort of question is to spot the trick and not to attempt to solve equations. If it doubles in size every second, then it takes two seconds to quadruple in size. So the answer is 60 - 2 = 58 seconds. Here are some possible related questions: e Two trains start 60 miles apart. They head toward each other on the same track at 30 miles per hour. A fly starts in front of one train and flies at 45 mph until it meets the other. It then reverses direction. It does this until it gets squashed. How far does the fly fly? e What is the formula for the sum of N terms in a geometric series? O

It does this until it gets squashed. How far does the fly fly? e What is the formula for the sum of N terms in a geometric series? O

problems.titleEn: Question 8.6 - One lap of a racetrack is a mile long
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.6 - 赛道的一圈是一英里长。一辆汽车以 30 英里/小时的平均速度绕行
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: One lap of a racetrack is a mile long. A car drives round it with an average speed of 30 mph. How fast would it have to go on a second lap to have an average speed for the two laps of 60 mph?
problems.promptZh: 赛道的一圈是一英里长。一辆汽车以 30 英里/小时的平均速度绕行。第二圈需要多快才能使两圈的平均速度达到 60 英里/小时？
problems.explanation:
This is a trick question. The obvious answer of averaging the speeds is incorrect. Instead, we compute the times needed. Two laps means 2 miles. To travel 2 miles at 60 mph takes 2 minutes. To travel 1 mile at 30 mph also takes 2 minutes. We therefore have no time left to go round the second time. The car would have to travel infinitely fast.

8. LOGIC/BRAINTEASERS Here are some possible related questions: e If the car has a top speed of 120 mph, what's the fastest average speed that can be attained? e Why doesn't averaging the speeds work? O

problems.titleEn: Question 8.7 - We have a glass of white wine and a glass of red wine
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.7 - 我们有一杯白葡萄酒和一杯红酒
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: We have a glass of white wine and a glass of red wine. Each has 100mls initially. We have Smls from the red glass and put it in the white glass and make the mixture homogeneous. We then take Sml from the white glass and put it in the red. Is there more white in the red or red in the white?
problems.promptZh: 我们有一杯白葡萄酒和一杯红酒。每个最初有 100 毫升。我们从红色玻璃中取出 Sml，并将其放入白色玻璃中，并使混合物均匀。然后我们从白色玻璃中取出 Sml 并将其放入红色玻璃中。是红中多白还是白中红多？
problems.explanation: This is a very old one but still seems to come up. There are two main approaches: ® Compute the position after the first pouring and then compute again after the second one. e Use conservation of mass. We will do the second one, since it is easier and whether you spot this is the test of how smart you are (or how experienced...) The crucial point is that there is 100 mls of wine in each glass at the end. This can only be the case if the two amounts are the same. Not convinced? Well here's the proof in equations. Suppose there are x mls of red in the white and y of white in the red. Then the red glass contains 100 - y mis of red since the other y is white. But there are only x mls more so 100-—y+z= 100 since we started with 100. Here are some possible related questions: e Suppose we pour 6mls on the second pour. What then? e Suppose we pour 6mls on the first pour. What then? e What colour will the wine in the white wine glass be after the procedure? O

problems.titleEn: Question 8.8 - Imagine a chocolate bar consisting of n squares
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.8 - 想象一根由 n 个正方形组成的巧克力棒
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Imagine a chocolate bar consisting of n squares. How many times do you have to break the chocolate bar to get n individual squares? Does the result depend on the shape of the chocolate bar? Prove it. Additional question: Suppose you can stack the pieces on top of each other so that you can break several at once. How many breaks then?
problems.promptZh: 想象一根由 n 个正方形组成的巧克力棒。你需要打破巧克力块多少次才能得到 n 个单独的方块？结果取决于巧克力棒的形状吗？证明一下。附加问题：假设您可以将碎片堆叠在一起，这样您就可以一次打破多个碎片。那要休息几次呢？
problems.explanation: The key to the first part is to notice that if you have k pieces and break one then you have k + 1 pieces. This means that to get from 1 piece to n pieces takes n - 1 breaks. This clearly does not depend on the shape of the bar. For the second part, if we break all the pieces we have, then we can double the number of pieces with one break and we can do no better. This means that we need at least k breaks where k is the smallest number such that gk >n. In this case, dependence on shape is trickier: if any of our pieces only ever has one square in it, then clearly we cannot break it again. For example, a 2 x 2 square can be done with two breaks. However, if we take 3 squares in a row with an extra square below the centre one, then any initial break will detach precisely one piece, and we will need at least 3 breaks in total. Here are some possible related' questions: e Does the shape of the small pieces matter? e Discuss the 3 dimensional analogue of the two problems. @ What is a toblerone? O

This means that we need at least k breaks where k is the smallest number such that gk >n. In this case, dependence on shape is trickier: if any of our pieces only ever has one square in it, then clearly we cannot break it again. For example, a 2 x 2 square can be done with two breaks. However, if we take 3 squares in a row with an extra square below the centre one, then any initial break will detach precisely one piece, and we will need at least 3 breaks in total. Here are some possible related' questions: e Does the shape of the small pieces matter? e Discuss the 3 dimensional analogue of the two problems. @ What is a toblerone? O
problems.explanationZh: 第一部分的关键是要注意，如果您有 k 块并打破了其中一个，那么您就有 k + 1 块。这意味着从 1 块变成 n 块需要 n - 1 次休息。这显然不取决于条形的形状。对于第二部分，如果我们打破了所有的碎片，那么我们可以通过一次打破来使碎片数量增加一倍，而且我们不能做得更好。这意味着我们至少需要 k 个中断，其中 k 是 gk >n 的最小数字。在这种情况下，对形状的依赖就更棘手了：如果我们的任何一块只有一个正方形，那么显然我们不能再次打破它。例如，一个 2 x 2 的正方形可以用两次中断来完成。然而，如果我们连续取 3 个方块，并在中心方块下方多出一个方块，那么任何初始断裂都将精确地分离出一块，并且我们总共需要至少 3 个断裂。以下是一些可能相关的问题： e 小块的形状重要吗？ e 讨论这两个问题的 3 维类比。 @什么是三角巧克力？氧

problems.titleZh: 问题 8.9 - 假设我们两个人走进一家酒吧，点了一品脱啤酒

problems.promptEn: Suppose the two of us go into a pub and order a pint of beer. I drink half the pint. You drink half of the remainder. I drink half of what's left after this, and we continue. How much of the pint do I drink, and how much do you?
problems.promptZh: 假设我们两个人走进一家酒吧，点了一品脱啤酒。我喝了一半品脱。你喝了剩下的一半。我喝掉了剩下的一半，然后我们继续。我喝多少品脱，你喝多少？
problems.explanation:
To solve this problem we just need to identify the infinite sums that describe how much each person drinks. The sums themselves will not need to be computed, as we shall see. Initially I drink 1/2, leaving 1/2 in the glass. You drink half of this, or 1/4, leaving 1/4 in the glass. Thus my next drink is 1/8 of a pint, then 1/32 and so on, while you drink the sequence 1/4, 1/16,... In other words, ae Amount I drink = SgaktT? k=0 ae I k=0 k=0

8. LOGIC/BRAINTEASERS This shows that if we let the amount I drink be x, then the amount you drink is x/2: I drink twice as much as you. For completeness we should check that the entire pint is drunk. Adding the above two series gives “1 Total drunk = Ss? OR? k=1 and using summation of a geometric series we see the total proportion drunk is 1. Given I drink twice as much as you, I must have drunk 2/3 of the pint, while you drink 1/3. Here are some possible related questions: e If instead I drink 1/3, then you drink 1/3 of the remainder, and so on, how much more do I drink now? Will the entire pint be consumed? Generalise to a fraction 1/n of a pint at each step. e Take the original question and add a third person, so I drink half, you drink half of what's left before they drink half of the remainder after that and so on. What amount do each of us drink? e Say I'ma bit thirstier than you, and drink 1/2 of what's left each time while you only drink 1/3. Now how much more doI drink? O

problems.titleEn: Question 8.10 - A rabbit has N steps to climb
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.10 - 兔子有 N 级台阶要爬。它可以一次跳一步或两步
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: A rabbit has N steps to climb. It can either jump one step or two steps at a time. How many distinct ways are there to the top?
problems.promptZh: 兔子有 N 级台阶要爬。它可以一次跳一步或两步。到达顶峰有多少种不同的方式？
problems.explanation:
This question screams to be solved backwards, that is starting from a position one step away from the top and working our way back to N steps. This is because it is immediately apparent that the number of distinct ways to the top from a position k from the top will be the sum of the number of distinct ways from each possible next position. To state this mathematically, let p(k) be the number of distinct ways to the top from a distance of &. From this position, we can move either one or two steps, so p(k) = p(k - 1) + p(k 2). We have our recursive case, now we need an initial (base) case. From a distance of 1 step, there is clearly only one way to the top. We can define the number of unique ways when we are at the top to be 1, or by considering the number from

a distance of 2 we see p(2) = 2. Either way, we have the base cases which we require two of. Along with our recursive case, this gives the Fibonacci sequence. See Question 6.3 for further details on this sequence. Here are some possible related questions: e@ What if it can move 3 steps as well? e Formulate and solve a two-dimensional analogue to the problem. O

problems.explanationZh:
这个问题迫切需要倒退解决，即从距离顶部一步的位置开始，一直回到 N 步。这是因为很明显，从距离顶部的位置 k 到顶部的不同路径的数量将是从每个可能的下一个位置开始的不同路径的数量之和。为了以数学方式表述这一点，令 p(k) 为从 & 距离到达顶部的不同路径的数量。从这个位置开始，我们可以移动一步或两步，因此 p(k) = p(k — 1) + p(k 2)。我们有了递归案例，现在我们需要一个初始（基本）案例。从1级台阶的距离来看，显然只有一条路可以到达山顶。当我们处于顶部时，我们可以将唯一方式的数量定义为 1，或者考虑来自

8.3.解 距离为 2 时，我们看到 p(2) = 2。无论哪种方式，我们都有需要其中两个的基本情况。与我们的递归案例一起，这给出了斐波那契数列。有关此序列的更多详细信息，请参阅问题 6.3。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e@ 如果它也可以移动 3 步怎么办？ e 制定并解决该问题的二维模拟。氧

problems.titleEn: Question 8.11 - Snowplow start time
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.11 - 扫雪车问题中的下雪开始时间
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Snow starts falling sometime before noon. A snowplow begins work at noon, and its speed is inversely proportional to the time since the snow began falling. It travels twice as far from noon to 1 pm as it travels from 1 pm to 2 pm. When did the snow start?
problems.promptZh: 中午前某个时刻开始下雪。扫雪车从中午开始工作，其速度与从开始下雪以来经过的时间成反比。扫雪车从中午到下午 1 点行驶的距离，是从下午 1 点到 2 点行驶距离的两倍。雪是什么时候开始下的？
problems.answer:
The snow began
$$\frac{\sqrt5-1}{2}\text{ hours}$$
before noon, about $0.618$ hours or about $37$ minutes before noon.
problems.answerEn:
The snow began
$$\frac{\sqrt5-1}{2}\text{ hours}$$
before noon, about $0.618$ hours or about $37$ minutes before noon.
problems.answerZh:
雪在中午前
$$\frac{\sqrt5-1}{2}\text{ 小时}$$
开始下，约为 $0.618$ 小时，也就是中午前约 $37$ 分钟。
problems.explanation:
Let $x$ be the number of hours the snow has already been falling at noon. Since speed is inversely proportional to time since the snow started,
$$v(t)=\frac{a}{t},$$
where $t$ is measured from the start of snowfall.

The distance from noon to 1 pm is
$$\int_x^{x+1}\frac{a}{t}\,dt=a\log\frac{x+1}{x}.$$
The distance from 1 pm to 2 pm is
$$\int_{x+1}^{x+2}\frac{a}{t}\,dt=a\log\frac{x+2}{x+1}.$$
The first distance is twice the second, so
$$\log\frac{x+1}{x}=2\log\frac{x+2}{x+1}.$$
Exponentiating gives
$$\frac{x+1}{x}=\left(\frac{x+2}{x+1}\right)^2.$$
Thus
$$(x+1)^3=x(x+2)^2,$$
which reduces to
$$x^2+x-1=0.$$
The positive solution is
$$x=\frac{\sqrt5-1}{2}.$$
problems.explanationEn:
Let $x$ be the number of hours the snow has already been falling at noon. Since speed is inversely proportional to time since the snow started,
$$v(t)=\frac{a}{t},$$
where $t$ is measured from the start of snowfall.

The distance from noon to 1 pm is
$$\int_x^{x+1}\frac{a}{t}\,dt=a\log\frac{x+1}{x}.$$
The distance from 1 pm to 2 pm is
$$\int_{x+1}^{x+2}\frac{a}{t}\,dt=a\log\frac{x+2}{x+1}.$$
The first distance is twice the second, so
$$\log\frac{x+1}{x}=2\log\frac{x+2}{x+1}.$$
Exponentiating gives
$$\frac{x+1}{x}=\left(\frac{x+2}{x+1}\right)^2.$$
Thus
$$(x+1)^3=x(x+2)^2,$$
which reduces to
$$x^2+x-1=0.$$
The positive solution is
$$x=\frac{\sqrt5-1}{2}.$$
problems.explanationZh:
令 $x$ 表示到中午时雪已经下了多少小时。由于速度与开始下雪以来经过的时间成反比，
$$v(t)=\frac{a}{t},$$
其中 $t$ 从下雪开始计时。

中午到下午 1 点的距离为
$$\int_x^{x+1}\frac{a}{t}\,dt=a\log\frac{x+1}{x}.$$
下午 1 点到 2 点的距离为
$$\int_{x+1}^{x+2}\frac{a}{t}\,dt=a\log\frac{x+2}{x+1}.$$
题目说第一段距离是第二段的两倍，因此
$$\log\frac{x+1}{x}=2\log\frac{x+2}{x+1}.$$
指数化得到
$$\frac{x+1}{x}=\left(\frac{x+2}{x+1}\right)^2.$$
于是
$$(x+1)^3=x(x+2)^2,$$
化简为
$$x^2+x-1=0.$$
正根为
$$x=\frac{\sqrt5-1}{2}.$$

problems.titleEn: Question 8.12 - What is the smallest number of integer weights required to exactly balance every integer between | and 40
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.12 - 精确平衡 | 之间的每个整数所需的最小整数权重是多少
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: What is the smallest number of integer weights required to exactly balance every integer between | and 40. Prove it.
problems.promptZh: 精确平衡 | 之间的每个整数所需的最小整数权重是多少？ 40.证明这一点。
problems.explanation: Each weight can either be used or not used. Thus this is really a problem about binary arithmetic. We should therefore use 1,2, 4,8,16, and 32. How do we prove that it is impossible with 5? There are only 2° = 32 possible combinations so you cannot balance 40 different numbers. Here are some possible related questions: e What if I want to balance every number between 1 and 64? Show me how to balance 64 with your chosen weights. e Write an algorithm to find the weights needed to balance a number in the range. e Suppose you can put weights on either scale, so that a number can be used positively or negatively. How many then? O

problems.titleEn: Question 8.13 - Find the smallest subset of integers that you can use to produce 1,2,
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.13 - 仅使用“+”或“-”即可找到可用于生成 1,2,
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Find the smallest subset of integers that you can use to produce 1,2,...,40 by only using "+" or "-" (each number in the subset can be used at most one time).
problems.promptZh: 仅使用“+”或“-”即可找到可用于生成 1,2,...,40 的最小整数子集（子集中的每个数字最多只能使用一次）。
problems.explanation:
First, we derive a lower bound on how many numbers we need. If we have k numbers, we can use each of them in three different ways. We can therefore create 3k different numbers. However, note that one of the numbers must be zero, and for every positive number we can also create its negative.

To make numbers 1 through N, we must therefore have 3° >2N +1, For the case we are interested in, we need 3% > 81, sok > 4. To see that it can be done with 4, we work explicitly. If we start with 1 then we can get - 1,0,1. To be able to get 2, we must add in 3, we can then get - 4, —3, —2, -1,0,1, 2,3, 4. To obtain 5 without any overlaps, we can use 9. We can then get - 13, —12, —11, —10,...,10,11, 12, 13. Clearly, the next choice will be 27, and with it we can get all numbers from 1 to 40. Here are some possible related questions: e What if we need to construct 1 through 50? e What if we can use each number twice? e Can you think of a physical application? O
problems.explanationEn:
First, we derive a lower bound on how many numbers we need. If we have k numbers, we can use each of them in three different ways. We can therefore create 3k different numbers. However, note that one of the numbers must be zero, and for every positive number we can also create its negative.

To make numbers 1 through N, we must therefore have 3° >2N +1, For the case we are interested in, we need 3% > 81, sok > 4. To see that it can be done with 4, we work explicitly. If we start with 1 then we can get - 1,0,1. To be able to get 2, we must add in 3, we can then get - 4, —3, —2, -1,0,1, 2,3, 4. To obtain 5 without any overlaps, we can use 9. We can then get - 13, —12, —11, —10,...,10,11, 12, 13. Clearly, the next choice will be 27, and with it we can get all numbers from 1 to 40. Here are some possible related questions: e What if we need to construct 1 through 50? e What if we can use each number twice? e Can you think of a physical application? O
problems.explanationZh:
首先，我们得出需要的数字的下限。如果我们有 k 个数字，我们可以通过三种不同的方式使用它们。因此我们可以创建 3k 个不同的数字。但是，请注意，其中一个数字必须为零，并且对于每个正数，我们也可以创建其负数。

8.3.解决方案 为了使数字 1 到 N，我们必须有 3° >2N +1，对于我们感兴趣的情况，我们需要 3% > 81，sok > 4。为了看到它可以用 4 完成，我们明确地工作。如果我们从 1 开始，那么我们可以得到 —1,0,1。为了得到 2，我们必须加上 3，然后我们可以得到 —4, —3, —2, -1,0,1, 2,3, 4。为了得到没有任何重叠的 5，我们可以使用 9。然后我们可以得到 —13, —12, —11, —10,...,10,11, 12, 13。显然，下一个选择将是 27，用它我们可以得到 1 中的所有数字到 40。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 如果我们需要构造 1 到 50 怎么办？ e 如果我们可以使用每个数字两次怎么办？ e 你能想到一个物理应用吗？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 8.14 - How many ways can you tile dominoes (2 by 1) on a 2 by n grid without gaps
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.14 - 在 2 × n 网格上无间隙地平铺多米诺骨牌（2 × 1）有多少种方式
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: How many ways can you tile dominoes (2 by 1) on a 2 by n grid without gaps?
problems.promptZh: 在 2 × n 网格上无间隙地平铺多米诺骨牌（2 × 1）有多少种方式？
problems.explanation:
Each domino either lies horizontally across, or is vertical and another vertical domino lies next to it. This means that slicing into two 1 x n grids, these will have the same tilings, and the problem is equivalent to how many ways can you tile a 1 x n grid with squares and dominoes? Let F'(n) be the number of tilings. The first tile is either a square or a domino. If it is a square then the number of possible such tilings is F(n - 1). If it is a domino then the number of tilings is F(n - 2). So F(n) = F(n-1)+ F(n- 2). We have F(0) =1 and F(1) = 2.

8. LOGIC/BRAINTEASERS This means that F'(n) is the (n + 1)th Fibonacci number! Here are some possible related questions: e Why does F'(0) = 1? e How would you compute F'(10000) quickly? e How many ways can you tile a 3 x n rectangle with dominoes? e A polyomino of size k can be obtained from one of size k - 1 by sticking a square to its side. When k = 2, we have dominoes and there is only 1 configuration. How many are there with k = 3 and k = 4? O

When k = 2, we have dominoes and there is only 1 configuration. How many are there with k = 3 and k = 4? O
problems.explanationZh:
每个多米诺骨牌要么水平放置，要么垂直放置，旁边还有另一个垂直的多米诺骨牌。这意味着分割成两个 1 x n 网格，它们将具有相同的平铺，问题相当于用正方形和多米诺骨牌平铺 1 x n 网格有多少种方式？令 F'(n) 为平铺数量。第一个瓷砖是正方形或多米诺骨牌。如果它是一个正方形，那么可能的这种平铺的数量是 F(n - 1)。如果是多米诺骨牌，则平铺的数量为 F(n – 2)。所以 F(n) = F(n-1)+ F(n-2)。我们有 F(0) =1 且 F(1) = 2。

8. 逻辑/脑筋急转弯 这意味着 F'(n) 是第 (n + 1) 个斐波那契数！以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 为什么 F'(0) = 1？ e 如何快速计算 F'(10000)？ e 用多米诺骨牌平铺 3 x n 矩形有多少种方式？ e 大小为 k 的多联骨牌可以通过在大小为 k-1 的一侧粘贴一个正方形来获得。当 k = 2 时，我们有多米诺骨牌，并且只有 1 个配置。 k = 3 和 k = 4 的有多少个？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 8.15 - You have four cards, displaying 7, 6, A, C
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.15 - 你有四张卡片，分别显示 7、6、A、C
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: You have four cards, displaying 7, 6, A, C. The claim is: if there is a vowel on one side of a card, then there must be an even number on the other. You may now turn some cards over to test this claim. Which cards would you turn over and why?
problems.promptZh: 你有四张卡片，分别显示 7、6、A、C。要求是：如果卡片的一侧有元音字母，则另一侧一定有偶数。您现在可以翻出一些卡片来检验这一说法。你会翻开哪些牌，为什么？
problems.explanation:
This is a test of basic deductive logic. We are testing the truth of a statement of the form “P implies Q.” This means that if P holds then Q must also hold. This is logically equivalent to “not Q implies not P” so Q not holding means that P does not hold either. It does not say anything about what is on the back of odd-numbered cards. The question is ambiguous in that it is not clear whether a card must have a letter on one side and a number on the other. We proceed as if this were the case. The first card “7” is odd so we should check the back. If the back has a vowel, then the claim is false. The second card “6” is even so the statement does not say anything about this card. The other side may have a vowel, or it may not. The third card “A” is a vowel, so we must check that the back is even. The fourth card “C” is not a vowel, so the statement says nothing and we not look at the back. Here are some possible related questions: e Same four cards. The claim is: each card has either a vowel or an even number. How many cards to test the claim? e Same four cards. The claim is: each card has a vowel and an even number. How many cards to test the claim?

e Same four cards. The claim is: each card has precisely one of a vowel and an even number. How many cards to test the claim? e How would you express the relation “implies” in terms of elementary logical operations? e@ What if a card could have two numbers or two letters? O
problems.explanationEn:
This is a test of basic deductive logic. We are testing the truth of a statement of the form “P implies Q.” This means that if P holds then Q must also hold. This is logically equivalent to “not Q implies not P” so Q not holding means that P does not hold either. It does not say anything about what is on the back of odd-numbered cards. The question is ambiguous in that it is not clear whether a card must have a letter on one side and a number on the other. We proceed as if this were the case. The first card “7” is odd so we should check the back. If the back has a vowel, then the claim is false. The second card “6” is even so the statement does not say anything about this card. The other side may have a vowel, or it may not. The third card “A” is a vowel, so we must check that the back is even.
