The claim is: each card has either a vowel or an even number. How many cards to test the claim? e Same four cards. The claim is: each card has a vowel and an even number. How many cards to test the claim?

e Same four cards. The claim is: each card has precisely one of a vowel and an even number. How many cards to test the claim? e How would you express the relation “implies” in terms of elementary logical operations? e@ What if a card could have two numbers or two letters? O
problems.explanationZh:
这是对基本演绎逻辑的测试。我们正在测试“P 暗示 Q”形式的陈述的真实性。这意味着如果 P 成立，那么 Q 也必须成立。这在逻辑上相当于“不 Q 意味着 P 不成立”，因此 Q 不成立意味着 P 也不成立。它没有说明奇数卡片背面的内容。该问题含糊不清，因为不清楚卡片是否必须一侧有字母，另一侧必须有数字。我们按照实际情况进行处理。第一张牌“7”是奇数，所以我们应该检查背面。如果后面有元音，则该说法是错误的。第二张牌“6”是偶数，因此该声明没有提及这张牌的任何内容。另一侧可能有元音，也可能没有。第三张卡“A”是元音字母，所以一定要检查背面是否是偶数。第四张卡“C”不是元音，因此该语句什么也没说，我们也不看后面。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 相同的四​​张牌。主张是：每张卡片都有一个元音或一个偶数。需要多少张卡来测试该声明？ e 同样的四张牌。主张是：每张卡片都有一个元音和一个偶数。需要多少张卡来测试该声明？

8.3.解决方案 e 同样的四张牌。主张是：每张卡片都恰好有一个元音和一个偶数。需要多少张卡来测试该声明？ e 你会如何用基本逻辑运算来表达“蕴涵”关系？ e@ 如果一张卡片可以有两个数字或两个字母怎么办？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 8.16 - Suppose there are nine marbles in a jar, one heavier than the rest
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.16 - 假设一个罐子里有九颗弹珠，其中一颗比其余的重
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Suppose there are nine marbles in a jar, one heavier than the rest. You have a set of scales and can weigh as many as you want a maximum of three times to ascertain which marble is the heaviest. How do you achieve this?
problems.promptZh: 假设一个罐子里有九颗弹珠，其中一颗比其余的重。您有一套秤，可以称量任意数量的大理石，最多三倍，以确定哪种大理石最重。你如何实现这一目标？
problems.explanation:
On each weighing, we can split the marbles in half and eliminate one half as not containing the heaviest. This would indicate that with one weighing we could compare two marbles, with two weighings four marbles and with three weighings eight marbles. So how do we find the heaviest from a group of nine? The above reasoning only uses two possible outcomes: if all marbles are weighed then either the first group is heavier or the second group is heavier. However, if we leave some marbles out we introduce a third possibility: that the third group is the one containing the heavier marble. This will be obvious if the two groups weighed have an equal weight. With this in mind, how do we solve our problem? On our first weighing, compare two groups of four marbles, leaving the ninth marble out. If the two groups of four are of equal weight, the ninth must be the heaviest and we are done. If one of the groups is heavier, we are in the situation of having two weighings remaining with four marbles. The problem is easily solved by weighing two groups of two to find the heaviest pair, and then using your last weighing to find which of the pair is the heaviest. Here are some possible related questions: e Can you do this in two weighings? e What is the solution to this problem if there are eleven marbles? e@ What is the maximum number of marbles this problem can be solved for with only three weighings? (Hint: Start with the maximum for two weighings) O

8. LOGIC/BRAINTEASERS
problems.explanationEn:
On each weighing, we can split the marbles in half and eliminate one half as not containing the heaviest. This would indicate that with one weighing we could compare two marbles, with two weighings four marbles and with three weighings eight marbles. So how do we find the heaviest from a group of nine? The above reasoning only uses two possible outcomes: if all marbles are weighed then either the first group is heavier or the second group is heavier. However, if we leave some marbles out we introduce a third possibility: that the third group is the one containing the heavier marble. This will be obvious if the two groups weighed have an equal weight. With this in mind, how do we solve our problem? On our first weighing, compare two groups of four marbles, leaving the ninth marble out. If the two groups of four are of equal weight, the ninth must be the heaviest and we are done. If one of the groups is heavier, we are in the situation of having two weighings remaining with four marbles. The problem is easily solved by weighing two groups of two to find the heaviest pair, and then using your last weighing to find which of the pair is the heaviest. Here are some possible related questions: e Can you do this in two weighings? e What is the solution to this problem if there are eleven marbles? e@ What is the maximum number of marbles this problem can be solved for with only three weighings? (Hint: Start with the maximum for two weighings) O

8. LOGIC/BRAINTEASERS
problems.explanationZh:
每次称重时，我们都可以将弹珠分成两半，并消除一半，因为其中不含最重的弹珠。这表明，用一次称重我们可以比较两个弹珠，用两个称重四个弹珠，用三个称重八个弹珠。那么我们如何从九个一组中找到最重的呢？上述推理仅使用两种可能的结果：如果对所有弹珠进行称重，则要么第一组较重，要么第二组较重。然而，如果我们留下一些弹珠，我们就会引入第三种可能性：第三组是包含较重弹珠的组。如果称重的两组重量相等，这一点就会很明显。考虑到这一点，我们如何解决我们的问题呢？在我们第一次称重时，比较两组四个弹珠，将第九个弹珠排除在外。如果两组四人的重量相等，则第九个一定是最重的，我们就完成了。如果其中一组较重，那么我们就处于用四颗弹珠进行两次称重的情况。这个问题很容易解决，方法是称量两组，每组两个，找到最重的一对，然后使用最后一次称重找到哪一对最重。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 您可以分两次称重来完成此操作吗？ e 如果有十一个弹珠，这个问题的解法是什么？ e@ 仅通过三次称重就可以解决此问题的最大弹珠数量是多少？ （提示：从两次称量的最大值开始）O

8. 逻辑/脑筋急转弯

problems.titleEn: Question 8.17 - There are n lions in a cage
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.17 - 笼子里有n只狮子。一块肉被扔进笼子里
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: There are n lions in a cage. A piece of meat is thrown into the cage. A lion can either eat the whole piece of meat or none of it. If it eats the meat, it falls asleep and becomes to the other lions a piece of meat. What happens?
problems.promptZh: 笼子里有n只狮子。一块肉被扔进笼子里。狮子可以吃整块肉，也可以不吃一块肉。如果它吃了肉，它就会睡着，对其他狮子来说就变成了一块肉。会发生什么？
problems.explanation: We have to assume the following: e the lions are very intelligent; e one lion is closest to the meat; e the lions prefer to eat; e the lions prefer to be hungry and alive over being eaten. It is unwise to argue with the interviewer over how silly this problem is. (It is, of course, very silly!) This sort of problem should always be done by induction. You start with no lions and work up. Tf no lions, nothing happens. If one lion, he eats the meat. If two lions, no one eats the meat since if they do we will be back to one lion, and the lion who ate the meat will be eaten by the above rule. If three lions, the closest lion eats the meat since they know two lions will not eat the meat. If four lions, don't eat. If five lions, do eat. In conclusion, if the number of lions is even no one eats. If the number of lions is odd, one eats it. Here are some possible related questions: e 6 pirates discover a treasure chest filled with 1000 gold coins. After rolling dice to decide the overall order, each pirate will have a chance to explain how he thinks the wealth should be divided up. Following each proposal, all the pirates will vote on it. If a majority agrees then the proposal is adopted. (The majority must be strict.) Otherwise, the pirate walks the plank. What happens assuming a pirate will vote down a division if and only if he thinks he will get more by doing so? e What if a tie results in the proposal passing? O
problems.explanationEn: We have to assume the following: e the lions are very intelligent; e one lion is closest to the meat; e the lions prefer to eat; e the lions prefer to be hungry and alive over being eaten. It is unwise to argue with the interviewer over how silly this problem is. (It is, of course, very silly!) This sort of problem should always be done by induction. You start with no lions and work up. Tf no lions, nothing happens. If one lion, he eats the meat. If two lions, no one eats the meat since if they do we will be back to one lion, and the lion who ate the meat will be eaten by the above rule. If three lions, the closest lion eats the meat since they know two lions will not eat the meat. If four lions, don't eat. If five lions, do eat. In conclusion, if the number of lions is even no one eats. If the number of lions is odd, one eats it. Here are some possible related questions: e 6 pirates discover a treasure chest filled with 1000 gold coins. After rolling dice to decide the overall order, each pirate will have a chance to explain how he thinks the wealth should be divided up. Following each proposal, all the pirates will vote on it. If a majority agrees then the proposal is adopted. (The majority must be strict.) Otherwise, the pirate walks the plank. What happens assuming a pirate will vote down a division if and only if he thinks he will get more by doing so? e What if a tie results in the proposal passing? O
problems.explanationZh:
我们必须假设以下几点： e 狮子非常聪明；一只狮子最接近肉； e 狮子更喜欢吃东西；狮子宁愿挨饿又活着，也不愿被吃掉。与面试官争论这个问题有多愚蠢是不明智的。 （当然，这是非常愚蠢的！）这类问题应该始终通过归纳法来完成。你从没有狮子开始，然后逐步提高。如果没有狮子，什么也不会发生。如果是一只狮子，他会吃肉。如果有两只狮子，则没有人会吃肉，因为如果他们吃了，我们将回到一只狮子，而吃掉肉的狮子将按照上述规则被吃掉。如果三只狮子，最近的狮子会吃肉，因为它们知道两只狮子不会吃肉。如果有四只狮子，就不要吃。如果有五只狮子，就吃吧。结论是，如果狮子的数量连人都吃不完。如果狮子的数量是奇数，则一只吃掉它。以下是一些可能的相关问题： 6名海盗发现了一个装满1000金币的宝箱。在掷骰子决定总体顺序后，每个海盗将有机会解释他认为应该如何分配财富。每个提案后，所有海盗都会对其进行投票。如果多数人同意，则该提案将被通过。 （大多数人必须严格。）否则，海盗就走上木板。假设一个海盗会投票否决一个部门，当且仅当他认为这样做会得到更多时，会发生什么？ e 如果提案通过时出现平局怎么办？氧

8.3.解决方案

problems.titleEn: Question 8.18 - You are sitting in a boat in a swimming pool, you throw the anchor over the side
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.18 - 你坐在游泳池里的船上，把锚抛到一边
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: You are sitting in a boat in a swimming pool, you throw the anchor over the side. What happens to the level of the water in the pool?
problems.promptZh: 你坐在游泳池里的船上，把锚抛到一边。泳池里的水位会发生什么变化？
problems.explanation: We have to consider two effects: e throwing the anchor into the pool will raise the water level, since it will displace water equal to its volume; e removing the anchor from the boat will lower the water in the pool, since it will displace water according to its weight when inside the boat. An anchor will be denser than water so its weight is greater than that of the volume of water equal to its volume. It therefore displaces more water when inside the boat than outside. The level goes down. Here are some possible related questions: e An iceberg floating in the ocean melts. What happens to the sea level? e Why do you think this question is phrased for a swimming pool and not a lake? O
problems.explanationEn: We have to consider two effects: e throwing the anchor into the pool will raise the water level, since it will displace water equal to its volume; e removing the anchor from the boat will lower the water in the pool, since it will displace water according to its weight when inside the boat. An anchor will be denser than water so its weight is greater than that of the volume of water equal to its volume. It therefore displaces more water when inside the boat than outside. The level goes down. Here are some possible related questions: e An iceberg floating in the ocean melts. What happens to the sea level? e Why do you think this question is phrased for a swimming pool and not a lake? O
problems.explanationZh: 我们必须考虑两个影响： 将锚扔进水池中会提高水位，因为它会排出与其体积相等的水；从船上取下锚会降低水池中的水位，因为锚在船内时会根据其重量排出水。锚的密度比水大，因此它的重量大于等于其体积的水的重量。因此，它在船内排出的水比在船外排出的水更多。水平下降。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 漂浮在海洋中的冰山融化。海平面会发生什么变化？ e 为什么你认为这个问题是针对游泳池而不是湖泊提出的？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 8.19 - Arrange black and white balls in two sacks
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.19 - 两个袋子中的黑白球安排
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Let's play a game with 100 balls: 50 white and 50 black and two sacks. You can arrange the balls within the two sacks in any way you want. I then come into the room and pick a ball from one of the sacks. If I pick a black, I win; a white, you win. How do you arrange the balls such that you have the highest chance of winning?
problems.promptZh: 有 100 个球：50 个白球、50 个黑球，以及两个袋子。你可以任意安排这些球。然后我随机选一个袋子，再从该袋中随机摸一个球。如果摸到黑球，我赢；如果摸到白球，你赢。如何安排才能让你获胜概率最大？
problems.answer:
Put one white ball alone in one sack, and put the remaining 49 white balls plus all 50 black balls in the other sack. Your winning probability is
$$\frac12+\frac12\cdot\frac{49}{99}=\frac{74}{99}\approx0.747.$$
problems.answerEn:
Put one white ball alone in one sack, and put the remaining 49 white balls plus all 50 black balls in the other sack. Your winning probability is
$$\frac12+\frac12\cdot\frac{49}{99}=\frac{74}{99}\approx0.747.$$
problems.answerZh:
把 1 个白球单独放进一个袋子，把剩下的 49 个白球和全部 50 个黑球放进另一个袋子。你的获胜概率为
$$\frac12+\frac12\cdot\frac{49}{99}=\frac{74}{99}\approx0.747.$$
problems.explanation:
The sack is chosen uniformly, so even a sack with only one ball is selected with probability $1/2$. Put a single white ball in one sack. If that sack is chosen, you win with probability $1$.

The other sack contains $49$ white balls and $50$ black balls, so if that sack is chosen your winning probability is $49/99$. Therefore
$$P(\text{win})=\frac12\cdot1+\frac12\cdot\frac{49}{99}
=\frac{74}{99}.$$

This concentrates one sure win into one sack while leaving the other sack with nearly the original white-ball proportion. Any strategy that puts more than one white ball in the first sack lowers the sure-win sack's leverage without enough compensation in the second sack.
problems.explanationEn:
The sack is chosen uniformly, so even a sack with only one ball is selected with probability $1/2$. Put a single white ball in one sack. If that sack is chosen, you win with probability $1$.

The other sack contains $49$ white balls and $50$ black balls, so if that sack is chosen your winning probability is $49/99$. Therefore
$$P(\text{win})=\frac12\cdot1+\frac12\cdot\frac{49}{99}
=\frac{74}{99}.$$

This concentrates one sure win into one sack while leaving the other sack with nearly the original white-ball proportion. Any strategy that puts more than one white ball in the first sack lowers the sure-win sack's leverage without enough compensation in the second sack.
problems.explanationZh:
袋子是等概率选择的，因此即使某个袋子只有一个球，也会以 $1/2$ 的概率被选中。把一个白球单独放入一个袋子；如果选到该袋，你必胜。

另一个袋子中有 $49$ 个白球和 $50$ 个黑球，所以如果选到该袋，你获胜的概率为 $49/99$。因此
$$P(\text{win})=\frac12\cdot1+\frac12\cdot\frac{49}{99}
=\frac{74}{99}.$$

这个策略把一个袋子变成“必胜袋”，同时让另一个袋子仍保留接近原始比例的白球。若在第一个袋子里放入超过一个白球，会降低这个必胜袋的杠杆效果，而第二个袋子的改善不足以弥补。
problems.manualContentReviewReason: Ball-sack game prompt duplicated the black-ball win condition and answer formula was corrupted.

problems.titleEn: Question 8.20 - 50 smart, logical people are on an island
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.20 - 一座岛上有 50 个聪明、有逻辑的人
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn:
50 smart, logical people are on an island. The people either have blue or brown eyes. The island has a certain set of rules. If you can work out that you definitely have blue eyes, then you have to commit suicide at 12 midnight. But you have to be 100% certain - i.e. you have to deduce logically

8. LOGIC/BRAINTEASERS that you have blue eyes. There are no mirrors and nobody can tell you what colour your eyes are. First, what happens? Then, one day a man from outside the village comes along and tells everybody on the island that there is at least one person that has blue eyes on the island. What happens?
problems.promptZh:
一座岛上有 50 个聪明、有逻辑的人。人们的眼睛要么是蓝色的，要么是棕色的。岛上有一套特定的规则。如果你能算出你肯定有蓝眼睛，那么你必须在午夜12点自杀。但你必须 100% 确定——也就是说，你必须逻辑推理

8. 逻辑/脑筋急转弯：你有蓝眼睛。没有镜子，没有人能告诉你你的眼睛是什么颜色。首先，会发生什么？然后，有一天，村外的一名男子告诉岛上的每个人，岛上至少有一个人是蓝眼睛的。会发生什么？
problems.explanation:
We have to work up from the zero case. If there was no one with blue eyes then the stranger would not have said someone did. So at least one person does. Suppose precisely one person has blue eyes. That person knows that the 49 other people have brown eyes and they know that someone has blue eyes. That person then knows they are the one with blue eyes. So they commit suicide at 12 midnight. Suppose precisely two people have blue eyes. Each of the two blue-eyed people analyses the situation and sees that there are two possibilities: e Ihave brown eyes in which case the blue-eyed person will kill themselves on the first day after doing the analysis above. e I have blue eyes in which case they will not. When the other blue-eyed person does not commit suicide they conclude it is because they both have blue eyes and both commit suicide at midnight on the second day. Following this to its logical conclusion, if n people have blue eyes they all commit suicide on the nth day. Here are some possible related questions: e If there were 9 people with blue eyes initially, then everyone already knew that someone had blue eyes so why does the stranger saying that someone has blue eyes make a difference? e What if the stranger lied (but was believed)?

e Suppose you have to commit suicide if you are p% sure that you have blue eyes. What difference would this make to the problem, if everyone starts with a prior belief that they are equally likely to have blue or brown eyes? O
problems.explanationEn:
We have to work up from the zero case. If there was no one with blue eyes then the stranger would not have said someone did. So at least one person does. Suppose precisely one person has blue eyes. That person knows that the 49 other people have brown eyes and they know that someone has blue eyes. That person then knows they are the one with blue eyes. So they commit suicide at 12 midnight. Suppose precisely two people have blue eyes. Each of the two blue-eyed people analyses the situation and sees that there are two possibilities: e Ihave brown eyes in which case the blue-eyed person will kill themselves on the first day after doing the analysis above. e I have blue eyes in which case they will not. When the other blue-eyed person does not commit suicide they conclude it is because they both have blue eyes and both commit suicide at midnight on the second day. Following this to its logical conclusion, if n people have blue eyes they all commit suicide on the nth day. Here are some possible related questions: e If there were 9 people with blue eyes initially, then everyone already knew that someone had blue eyes so why does the stranger saying that someone has blue eyes make a difference? e What if the stranger lied (but was believed)?

e Suppose you have to commit suicide if you are p% sure that you have blue eyes. What difference would this make to the problem, if everyone starts with a prior belief that they are equally likely to have blue or brown eyes? O
problems.explanationZh:
我们必须从零开始努力。如果没有蓝眼睛的人，那么陌生人就不会说有人有蓝眼睛。所以至少有一个人这样做。假设恰好有一个人有蓝眼睛。那个人知道其他 49 个人有棕色眼睛，并且他们知道有人有蓝眼睛。然后那个人就知道他们就是蓝眼睛的人。于是他们在午夜12点自杀了。假设正好有两个人有蓝眼睛。两个蓝眼睛的人各自分析了情况，发现有两种可能性： e 我有棕色的眼睛，在这种情况下，蓝眼睛的人在进行上述分析后的第一天就会自杀。 e 我有蓝眼睛，但他们不会。当另一个蓝眼睛的人没有自杀时，他们得出结论，这是因为他们都是蓝眼睛，并且都在第二天的午夜自杀了。由此得出逻辑结论，如果n个人有蓝眼睛，他们都会在第n天自杀。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 如果最初有 9 个人是蓝眼睛，那么每个人都已经知道某人有蓝眼睛，那么为什么陌生人说某人有蓝眼睛会有影响呢？ e 如果陌生人撒谎（但被相信了）怎么办？

8.3.解决方案 e 假设如果您确信自己有蓝眼睛，则必须自杀。如果每个人都一开始就相信自己拥有蓝色或棕色眼睛的可能性相同，那么这会对问题产生什么影响呢？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 8.21 - There is a prison of 22 prisoners in 22 cells
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.21 - 监狱有22间牢房，关押着22名囚犯
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: There is a prison of 22 prisoners in 22 cells. The warden says to them he will take them into a room with two light switches, which will each begin in the down position, and the prisoner may use a switch. However he will take them randomly, one a day. The warden says that as soon as anyone can tell him that all the prisoners have been into the room he will let them free, but if they get it wrong they hang. He gives them one hour to discuss their strategy, and after that there will be no communication. How do the prisoners guarantee their freedom?
problems.promptZh: 监狱有22间牢房，关押着22名囚犯。典狱长对他们说，他将带他们进入一个有两个电灯开关的房间，每个电灯开关都从向下的位置开始，囚犯可以使用开关。不过，他会随机服用，每天服用一个。典狱长说，一旦有人告诉他所有囚犯都进入了房间，他就会释放他们，但如果他们错了，就会被绞死。他给了他们一小时的时间来讨论他们的策略，之后就不会再有任何交流。囚犯如何保障自己的自由？
problems.explanation:
The prisoners must elect one prisoner to be the 'captain'. This prisoner will keep count of how many other prisoners he knows have visited the room, and can then tell the warden when they all have. To do this, the other 21 prisoners and the captain have two different strategies they employ upon visiting the room. For the 21 other prisoners, their strategy is as follows: (i) When they visit the room, if one or more switches are down they move one switch up. However they only ever move a switch once, if they have moved one in the past and one or more are down, they leave them. (ii) If both switches are up, they leave them alone. This is regardless of if they have moved a switch before in the past or not. The captain however, must tally the number of unique prisoners who have been to the room. He does this by counting the number of switches in the up position each time they visit, and then moving any switches in the up position to the down position. When their tally reaches 21, they know every prisoner has been to the room and may tell the warden. Here are some possible related questions: ® What is the expected number of days until the prisoners are free? Calculate this by simulation. e How can they make better use of the two switches to optimise the above algorithm? (Hint: How can you count to 31 on one hand?) O

8. LOGIC/BRAINTEASERS

8. LOGIC/BRAINTEASERS
problems.explanationZh:
囚犯必须选举一名囚犯作为“队长”。该囚犯将记录他认识的其他囚犯有多少人访问过该房间，然后可以在他们都访问过该房间时告诉典狱长。为此，其他 21 名囚犯和队长在探访房间时采用了两种不同的策略。对于其他 21 名囚犯，他们的策略如下： (i) 当他们参观房间时，如果一个或多个开关向下，则将一个开关向上移动。然而，他们只移动一次开关，如果他们过去移动过一个开关并且有一个或多个开关关闭，他们就会离开它们。 (ii) 如果两个开关都打开，则不理会它们。这与他们过去是否移动过开关无关。然而，队长必须统计去过该房间的独特囚犯的数量。他通过计算每次访问时处于向上位置的开关数量，然后将处于向上位置的所有开关移动到向下位置来实现此目的。当他们的计数达到 21 时，他们就知道每个囚犯都去过这个房间，并可以告诉典狱长。以下是一些可能的相关问题： ® 囚犯预计需要多少天才能获释？通过模拟计算这一点。 e 如何更好地利用这两个开关来优化上述算法？ （提示：如何用一只手数到 31？）O

8. 逻辑/脑筋急转弯

problems.titleEn: Question 8.22 - Given a small rectangle completely contained in a large rectangle and a straight edge, draw a straight line dividing the area conta
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.22 - 给定一个完全包含在一个大矩形和一条直边中的小矩形，画一条直线将大矩形中包含的区域而不是小矩形中包含的区域分成两半
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers

problems.promptEn: Given a small rectangle completely contained in a large rectangle and a straight edge, draw a straight line dividing the area contained in the big rectangle but not the small rectangle in half.
problems.promptZh: 给定一个完全包含在一个大矩形和一条直边中的小矩形，画一条直线将大矩形中包含的区域而不是小矩形中包含的区域分成两半。
problems.explanation: If a single line divides both rectangles in half it will inevitably divide the difference in half as well. Observe that any line through the centre of a rectangle will divide it in half. We therefore draw a line through the centre of both rectangles, and we are done. Of course, this requires us to be able to find the centre of a rectangle, however, the centre is at the intersection of the diagonals so that is easy. Here are some possible related questions: e Do you think that your solution to the problem is unique? e Given a rectangular cake with a rectangular piece missing, find a way to split the remaining cake in two with a single cut. (Give at least two solutions.) O

Observe that any line through the centre of a rectangle will divide it in half. We therefore draw a line through the centre of both rectangles, and we are done. Of course, this requires us to be able to find the centre of a rectangle, however, the centre is at the intersection of the diagonals so that is easy. Here are some possible related questions: e Do you think that your solution to the problem is unique? e Given a rectangular cake with a rectangular piece missing, find a way to split the remaining cake in two with a single cut. problems.explanationZh: 如果一条线将两个矩形分成两半，那么它也将不可避免地将差异分成两半。观察任何穿过矩形中心的线都会将其分成两半。因此，我们通过两个矩形的中心画一条线，就完成了。当然，这需要我们能够找到矩形的中心，但是，中心位于对角线的交点处，所以这很容易。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 您认为您对问题的解决方案是唯一的吗？ e 给定一个长方形蛋糕，其中缺一块长方形，找到一种方法，将剩余的蛋糕一分为二。 （至少给出两种解决方案。） O

problems.titleEn: Question 8.23 - You are asked to play NIM, with the last one to pick up a stick losing
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.23 - 你被要求玩 NIM，最后一个拿起棍子的人就输了
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: You are asked to play NIM, with the last one to pick up a stick losing. What's the optimal strategy? There are various versions of the game of NIM but let's consider a simple one. There are n matches, for example 10, on the table. Each turn you can take 1,2 or 3 matches. The person who takes the last stick loses.
problems.promptZh: 你被要求玩 NIM，最后一个拿起棍子的人就输了。最佳策略是什么？ NIM 游戏有多种版本，但让我们考虑一个简单的版本。桌子上有 n 场比赛，例如 10 场。每回合您可以参加 1、2 或 3 场比赛。拿走最后一根棍子的人就输了。
problems.explanation:
Since a player will take the last stick if and only if there is one left, our objective is to leave only one stick on the table. This shows that the game where the last person to take loses is the same as the game where the last person to take wins with one less stick. Suppose there are 5 matches on the table and it is our opponent's turn to play. After he plays there will be 2,3 or 4 matches left so we can take 1, 2 or 3 matches, respectively, to make him lose. So if there are 1 or 5 matches on the table he loses. We can play the same way if he has to play with 9 to get him to 5. More generally, we always play in such a way as to ensure that there are 4j4+1 matches on the table for some 7. If we cannot do this then we must lose if our opponent plays optimally. If it is “last player to pick up wins,” we aim for sticks instead.

Here are some possible related questions: e If the number of matches were random, would you play first or second? e If there were several piles, and you could only take from one pile, what's the winning strategy in both variants? O
problems.explanationEn:
Since a player will take the last stick if and only if there is one left, our objective is to leave only one stick on the table. This shows that the game where the last person to take loses is the same as the game where the last person to take wins with one less stick. Suppose there are 5 matches on the table and it is our opponent's turn to play. After he plays there will be 2,3 or 4 matches left so we can take 1, 2 or 3 matches, respectively, to make him lose. So if there are 1 or 5 matches on the table he loses. We can play the same way if he has to play with 9 to get him to 5. More generally, we always play in such a way as to ensure that there are 4j4+1 matches on the table for some 7. If we cannot do this then we must lose if our opponent plays optimally. If it is “last player to pick up wins,” we aim for sticks instead.

Here are some possible related questions: e If the number of matches were random, would you play first or second? e If there were several piles, and you could only take from one pile, what's the winning strategy in both variants? O
problems.explanationZh:
由于当且仅当还剩下一根棍子时，玩家才会拿走最后一根棍子，因此我们的目标是在桌子上只留下一根棍子。这说明最后一个拿走的人输了的游戏和最后一个拿走的人少一根棍子获胜的游戏是一样的。假设桌上有 5 场比赛，轮到我们的对手进行比赛。他打完后，还剩下2、3或4场比赛，所以我们可以分别进行1、2或3场比赛，让他输。因此，如果桌上有 1 或 5 场比赛，他就会输。如果他必须打 9 才能让他达到 5，我们可以用同样的方式进行比赛。更一般地说，我们总是以这样的方式进行比赛，以确保桌子上有 4j4+1 场比赛，而 7 号。如果我们不能做到这一点，那么如果我们的对手发挥最佳，我们就必须输。如果是“最后一个拿起胜利的玩家”，我们的目标是棍子。

8.3.解决方案 以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 如果比赛数量是随机的，您会先玩还是第二玩？ e 如果有几堆，而你只能从一堆中拿走，两种变体的获胜策略是什么？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 8.25 - How many petrol stations are there in the UK (or whichever country/city you are currently in)
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.25 - 英国（或您目前所在的国家/城市）有多少个加油站
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: How many petrol stations are there in the UK (or whichever country/city you are currently in)?
problems.promptZh: 英国（或您目前所在的国家/城市）有多少个加油站？
problems.explanation:
Initially this seems like an impossible question. It is unlikely that the interviewer will know the answer, so why have they asked the question? What they want to test is how the answer is structured and which assumptions are used to get there. One approach to this question is breaking it down to how many petrol stations there are in a town/suburb. So for example there are 3 petrol stations in a town of 10,000 people. This town has a large amount of tourists, and hence more cars than one would expect for 10,000 people. Taking this into consideration we could say there is 1 petrol station for 5,000 people. The population of the UK is roughly 60 million, so the number of petrol stations would be close to 12,000. In fact, one can check the number at the National Audit Office and it is around 12,000.

8. LOGIC/BRAINTEASERS Here are some possible related questions: e How many pairs of shoes are bought each year in the UK? e What is the real estate value of Central Park in New York? O

problems.explanationZh:
最初这似乎是一个不可能的问题。面试官不太可能知道答案，那么他们为什么要问这个问题呢？他们想要测试的是答案是如何构建的以及使用哪些假设来实现这一目标。解决这个问题的一种方法是将其分解为城镇/郊区有多少个加油站。例如，一个拥有 10,000 人的城镇有 3 个加油站。这个小镇有大量的游客，因此汽车数量比预计的 10,000 人要多。考虑到这一点，我们可以说有 1 个加油站可供 5,000 人使用。英国人口约为 6000 万，因此加油站数量接近 12,000 个。事实上，到审计署查一下这个数字，大约是12000人左右。

8. 逻辑/脑筋急转弯 以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 英国每年购买多少双鞋？ e 纽约中央公园的房地产价值是多少？氧

problems.titleEn: Question 8.26 - How many piano tuners are there in Oxford
problems.titleZh: 问题 8.26 - 牛津有多少钢琴调音师
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: How many piano tuners are there in Oxford?
problems.promptZh: 牛津有多少钢琴调音师？
problems.explanation: This is similar to Question 8.25 since the interviewer is mainly is interested in how the answer is structured. First, we need to assume a population for Oxford, say 100,000. Since people who live in Oxford like playing the piano, we'll assume there is one piano for every 4 people, so 25,000 pianos. To keep these pianos playing beautiful notes the diligent owners like to tune them twice a year, 50,000 tunings per year. A good piano tuner can take care of 4 pianos per day and assuming he works 200 days per year he can tune 800 pianos. There is therefore approximately 60 piano tuners in Oxford. The numbers used in this example are very approximate (and probably wrong) and aren't as important as the way in which the problem is broken down. Here are some possible related questions: e Do you think the number will increase or decrease over time? e How would you get a more accurate number? O

problems.explanationZh:
这与问题 8.25 类似，因为访谈者主要对答案的结构感兴趣。首先，我们需要假设牛津的人口，比如说 100,000。由于住在牛津的人喜欢弹钢琴，我们假设每 4 人就有一架钢琴，即 25,000 架钢琴。为了让这些钢琴演奏出优美的音符，勤奋的主人喜欢每年给它们调音两次，每年调音 50,000 次。一个好的钢琴调音师每天可以调音 4 架钢琴，假设他每年工作 200 天，他可以调音 800 架钢琴。因此，牛津大约有 60 名钢琴调音师。本示例中使用的数字非常近似（并且可能是错误的），并不像问题的分解方式那么重要。以下是一些可能的相关问题： e 您认为随着时间的推移，这个数字会增加还是减少？ e 如何获得更准确的数字？氧

第 9 章 软访谈 9.1。简介 好消息是，量化团队倾向于进行技术面试，而人力资源则是事后才想到的。如果这对你来说不是个好消息，那么你最好重新考虑你的职业选择。坏消息是，有时会问“软”问题，对于应届毕业生的工作来说，这类问题可能很重要。因此，在本章中，我们将讨论一些可能会提出的问题并讨论如何处理它们。如果进行软面试，准备好所有这些问题的答案将会非常有帮助。然后，我们会提出一些一般财务问题，以测试您对财务的一般认识。 9.2.软问题和答案 问题 9.1。你为什么想在银行工作？

problems.titleEn: Question 9.1 - Why do you want to work in banking
problems.titleZh: 问题 9.1 - 你为什么想在银行工作
tags: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.sourceUrl: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.bookName: 红宝书 Quant Job Interview Questions And Answers
problems.promptEn: Why do you want to work in banking?
problems.promptZh: 你为什么想在银行工作？
problems.explanation:
Well, the truth is generally “I got frustrated with an academic career and I want more money.” However, it is considered bad form to say this, and if this is really the only reason you may not do well in banking. Work out what the real reason is for you and how to present it. Some points to consider mentioning: e Dynamism: fast moving, I want to see the real results of my work in a short time frame. e Teamwork: I want to work with other people rather than on my own. This tends to go down well, although a lot of quants do not have the easiest personalities.

9, THE SOFT INTERVIEW e Transparency: I want to do something where my contribution is clear and recognized. e Ability to leverage existing skill set. Do not give negative reasons, i.e., don't say that I didn't like what I was doing before. Banks are not strong on e ethics, e reasonableness, so best not to mention these as reasons to work in banking. Bankers tend to get upset if you describe their role in negative terms. They provide financial products that other people want to buy and no one's forcing those people. The job of a quant is to help to produce those products, and if the products were not worthwhile no one would buy them. O QUESTION 9.2, Tell me about X where X is a random phrase from your CV.

problems.explanationZh:
好吧，事实通常是“我对学术生涯感到沮丧，我想要更多的钱。”然而，这样说被认为是不好的形式，如果这确实是你在银行业表现不佳的唯一原因。弄清楚你的真正原因是什么以及如何表达它。需要考虑提及的一些要点： e 活力：快速发展，我想在短时间内看到我工作的真正结果。 e 团队合作：我想与其他人一起工作，而不是独自工作。尽管很多宽客的性格并不简单，但这种情况往往会很顺利。

9、软采访 e 透明度：我想做一些我的贡献明确且得到认可的事情。 e 能够利用现有技能。不要给出消极的理由，即不要说我不喜欢我以前所做的事情。银行在道德、合理性方面不强，所以最好不要将这些作为在银行业工作的理由。如果你用负面的词语描述银行家的角色，他们往往会感到不安。他们提供其他人想要购买的金融产品，并且没有人强迫这些人。宽客的工作是帮助生产这些产品，如果产品不值得，就没有人会购买它们。 O 问题 9.2，请告诉我有关 X 的信息，其中 X 是您简历中的随机短语。

However, the important thing is to demonstrate some ability to take a non-straightforward approach rather than getting into confrontations with them, e.g., e Make them think something was their idea so they back it. e Work round them where possible. e Butter them up. e Try to understand what personality traits make them difficult and how you can exploit them. e Use extreme clarity when dealing with them to avoid misunderstanding and arguments about what was said. e Be extra polite with them. problems.explanationZh: 这很烦人，因为解决方案会根据情况而有很大差异。然而，重要的是要表现出采取非直接方法的能力，而不是与他们发生冲突，例如，让他们认为某件事是他们的想法，因此他们支持它。 e 尽可能绕过这些问题。 e 给它们涂上黄油。 e 尝试了解哪些性格特征使他们变得困难以及如何利用它们。 e 与他们打交道时要极其清晰，以避免误解和争论。 e 对他们要格外有礼貌。问题 9.6。你的弱点是什么？

problems.promptEn: What are your weaknesses?

problems.explanation:
This is a tricky one, in that who wants to admit all their bad aspects? The standard response is “I don't suffer fools gladly.” However, this is so hackneyed that you might just come across as glib. It is probably better to do some self-examination and then think about how to present honestly some of your true weaknesses. If you really think you do not have any, your weakness is certainly poor self-knowledge. O

Are you comfortable working with our in-house computer language instead of C++?
problems.explanationZh: 这类问题正是拥有外部兴趣和工作经验的有用之处。如果你确实有工作经验，那么从中选择一个方面，思考你做了什么以及取得了什么成就。如果您没有任何工作经验，那么您可以使用您担任运动队或其他俱乐部队长的例子。问题 9.22。您是否愿意使用我们的内部计算机语言而不是 C++ 进行工作？

problems.promptEn: Are you comfortable working with our in-house computer language instead of C++?

problems.explanation:
If you want the job, the answer had better be “yes.” But bear in mind that a job where you do C++ gives you a transferable skill and ability to leverage off many existing libraries, so do you really want the job? Goldman Sachs is particularly notorious for having its own in-house programming language. O 9.3. Finance data questions The following sorts of questions really are asked so look them up in the Financial Times, the Wall Street Journal or the web before turning up to the interview. Clearly, one should be most on top of the local financial indicators. For longer term understanding of the global economic situation, one good solution is to read the Economist every week.

9. THE SOFT INTERVIEW QUESTION 9.23. What is the current level of the FTSE? QUESTION 9.24, What is the current price of oil? QUESTION 9.25. Sketch the current UK yield curve and discuss its shape. QUESTION 9.26. What is the current Fed funds rate? (And what does that mean anyway?) QUESTION 9.27. What is the Bank of England base rate? (And what does that mean anyway?) QUESTION 9.28. What is the Euro base rate? QUESTION 9.29. Explain the sub-prime crisis. QUESTION 9.30. What's the dollar-yen exchange rate? Question 9.31. Which EU countries have the highest unemployment rates? Why do you think this is? QUESTION 9.32. Who is the chairman of the Fed? QUESTION 9.33. Who is the governor of the Bank of England? QUESTION 9.34. What is the FSA and how is it related to the Bank of England? What happened historically?

problems.explanationZh:
如果你想要这份工作，答案最好是“是”。但请记住，从事 C++ 的工作为您提供了可转移的技能和利用许多现有库的能力，所以您真的想要这份工作吗？高盛因其拥有自己的内部编程语言而臭名昭著。 9.3。金融数据问题 确实会被问到以下几类问题，因此在参加面试之前请先在《金融时报》、《华尔街日报》或网络上查找这些问题。显然，最应该关注的是当地的财政指标。为了更长期地了解全球经济形势，一个好的解决方案是每周阅读《经济学人》。

9. 软面试问题 9.23。富时指数目前的水平是多少？问题 9.24, 目前的石油价格是多少？问题 9.25。绘制当前英国收益率曲线并讨论其形状。问题 9.26。目前的联邦基金利率是多少？ （这到底意味着什么？） 问题 9.27。英国央行的基本利率是多少？ （这到底意味着什么？） 问题 9.28。欧元基本利率是多少？问题 9.29。解释一下次贷危机。问题 9.30。美元兑日元汇率是多少？问题 9.31。哪些欧盟国家失业率最高？你认为这是为什么？问题 9.32。美联储主席是谁？问题 9.33。英格兰银行行长是谁？问题 9.34。什么是 FSA？它与英格兰银行有何关系？历史上发生了什么？

第 10 章 十大问题 10.1。简介 大多数面试官都不是很有创意，他们倾向于问别人问过的问题。年复一年，某些问题一次又一次地出现。一些 1999 年流行的问题到了 2007 年仍然经常被问到。对于其中许多人来说，这是因为它们是好问题，因为它们间接测试了对关键概念的理解。有的直接测试。在这里，我们根据我们的经验和问题收集者的经验列出了十个最常见的问题。除非你完全掌握了所有这些，否则不要去参加面试。大多数时候你会被问到至少其中之一。 10.2.问题 问题 2.1。推导布莱克-斯科尔斯方程。 S = 0 和 S = oo 时满足什么边界条件？解决方案请参见第 22 页。 问题 2.4。假设布莱克-斯科尔斯世界中的两种资产具有相同的波动性但不同的漂移。它们的看涨期权价格如何比较？参见第 27 页。问题 2.9。尽早行使美式看涨期权是否是最佳选择？看跌期权怎么样？参见第 34 页。问题 2.13。看跌期权平价是什么意思？参见第 38 页。

10. 十大问题 问题 2.34。 A队与B队进行7场系列赛，谁先赢4局谁就获胜。您想下注 100，您的球队将赢得系列赛，在这种情况下，您将获得 200，如果他们输了，您将获得 0。然而，经纪人只允许对单场比赛进行投注。您可以在任何一场比赛发生前一天对任何一场比赛下注 X，如果获胜则获得 2X，如果失败则获得 0。您如何实现预期的支付？特别是第一场比赛你押注什么？参见第 52 页。问题 2.37。假设如果到期时现货价格在 100 到 110 之间，则期权支付 1，否则支付 0。从普通看涨期权合成期权。参见第 53 页。问题 2.50。今天一只股票价值 100 美元。利率为零。明天该股票的价值可能是 90 或 110。它以概率 p 移动到 110。将看涨期权定价为 100。参见第 65 页。问题 3.2。假设我们玩一个游戏。我最多可以掷骰子 3 次。每次我掷骰子时，您可以将显示的数字视为美元，也可以再次掷骰子。您的预期奖金是多少？参见第 86 页。问题 5.1。编写一个算法来进行数值积分。您如何优化算法？参见第 159 页。 问题 7.10。什么是虚函数？参见第 241 页。
